items.headline: USC's marquee NIL star — most-marketed female college athlete since Caitlin Clark
items.summary: Watkins's NIL deals (Nike, Pandora, AT&T, Gatorade) have made her one of the highest-valued college athletes in the country, projected to dwarf even Caitlin Clark's pre-draft commercial profile by the time she enters the WNBA.

items.headline: Stunned the basketball world by returning to college instead of declaring for 2025 WNBA Draft
items.summary: Miles, projected as a top-3 pick in the 2025 WNBA Draft, instead announced a return to Notre Dame for her senior year — citing the new TV deal making the WNBA more lucrative for future drafts and her desire to win a national title.

items.headline: 'Hawk Tuah' viral moment June 2024 → podcast empire by year end
items.summary: Welch's 2024 Nashville street interview launched her into pop culture — followed by a Talk Tuah podcast, NFL appearances, sports-betting platform partnerships, and a notorious Hawk Tuah Coin meme-token launch + collapse late 2024.

items.headline: Won 2024 Olympic gold with USWNT in Paris — daughter of Dennis Rodman as the league's biggest crossover figure
items.summary: Trinity Rodman, daughter of NBA legend Dennis Rodman, was a key contributor to the 2024 USWNT gold medal win. She plays for Washington Spirit (NWSL) and is widely considered the future face of US women's soccer.

items.headline: Has been openly distant from father Dennis throughout her career
items.summary: Trinity has spoken openly about her complicated relationship with father Dennis Rodman, including saying he was largely absent during her childhood. Their public reconciliation moments at NWSL games have been treated as media events.

items.headline: Wins Olympic gold medal with USWNT at Paris 2024
items.summary: Trinity Rodman helped the United States women's national team win the gold medal at the 2024 Paris Olympics. She scored a memorable extra-time goal in the quarterfinals against Japan (1-0) and was part of the championship roster. Despite being Dennis Rodman's daughter, she had little contact with her NBA legend father growing up.

items.headline: USWNT young star and Portland Thorns standout — 2024 Olympics squad member
items.summary: Wilson, who plays forward for the Portland Thorns and the USWNT, was a key squad member at Paris 2024 and is widely considered one of the next-generation American faces in women's soccer.

items.headline: Retired in September 2024 after 17-year USWNT/club career
items.summary: Alex Morgan retired from professional soccer in September 2024, ending one of the most-decorated US women's soccer careers of all time (2x World Cup champion, Olympic gold medalist).

items.headline: On-and-off relationship with WTA player Paula Badosa — tennis's most-followed couple
items.summary: Tsitsipas and Badosa have dated on and off since 2023. Their relationship has been one of the most-followed couples on the tennis tour and includes mutual social-media activity at major events.

items.headline: Joined Monday Night Football pre-game show in 2024 — analytics-first NFL voice
items.summary: Kimes, ESPN's most-respected analytics-leaning NFL voice, joined the MNF pre-game booth in 2024. She's widely cited as the most-rigorous female NFL analyst at any major network.

items.headline: Host of First Take for 10+ years — anchored the show through Stephen A's negotiations
items.summary: Qerim has been the steady moderator of ESPN's First Take throughout the show's various co-host iterations (Sharpe, Kellerman, Bayless, etc.). She's seen as one of the most professional moderators in sports media.

items.headline: Anchor of The Ringer's Russillo Show — close confidant of Bill Simmons
items.summary: Russillo has been the anchor podcaster of The Ringer's NBA-adjacent stable since 2017, with his solo show one of the most-listened sports pods in the country.

items.headline: Pablo Torre Finds Out — Meadowlark's investigative-comedy hit
items.summary: Torre's solo podcast launched in 2023 on Dan Le Batard's Meadowlark Media. Has broken multiple league-shifting stories (the Brett Favre Mississippi welfare case follow-ups) while in a comedy-format wrapper.

items.headline: Joined FanDuel TV in 2024 — left long ESPN tenure
items.summary: Nolan, who built her name on Fox Sports 1 and ESPN, moved to FanDuel TV in 2024 with a daily show. The move was part of FanDuel's larger push to recruit established sports media personalities.

items.headline: Built sports' biggest indie media empire from baseball play breakdowns
items.summary: Jomboy Media (founded by Jimmy O'Brien) grew from viral MLB breakdowns into a multi-sport network with a YouTube channel that out-watches some MLB team accounts. Fundraised $30M+ in 2023.

items.headline: Owns the most-talked-about sneaker collection in the NBA
items.summary: Tucker is widely considered the most serious sneaker collector in pro sports — players from across the league regularly dap-up his pre-game fits. Brand partners covet his co-signs.

items.headline: Negotiated buyout from Sixers in 2024 after sitting on Embiid-era bench
items.summary: Tucker was bought out by the Sixers in 2024 after multiple seasons of being a marginalized veteran. He's bounced through several teams since, with the Sixers chapter now treated as a cautionary contract case.

items.headline: 2018 Players' Tribune essay on panic attacks reshaped NBA mental-health discourse
items.summary: Love's March 2018 essay 'Everyone is going through something' opened up the NBA's conversation about mental health, alongside DeMar DeRozan's earlier piece. Love has since funded mental-health programs leaguewide.

items.headline: Plays in Monaco/EuroLeague after NBA exit — late-career renaissance abroad
items.summary: Walker, the 4x All-Star Hornets star, has been playing in EuroLeague (Monaco, then Milan) after his NBA career wound down due to chronic knee issues. He's spoken publicly about being happier playing overseas.

items.headline: Threw a heat pack onto the court during a 2024 playoff game vs Lakers
items.summary: During the 2024 first-round series, Murray threw a heat pack onto the court while a play was live. He was fined $100K by the league but not suspended; the moment became a recurring meme in his rough close-out series.

items.headline: Mavs GM Nico Harrison position increasingly precarious post-Luka trade
items.summary: After the February 2025 Luka trade, Mavs fans, season-ticket-holders, and ownership reportedly turned on GM Nico Harrison. Multiple reports suggested ownership was reviewing his position by mid-2025.

items.headline: Horse-racing obsession became NBA's most-charming side hustle
items.summary: Jokic's deep involvement in Serbian harness racing — he owns and trains horses, attends races, and has been photographed driving sulkies — became one of the league's most-shared cultural curiosities.

items.headline: Shams Charania's Feb 2 tweet announcing the trade broke the internet
items.summary: Shams Charania broke the Luka Doncic trade news at 11:18pm ET on February 1, 2025. Within 30 minutes, the tweet had 50M+ impressions and trended #1 globally. Mavs fans gathered outside American Airlines Center holding 'fire Nico' signs the next morning.

items.headline: Won 2024 Paris Olympics gold — the only major missing from his trophy case
items.summary: At Paris 2024, Djokovic finally won Olympic gold (defeating Carlos Alcaraz in the final), completing his career trophy case at age 37. He cried on the podium during the Serbian anthem.

items.headline: Won French Open + Wimbledon back-to-back in 2024 — youngest player ever to do it
items.summary: Alcaraz became the youngest player in tennis history to win the French Open and Wimbledon in the same year (2024) at age 21. Widely considered the heir to the Djokovic/Nadal generation.

items.headline: Won 2023 US Open at age 19 — first American teenager since Serena to win a major
items.summary: Gauff won the 2023 US Open over Aryna Sabalenka, becoming the first American teenager to win a major since Serena Williams in 1999. The post-match crowd reaction was the loudest at Arthur Ashe in years.

items.headline: Joined Ferrari for 2025 season — biggest F1 driver move in decades
items.summary: After 12 years and 6 championships at Mercedes, Hamilton joined Ferrari for the 2025 F1 season. The move was the most-discussed driver transfer in modern F1 history, dominating Drive to Survive coverage.

items.headline: Won 4th consecutive F1 championship in 2024 — joined Vettel + Schumacher tier
items.summary: Verstappen won his 4th straight F1 World Drivers' Championship in 2024, joining Sebastian Vettel and Michael Schumacher as the only drivers to win 4+ in a row in the modern era.

items.headline: McLaren's championship-contender — 2024 runner-up, popular with Drive to Survive crowd
items.summary: Norris finished 2nd in the 2024 F1 championship, leading McLaren to their first constructors' title since 1998. His personality has made him one of the most-marketed drivers on Drive to Survive.

items.headline: Arrested before the 2024 PGA Championship in viral traffic incident
items.summary: Scheffler was arrested by Louisville police on his way to the PGA Championship in May 2024 over a traffic confusion. Charges were quickly dropped. The mugshot became one of the most-shared sports images of the year. He went on to play 18 holes the same day.

items.headline: Won Paris 2024 Olympic gold in golf
items.summary: Scheffler won Olympic gold in men's golf at Paris 2024 with a final-round 62, his second major-stage title of the year.

items.headline: Heartbreaking 2024 US Open collapse — missed two short putts down the stretch
items.summary: McIlroy held the lead late in the 2024 US Open at Pinehurst but missed two short putts on 16 and 18, losing to Bryson DeChambeau. He has not finished a major since 2014.

items.headline: Filed for divorce, then reversed it within weeks in May 2024
items.summary: McIlroy filed divorce papers from wife Erica Stoll in May 2024, then publicly reversed the decision two weeks later. The episode dominated golf media coverage during the PGA Championship.

items.headline: Launched TGL with Rory McIlroy in January 2025 — virtual indoor golf league
items.summary: Woods and McIlroy's TGL (an indoor tech-driven team golf league) launched on ESPN in January 2025. Mixed reviews critically; strong launch numbers commercially.

items.headline: Confirmed relationship with Vanessa Trump in March 2025 — major sports/political crossover
items.summary: Woods and Vanessa Trump (ex-wife of Donald Trump Jr.) confirmed their relationship via Instagram in March 2025. The pairing dominated tabloid + sports coverage simultaneously.

items.headline: Returned to tour in 2024 after maternity break — reached 3 Grand Slam 4th rounds
items.summary: Osaka returned to professional tennis in 2024 after giving birth to daughter Shai in 2023. Made the round of 16 at three Grand Slams, stopping short of the deep runs that defined her early career but signaling the comeback is underway.

items.headline: Won Paris 2024 bronze with USA Rugby 7s — became biggest viral athlete of the Games
items.summary: Maher won bronze with the USA women's rugby 7s team and became the most-followed Paris 2024 athlete on TikTok with body-positive content that crossed into mainstream celebrity. Currently fronts a viral DWTS season + Sports Illustrated cover.

items.headline: Paris 2024: won 4 medals (2 gold) — now most-decorated American woman swimmer ever
items.summary: Ledecky won 2 gold + 2 silver at Paris 2024, bringing her career Olympic medal count to 14 — now the most-decorated American female swimmer in Olympic history, surpassing Jenny Thompson.

items.headline: Stunningly retired from ESPN in September 2024 to become St. Bonaventure GM
items.summary: Woj, the most-followed news-breaking journalist in basketball, abruptly retired from ESPN at the height of his career to become general manager of his alma mater St. Bonaventure's basketball program. The 'Woj retiring at peak' framing dominated sports media for weeks.

items.headline: 'Woj Bomb' as a verb — defined modern sports journalism for a decade
items.summary: For 10+ years, the term 'Woj Bomb' referred to Wojnarowski's news-breaking tweets, often dropped at strategically inconvenient moments for opponents. After his retirement, the term has migrated to Shams Charania.

items.headline: Joined ESPN in October 2024 — replacing Woj as the network's lead NBA insider
items.summary: Charania left The Athletic and Stadium for ESPN in October 2024, taking over as the network's primary breaking-news voice on the NBA after Woj's retirement.

items.headline: Broke the Luka Doncic-to-Lakers trade in February 2025 — biggest scoop of his career
items.summary: Charania's late-night tweet of the Luka Doncic trade in February 2025 became one of the most-viewed sports media moments of the year (50M+ impressions in 30 minutes). It firmly established him as the post-Woj generation's #1 NBA insider.

items.headline: NFL Network's lead insider for 12+ years — top free-agency news voice
items.summary: Rapoport has been the NFL Network's lead breaking-news reporter since 2012 and is generally considered the dominant insider voice during free-agency cycles, often racing Schefter to scoops.

items.headline: Re-signed with ESPN in 2023 — reportedly $9M+/year
items.summary: Schefter's contract extension reportedly made him the highest-paid breaking-news reporter in sports media history, at approximately $9M/year through 2027.

items.headline: Multiple controversies over editorial process — most notably 2022 emails with Bruce Allen
items.summary: Schefter was widely criticized in 2022 after leaked emails showed him allowing former Washington exec Bruce Allen to review a story before publication. He acknowledged it was a 'mistake' but the episode raised lasting questions about insider-reporter dynamics.

items.headline: First woman to do NBA Finals color commentary on a major U.S. broadcast
items.summary: Burke became the first woman to call the NBA Finals as primary color commentator in 2024 (alongside Mike Breen and Doc Rivers). The moment was treated as a major glass-ceiling break and was widely celebrated.

items.headline: Lost The Jump in 2021 after leaked recording — career still recovering
items.summary: Nichols was a leading NBA broadcaster until a leaked 2020 phone recording where she discussed Maria Taylor's contract surfaced in 2021, ending her ESPN tenure. She's since worked for Showtime + Showtime Basketball, with a slow rebuild ongoing.

items.headline: Founder of DraftExpress — sold to ESPN in 2017, now lead draft expert
items.summary: Givony built DraftExpress into the dominant NBA draft scouting site through the 2010s. ESPN acquired the property in 2017. He's now the network's lead pre-draft analyst and one of the most-cited names during draft season.

items.headline: Hosts Sports Media podcast at The Athletic — most-cited sports-media beat writer
items.summary: Deitsch's Sports Media podcast at The Athletic has been the dominant insider voice on the broadcast/contract beats for 7+ years. Frequently cited by industry trades.

items.headline: Jemele Hill is Unbothered — Spotify exclusive, top-tier sports-meets-culture podcast
items.summary: After her 2018 ESPN exit, Hill rebuilt as a Spotify-exclusive podcast host. The show became a leading sports-and-culture interview format and frequently breaks news at the intersection of sports and politics.

items.headline: Sold The Ringer to Spotify for $200M+ in 2020 — biggest indie sports-media exit
items.summary: Simmons sold The Ringer to Spotify in February 2020 for a price reported between $196-200M. The deal made him the highest-profile sports media founder-exit in history.

items.headline: ESPN's lead bilingual NBA reporter — covering Latino market growth
items.summary: Castillo has become the most-prominent bilingual NBA voice at ESPN, covering the league's significant Latino fan-base growth post-Wemby. Frequently featured during Spanish-language broadcasts and ESPN Deportes.

items.headline: NBA on TNT lead reporter — show ended after 2024-25 season due to lost rights
items.summary: Greenberg has been TNT's lead NBA play-by-play voice during a decade that included Inside the NBA's iconic run. The show formally ended after the 2024-25 season as part of the new Disney/NBC/Amazon media deal — the end of an era.

items.headline: Omaha Productions became sports' most-influential post-career media empire
items.summary: Manning's Omaha Productions, founded post-retirement, now produces ManningCast, Quarterback (Netflix), and Receiver (Netflix). Has redefined the post-career star vehicle.

items.headline: ManningCast remains MNF's most-discussed alt-broadcast — celebrity guests every week
items.summary: The Manning brothers' MNF alt-broadcast has averaged 1M+ viewers weekly since 2021 and become a celebrity destination (Stephen Curry, Ryan Reynolds, even Bill Belichick).

items.headline: Heir-apparent Cowboys owner — quietly running team operations for years
items.summary: Stephen Jones, son of Jerry Jones, is widely understood to be the eventual successor as Cowboys owner. League sources say he's been functionally running football operations for several years already as Jerry's age advances.

items.headline: Tech billionaire (TIBCO Software) — Kings owner since 2013, lit-the-beam evangelist
items.summary: Ranadive bought the Kings in 2013 and led the organization through the 'Light the Beam' renaissance. He's known as one of the league's most-publicly active owners on social media.

items.headline: Announced sale of Celtics in 2024 — process led to Bill Chisholm's $6.1B record bid
items.summary: Grousbeck and ownership group announced they were selling the Celtics in early 2024 — months after winning the championship. The sale process closed in March 2025 at $6.1B (a record for any North American sports franchise sale) to a group led by William Chisholm.

items.headline: Alibaba co-founder — Nets owner with one of the league's deepest pockets
items.summary: Tsai's net worth (estimated $9B+) makes him one of the league's wealthiest owners. He bought the Nets in 2019 and has been one of the league's most willing tax-payers.

items.headline: Colorado head coach since 2023 — turning a 1-11 program into a national brand
items.summary: 'Coach Prime' took over Colorado in December 2022 and immediately reshaped college football media — recruiting transfer-portal stars, drawing primetime broadcast slots, and bringing his Hall-of-Fame-NFL persona to college sidelines.

items.headline: Sons Shedeur and Shilo Sanders both joined Colorado — projected high NFL Draft picks
items.summary: Shedeur Sanders (QB) is widely projected as a top-5 pick in the 2025 NFL Draft. Shilo (S) was a Colorado standout. The Coach Prime era at Colorado is built around the Sanders family core.

items.headline: Hurdle vs Jaguars in November 2024 became GIF of the year
items.summary: Saquon Barkley's full-extension backwards hurdle over Jaguars defender Jarrian Jones in November 2024 instantly became the most-shared NFL play of the season and a defining image of his Eagles MVP-caliber year.

items.headline: Detroit OC turned Bears HC in January 2025 — most-coveted coordinator hire in years
items.summary: Johnson, the architect of Detroit's offensive renaissance, finally took a HC job in January 2025 with the Bears after passing on offers in 2024. His arrival paired him with Caleb Williams as the Bears' attempted Lions-style turnaround.

items.headline: Smith seeks extension as other QBs reset market
items.summary: In September 2024, Geno Smith's representatives approached the Seahawks about renegotiating his deal after seeing QBs like Trevor Lawrence and Jordan Love cash in with massive extensions. The Seahawks showed no urgency to extend Smith, leaving his future with Seattle uncertain.

items.headline: Darnold leaves Vikings for Seahawks with $110.5M deal
items.summary: Despite a strong 2024 season, Sam Darnold departed the Minnesota Vikings and signed with the Seattle Seahawks on a three-year, $110.5 million deal in March 2025. The Vikings' investment in J.J. McCarthy left no guaranteed starting role for Darnold.

items.headline: Plays through back fracture in 2024 season
items.summary: Indianapolis Colts WR Michael Pittman Jr. signed three-year, $71.5 million extension ($46M guaranteed) on March 11, 2024 after franchise tag. Caught 69 passes for 808 yards and 3 TDs in 16 games while managing back fracture, undergoing imaging scans every few weeks. Missed just one game despite injury issues.

items.headline: Traded to Steelers for second-round pick
items.summary: Seattle Seahawks traded WR D.K. Metcalf to Pittsburgh Steelers on March 9, 2025 for 2025 second-round pick (No. 52) plus pick swaps in rounds 6-7. Metcalf agreed to five-year, $150 million contract extension with Steelers. Finished 2024 with 66 receptions for 992 yards and 5 TDs.

items.headline: Career-best 45 catches, 756 yards, 9 TDs in 2024
items.summary: Baltimore Ravens WR Rashod Bateman posted career-best numbers in 2024 with 45 receptions for 756 yards and 9 touchdowns in 17 games. Signed three-year, $36.75 million extension two weeks after season. Emerged as solid No. 2 alongside Zay Flowers in Todd Monken's offense.

items.headline: Career-high 59 catches as primary target in 2024
items.summary: Tampa Bay Buccaneers TE Cade Otton finished 2024 with career-high 59 receptions for 600 yards and 4 touchdowns in 14 games. Emerged as top target in entire pass-catching corps when Mike Evans and Chris Godwin were sidelined with injuries. Targeted 10+ times in each of final three games.

items.headline: Torn labrum cuts 2024 season short
items.summary: Jacksonville Jaguars TE Evan Engram played just 9 games in 2024 due to torn labrum, catching 47 passes for 365 yards and 1 TD. Suffered hamstring injury in Week 1 vs Miami, missed Weeks 2-5, returned in Week 6. Ruled out for season December 13, 2024 after torn labrum in Week 14 vs Tennessee. Released after season.

items.headline: Signs $36M extension with Houston Texans
items.summary: Houston Texans TE Dalton Schultz agreed to three-year, $36 million contract extension on March 8, 2024. Has fully guaranteed base salaries of $5M (2024) and $11M (2025). Ranked as top-10 NFL TE by executives, coaches, scouts entering 2024. Caught 53 passes for 532 yards and 2 TDs in regular season.

items.headline: Danielle Hunter joined Texans elite defense with 12 sacks, 46 tackles in 2024
items.summary: The five-time Pro Bowler signed a two-year, $49 million contract with Houston in March 2024. In his debut season with the Texans, Hunter started all 17 games, posting 12.0 sacks and 46 combined tackles (30 solo, 16 assisted), helping establish Houston as a top-2 defense.

items.headline: Danielle Hunter re-signed with Texans on $40.1M one-year extension in 2026
items.summary: The Texans and Hunter agreed to a one-year, $40.1 million extension with a $30.7 million signing bonus in March 2026, keeping him under contract through 2027. His second year with Houston was elite as he finished with 15 sacks (3rd in NFL) and 15 TFLs (8th), earning second-team All-Pro honors.

items.headline: Patrick Queen led NFL in missed tackles (32) for Steelers in 2025
items.summary: After signing a three-year, $41 million deal with Pittsburgh in 2024, Queen has been inconsistent. In 2025, he led the league in missed tackles with 32 (per PFF) but still logged 120 tackles (56 solo), a sack, 4 PDs, a forced fumble and recovery. Cowboys and Steelers engaged in trade talks in early 2026.

items.headline: Rashan Gary traded from Packers to Dallas Cowboys on March 9, 2026
items.summary: The Packers moved Gary to the Cowboys after a disappointing 2025 season where he had zero sacks or TFLs in the final 10 games. Despite leading the team in sacks (7.5) and QB hits (15) in 2024, Gary's production declined sharply, making him a candidate for trade or release.

items.headline: Kenny Clark traded to Dallas Cowboys with Micah Parsons in historic 2025 deal
items.summary: On August 28, 2025, the Packers traded three-time Pro Bowler Kenny Clark to the Cowboys along with two first-round picks (2026 & 2027) in exchange for Micah Parsons. Clark missed time with a foot injury in 2024 and had limited production. In seven years with Green Bay, Clark had 35 sacks and 417 tackles.

items.headline: Trent McDuffie dealt to Rams on four-year, $124M extension after knee injury
items.summary: In 2025, McDuffie hyperextended his knee in Week 14 vs Houston and was placed on season-ending IR. The Chiefs traded him to the Rams in the offseason, where he signed a four-year, $124 million extension ($100M guaranteed) at $31M/year—the league's highest-paid CB.

items.headline: Historic inaugural season: first expansion team to make WNBA playoffs
items.summary: The Golden State Valkyries, WNBA's 13th franchise, made history in 2025 as the first expansion team ever to reach the playoffs in their inaugural season. The team went 23-18, won the most games by any expansion team, and sold out all 11 home games, leading the WNBA in attendance.

items.headline: Announces retirement after 20 WNBA seasons with Phoenix
items.summary: Diana Taurasi announced her retirement in February 2025 after spending her entire 20-season WNBA career with the Phoenix Mercury. The WNBA's all-time leading scorer (10,646 points) cited being 'full' mentally and physically. She won 3 WNBA championships and 6 Olympic gold medals.

items.headline: Hodgkin's lymphoma survivor diagnosed in 2015
items.summary: Diagnosed with Hodgkin's lymphoma while at University of Pittsburgh in 2015. Underwent chemotherapy and recovered to continue his football career. Drafted by Pittsburgh Steelers in 2017.

items.headline: Cancer survivor advocate and philanthropist
items.summary: Channels his cancer survival into charitable work supporting cancer research and awareness. Public advocate of his Christian faith which he credits for strength during illness.

items.headline: Famous handlebar mustache became signature trademark
items.summary: Known for his distinctive, well-groomed handlebar mustache which became iconic and earned significant fan following and media attention.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent success story with Jacksonville
items.summary: Signed as undrafted free agent with Jacksonville Jaguars in 2019. Had immediate NFL impact as backup, starting multiple games as rookie.

items.headline: Veteran backup quarterback with NFL longevity
items.summary: Career backup quarterback who has played for multiple NFL teams including Patriots, Colts, Saints, Browns, and Dolphins throughout his NFL career.

items.headline: First-round tight end selection by Cardinals in 2022
items.summary: Drafted in first round by Arizona Cardinals. Has developed into featured receiving target in their offense contributing significantly to passing game.

items.headline: First overall pick 2023 NFL Draft by Cardinals
items.summary: Selected first overall in 2023 NFL Draft by Arizona Cardinals. Franchise cornerstone offensive tackle and anchor for offensive line.

items.headline: First-round linebacker selection in 2021 by Cardinals
items.summary: Drafted in first round of 2021 NFL Draft as versatile linebacker with coverage abilities to anchor Cardinals defensive scheme.

items.headline: Drafted 4th overall in 2021 by Atlanta Falcons
items.summary: Kyle Pitts was selected with the 4th overall pick in the 2021 NFL Draft by the Falcons, making him one of the highest-drafted tight ends in NFL history. He played college football at the University of Florida.

items.headline: Born October 6, 2000; Florida Gators standout
items.summary: Pitts was born on October 6, 2000, and is 6'6" tall, weighing 250 lbs. He was a standout tight end for the University of Florida before his NFL career.

items.summary: Wide receiver Jahan Dotson was traded to the Atlanta Falcons in the 2024 offseason after spending his early career with the Washington Commanders. He is known for his solid route-running and production.

items.headline: Survived shooting incident in Washington D.C. in 2022
items.summary: Running back Brian Robinson Jr. survived a shooting incident in August 2022 in Washington D.C. before the start of the NFL season. He returned to play for the Washington Commanders that season.

items.summary: Austin Hooper is an NFL tight end who has played for multiple teams including the Falcons, Cleveland Browns, Arizona Cardinals, and Tennessee Titans throughout his career.

items.headline: Son of former NFL cornerback Al Terrell
