items.headline: Marshall standout RB becomes Ravens' kickoff returner after overcoming neck injury
items.summary: Rasheen Ali was drafted by the Ravens in the fifth round of the 2024 NFL draft. Born February 16, 2001, he played college football for Marshall and had a breakout 2021 season as a freshman with 1,401 rushing yards and 23 TDs. He was placed on injured reserve before the 2025 season due to a neck injury but was activated on October 12, making his season debut on special teams in Week 6. He has served as one of the Ravens' top return men this season with 712 kickoff-return yards over 13 appearances.

items.headline: FCS national champ guard drafted by Ravens after dominating Big Ten competition
items.summary: Evan Beerntsen, born September 19, 2000, was selected by the Ravens in the seventh round (253rd overall) of the 2026 NFL Draft. He was a key piece of back-to-back FCS national championship teams at South Dakota State in 2022 and 2023, earning Second Team All-American and First Team All-MVFC honors. He played exclusively at right guard during college and was notably asked to take snaps at center during the NFL Combine.

items.headline: Memphis receiver signed as undrafted free agent after 25-year career arc
items.summary: Cortez Braham Jr. signed as an undrafted free agent with the Ravens. The 25-year-old wide receiver ended his collegiate career with Memphis, totaling 63 receptions for 889 yards and eight touchdowns over 12 games in 2025. Braham began his college career at Hutchinson Community College in Kansas from 2019 to 2021 before transferring. He signed a 3-year, $3,100,000 contract with an average annual salary of $1,033,333.

items.headline: Maryland transfer Bullock becomes Ravens' center after undrafted path and practice squad year
items.summary: Corey Bullock was signed by the Ravens as an undrafted free agent following the 2024 NFL draft. Born December 9, 2001, he transferred to the University of Maryland for his final two seasons after starting his college career at North Carolina Central. After spending a year on the practice squad, he beat out others for the starting center role. In 80 preseason snaps at center, Bullock earned a 74.7 run blocking grade from Pro Football Focus and did not allow a single quarterback pressure.

items.headline: Alabama tight end drafted by Ravens in fifth round after productive final season
items.summary: Josh Cuevas was selected by the Baltimore Ravens with the 173rd overall pick in the 5th round of the 2026 NFL Draft out of Alabama. He played college football for the Cal Poly Mustangs, Washington Huskies, and Alabama Crimson Tide. In his first season with Alabama in 2024, he caught 16 passes for 218 yards and a touchdown. In 2025 with the Crimson Tide, Cuevas was a vital piece of Alabama's offense with 37 receptions for 411 yards and four touchdowns (career-highs). At the NFL scouting combine, Cuevas ran the 40-yard dash in 4.65 seconds.

items.headline: Son of NBA legend Darryl Dawkins aims to earn Ravens roster spot at center
items.summary: Nick Dawkins signed with the Baltimore Ravens as an undrafted free agent after six seasons at Penn State. The 6-foot-4, 305-pound center is the son of NBA Hall of Famer Darryl Dawkins, who died from a heart attack when Nick was just 14 years old. Darryl played in the NBA for 14 seasons and was nicknamed 'The Chocolate Thunder.' Nick is also the cousin of NFL Hall of Famer Brian Dawkins and current Buffalo Bill Dion Dawkins. He played side-by-side at Penn State next to guard Vega Ioane, Baltimore's first-round pick.

items.headline: UConn QB Heisman candidate signs with Ravens as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Joe Fagnano, a quarterback from the University of Connecticut, signed a three-year contract with the Baltimore Ravens on April 28, 2026, after going undrafted in the 2026 NFL draft. He was a 2025 Heisman Award candidate and led the Huskies to a 9-3 record and bowl berth for the second-straight season. During the season, he finished 285-for-413 for 3,448 yards, 28 TDs and one interception with a 161.03 passing efficiency rating. The signing sets up a competition for the No. 3 quarterback spot behind Lamar Jackson and Tyler Huntley.

items.headline: ASU receiver returns to Ravens after broken collarbone injury
items.summary: Xavier Guillory, who spent last summer with the Ravens as an undrafted free agent, was re-signed by Baltimore. Guillory played college football for Idaho State and Arizona State. The 6-foot-2, 200-pound receiver suffered a season-ending broken collarbone in the 2025 preseason finale against the Washington Commanders, having three catches for 17 yards during preseason action. During the 2024 season with ASU, he caught 22 passes for 339 yards and five touchdowns in 13 games.

items.headline: Former Falcons guard reunites with Baltimore coaching staff on one-year deal
items.summary: Jovaughn Gwyn, a 26-year-old backup guard/center with the Atlanta Falcons, signed a one-year contract with the Baltimore Ravens. Gwyn played for Offensive Line Coach/Run Game Coordinator Dwayne Ledford in Atlanta last season, and they reunited in Baltimore. The seventh-round draft pick out of South Carolina in 2023 has played primarily on special teams. He is versatile, having played both center and guard, which was key for the Ravens after losing Pro Bowl center Tyler Linderbaum to the Las Vegas Raiders in free agency.

items.headline: Ravens RB Hill returns from injured reserve, extends contract through 2027
items.summary: Justice Hill was drafted by the Baltimore Ravens in the fourth round (113th overall) of the 2019 NFL Draft and made his NFL debut in Week 1 of the 2019 season in the Ravens' 59-10 victory over Miami. Born November 14, 1997, he is 5-10 and weighs 205 lbs. On September 20, 2024, Hill agreed to a new two-year, $6 million contract extension with Baltimore. On February 15, 2026, the Ravens activated him off the IR. Hill has been an important part of the Ravens' third-down operation with 21 catches on 27 targets for 169 yards and one touchdown.

items.headline: Utah backup QB extends deal with Ravens after career-best performance replacing Jackson
items.summary: Tyler Isaiah Huntley, nicknamed 'Snoop,' played college football for the Utah Utes, leading them to Pac-12 South Division titles in 2018 and 2019. He was signed by the Ravens as an undrafted free agent in 2020. On August 27, 2025, Huntley was signed to the practice squad to fill the third-string QB role and was signed to the active roster on October 24. On October 26, 2025, he started his first game of the season, filling in for injured Lamar Jackson, ending a four-game Ravens losing streak with a 30-16 win over Chicago. On March 7, 2026, Huntley signed a two-year contract extension worth up to $11 million.

items.headline: Michigan national champ receiver signed to Ravens practice squad after NFL stops
items.summary: Cornelius Johnson is an American professional football wide receiver for the Baltimore Ravens. He played college football for the Michigan Wolverines, winning three consecutive Big Ten Conference titles and a national championship in 2023. Johnson posted 138 receptions for 2,038 yards and 14 touchdowns over his college career. He was selected by the Los Angeles Chargers in the seventh round (253rd overall) of the 2024 NFL draft. After stints with the Chargers and Packers, Johnson was signed to the Baltimore Ravens' practice squad on September 9, 2025.

items.headline: LSU tackle Jones selected in third round by Ravens, recovering from labrum injury
items.summary: Emery Jones Jr. played college football for the LSU Tigers and was selected by the Ravens in the third round (91st overall) of the 2025 NFL draft. Born March 5, 2004, he is 6'5" and weighs 320 lbs. Jones took over LSU's starting right tackle job two games into his freshman year and never gave it up, starting 36 games in three years and earning second-team All-SEC honors the past two seasons. The Ravens anticipated moving him inside to guard for defense or receiving early snaps there. Ravens head coach John Harbaugh noted that Jones has a labrum injury and is expected to be sidelined until at least training camp.

items.headline: Oregon State Pac-12 OL of the Year signs with Ravens as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Gerad Christian-Lichtenhan is an American professional football offensive tackle for the Baltimore Ravens. He played college football for the Colorado Buffaloes and Oregon State Beavers. Christian-Lichtenhan signed with the Ravens as an undrafted free agent on May 4, 2025, but was waived on August 26 as part of final roster cuts and re-signed to the practice squad. At Oregon State, he started all 12 games in his first season with the Beavers in 2024 and was named the Pac-12 Offensive Lineman of the Year.

items.headline: Eastern Michigan 1,000-yard back signs with Ravens as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Dontae McMillan, a running back from Eastern Michigan University (Seattle, Wash./Chief Sealth), signed a free agent contract with the Baltimore Ravens on April 25. In 2025, McMillan had a career year for the Eagles, rushing 177 times for 1,015 yards and four scores while adding 33 catches for 375 yards and three scores. He became the first running back to earn All-MAC honors since Samson Evans in 2022. McMillan signed a 3-year, $3,100,000 contract with the Ravens with an average annual salary of $1,033,333. He was added to provide depth at running back behind NFL All-Pro Derrick Henry.

items.headline: Heisman runner-up QB signs with Ravens as undrafted free agent despite size concerns
items.summary: Diego Pavia is a quarterback for the Baltimore Ravens who finished runner-up in Heisman Trophy voting to Indiana quarterback Fernando Mendoza. He went unselected in the 2026 NFL Draft, becoming the first Heisman finalist since Jordan Lynch in 2014 to go undrafted. However, he officially signed a three-year undrafted free agent contract with the Ravens. Pavia was listed at 5-foot-10 but reportedly measured closer to 5-foot-9 at the Senior Bowl, raising size concerns and placing him among the shortest quarterbacks in the league if he makes the roster.

items.headline: Northern Iowa guard signs with Ravens as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Jared Penning signed with the Baltimore Ravens as an undrafted free agent after not being selected in the 2025 NFL draft. On August 26, 2025, he was waived by the team but was signed to the practice squad the following day. Penning is a 6-5, 319-pound guard who was a STATS third-team All-American in 2023 at Northern Iowa.

items.headline: Former Colts center signs with Ravens to replace Tyler Linderbaum
items.summary: Danny Pinter signed a one-year deal with the Baltimore Ravens with a maximum value of $2.75 million. The 29-year-old had spent the past six seasons with the Indianapolis Colts, playing in 77 games and making 10 starts. Pinter, originally a fifth-round pick out of Ball State in 2020, hasn't become a full-time starter yet but could with a change of scenery with Baltimore. The 6-foot-4 Pinter joins Corey Bullock and Jovaughn Gwyn as top competitors for the starting center spot and could also serve as a sixth offensive lineman at right guard.

items.headline: Washington tackle drafted by Ravens earns All-Rookie honors on strong 2025 season
items.summary: Roger Rosengarten is an offensive tackle for the Baltimore Ravens who was selected with pick No. 62 in the second round of the 2024 NFL Draft. Born June 3, 2002, he played college football for the Washington Huskies. Rosengarten didn't allow any sacks in his 1,158 pass-blocking snaps for Washington and ran the fastest 40-yard dash (4.92 seconds) of any offensive lineman in the draft. He started 16 games (including playoffs) in 2024 and earned a spot on the Pro Football Writers Association All-Rookie team. In the 2025 season, he earned a 74.1 overall PFF grade, ranked 26th among 89 qualified offensive tackles.

items.headline: Ravens franchise left tackle returns after Pro Bowl season with three-year extension
items.summary: Ronnie Garrison Stanley is an offensive tackle for the Baltimore Ravens who was selected 6th overall in the first round of the 2016 NFL draft. He earned Pro Bowl and first-team All-Pro honors in 2019. All nine of his NFL seasons have been with Baltimore. On March 9, 2025, Stanley re-signed with the Ravens on a three-year, $60 million contract. He started all 17 games in 2024 and made the Pro Bowl for the first time in five years. Stanley explains his decision to return, citing the 'unfinished business' he and the Ravens eye for a Super Bowl run.

items.headline: Bengals cornerback rejoins Ravens on new contract extension
items.summary: Chidobe Awuzie is a cornerback for the Baltimore Ravens who played college football for the Colorado Buffaloes and was selected by the Dallas Cowboys in the second round of the 2017 NFL draft. He is very familiar with the AFC North, having played for the Cincinnati Bengals for three seasons (2021-2023). The Ravens signed him to a one-year contract on March 25, 2025, and re-signed him on March 12, 2026, on a one-year deal worth $5 million. He made 14 appearances (including 5 starts) in 2025 with 43 tackles and seven passes defended. Awuzie earned a 76.4 overall PFF defensive grade in 2025, 10th among qualified cornerbacks.

items.headline: Ravens All-Pro DT Madubuike undergoes neck surgery, expected to return in 2026
items.summary: Nnamdi Madubuike is a defensive tackle for the Baltimore Ravens who played college football for Texas A&M. During the 2024 regular season, he recorded 43 total tackles, 6.5 sacks, and 11 tackles for loss, earning his second consecutive Pro Bowl selection. Following the Week 2 win against Cleveland in 2025, Madubuike showed symptoms of a neck injury. On September 29, 2025, he was placed on injured reserve for the remainder of the season. In April 2026, Madubuike had neck surgery that would allow him to play in the 2026 season. The Ravens selected him with the 71st overall pick in the third round of the 2020 NFL draft. During the 2024 offseason, he switched to his Nigerian name Nnamdi in recognition of his heritage.

items.headline: Ravens DT Jones emerges as defensive star with massive extension through 2028
items.summary: Travis Jones was drafted by the Baltimore Ravens in the third round (76th overall) of the 2022 NFL draft. The 6-foot-4, 341-pound Jones is widely regarded as one of the better run-stopping nose tackles in the NFL. After a breakout 2024 season as a starter, he followed with a career season in 2025. He wrapped the 2025 season with a 79.2 PFF overall grade (second-highest on the defense) and led all Baltimore defenders in pressures (47), sacks (five) and PFF run-defense grade (81.1). The Ravens agreed to a three-year contract extension worth $40.5 million over three years with $25 million guaranteed, keeping him through the 2028 season. Pro Football Focus identified him as the Ravens' 'secret superstar.'

items.headline: 40-year-old Calais Campbell returns to Ravens for 19th NFL season
items.summary: The Ravens reunited with veteran defensive tackle Calais Campbell, who previously played for Baltimore from 2020-2022. Campbell signed a one-year deal worth approximately $5.5 million to return for a 19th NFL season in 2026. The 6-foot-8, 315-pound defensive end will turn 40 years old on September 1, 2026. Campbell recorded 6.5 sacks last season for the Arizona Cardinals (which would have led the Ravens). His 15% pass rush win rate was third-best in the league among defensive tackles, and his 117 career sacks is fourth-most among active NFL players.

items.headline: Georgia national champ safety joins Ravens as first-round pick alongside Kyle Hamilton
items.summary: Malaki Starks is a safety for the Baltimore Ravens who played college football for the Georgia Bulldogs, winning a national championship in 2022. The Ravens selected Starks 27th overall in the 2025 draft. He joins All-Pro Kyle Hamilton to form a dynamic safety duo. Rookie Malaki Starks earned NFL's highest-graded safety honors the past two weeks with back-to-back interceptions. He can play all over the secondary (400 snaps as deep safety, 271 in the slot, 215 in the box last year). Starks has premier intangibles - smarts, leadership, and character. Ravens GM Eric DeCosta said of him: 'He's wired like a Raven.'

items.headline: Veteran nose tackle Jenkins becomes primary DL after Madubuike injury
items.summary: Johnathan Jenkins, born July 11, 1989, is a nose tackle for the Ravens who played college football for the Georgia Bulldogs and was selected by the New Orleans Saints in the third round of the 2013 NFL draft. He has also played for the Seattle Seahawks, Chicago Bears, New York Giants, Miami Dolphins, and Las Vegas Raiders before signing with Baltimore on May 16, 2025. After Nnamdi Madubuike suffered a season-ending neck injury early in the 2025 season, Jenkins assumed an expanded role as the Ravens' primary nose tackle. Jenkins signed a one-year, $2.1 million extension with the Ravens. In 2025, he appeared in 16 games with 36 total tackles, 1.0 sacks, two forced fumbles and one pass defensed.

items.headline: Nigerian International Pathway Program graduate makes Ravens debut, becomes IPP success
items.summary: Basil Chijioke 'C.J.' Okoye, born October 20, 2001, is a Nigerian defensive tackle for the Ravens. He is 6-foot-6 and weighs 370 pounds. Okoye hails from Agbogugu, Nigeria, and came to America in 2023 as part of the NFL's International Pathway Program. He was allocated to the Los Angeles Chargers as a defensive lineman in 2023, recording a sack in his first preseason game but was later waived. He spent both the 2023 and 2024 seasons on the Chargers' practice squad. In January, Okoye signed a reserve/futures contract with the Ravens. He made his NFL regular season debut on September 28 and became the first African IPP graduate to appear in a regular season NFL game. Okoye has made eight tackles in three games.

items.headline: Ravens first-round CB Wiggins emerges as top emerging corner in 2025 season
items.summary: Nate Wiggins is a cornerback for the Baltimore Ravens who played college football for the Clemson Tigers. He was drafted by the Ravens in the first round of the 2024 NFL draft and became the first first-rounder from the 2024 draft to sign a contract. In the 2025 season, Wiggins earned a 63.8 overall PFF defensive grade, 54th among 114 qualified cornerbacks. The 22-year-old first-round pick proved why he's considered one of the NFL's top emerging corners in 2025, leading Baltimore in passes defensed and tying for the 12th-most league-wide. He played a career-high 1,024 defensive snaps. He remains under contract next season and is expected to continue blossoming as one of the league's premier boundary cornerbacks.

items.headline: Marshall sack leader drafted in second round, poised for 2026 breakout after slow rookie year
items.summary: Michael Green is a linebacker for the Baltimore Ravens, born July 28, 2003. He played college football for the Virginia Cavaliers and Marshall Thundering Herd, being named the 2024 Sun Belt Player of the Year with the latter. Green was selected by the Ravens with the 59th overall pick in the second round of the 2025 NFL draft. The talented pass rusher led the nation with 17 sacks in his final year at Marshall but slipped to 59th overall due to off-field questions. He led the OLB room in snaps in his rookie season but only produced 3.5 sacks and seven tackles for loss. The 22-year-old showed struggles setting a hard edge against the run or consistently generating pressure. Ravens head coach Jesse Minter expressed confidence in Green, expecting a big jump from rookie to sophomore year.

items.headline: Illinois State undrafted safety earns promotion to Ravens 53-man roster after special teams star turn
items.summary: Keondre Jackson is a safety for the Ravens who was signed as an undrafted free agent in 2025 and played college football for the Illinois State Redbirds. The undrafted rookie is making big-time impact on special teams that has fueled the Ravens' turnaround. He recorded five tackles and downed a punt inside the 10-yard line during special teams performances. Jackson forced a fumble and recovery on kickoff coverage that helped secure the Ravens' 27-19 victory over Minnesota. He has recorded six tackles in four games, with his speed and tackling ability helping Baltimore elevate its special teams play. The Ravens signed Jackson to the 53-man roster after releasing Sanoussi Kane.

items.headline: Clemson LB Simpson returns to starting role after Buchanan ACL injury
items.summary: Trenton Simpson is a linebacker for the Ravens who was selected in the third round (86th overall) of the 2023 NFL draft. The Clemson product earned a 70.7 overall PFF defensive grade in the 2025 season, 27th among qualified linebackers. Simpson recorded 66 total tackles (34 solo), including 2.5 sacks, and one pass defensed across all 17 regular-season games. However, despite appearing in all 17 games, he started just six contests and primarily served as the team's top reserve inside linebacker. When rookie Teddye Buchanan suffered a season-ending knee injury, Simpson stepped back into the spotlight as the starting inside linebacker alongside All-Pro Roquan Smith.

items.headline: Notre Dame TE Smythe joins Ravens to fill blocking role left by Kolar
items.summary: Durham Smythe is a tight end for the Baltimore Ravens who played college football for Notre Dame. On March 12, 2026, Smythe signed a one-year contract worth $3 million, including a $1.5 million signing bonus and $3 million guaranteed. The 30-year-old was expected to fill the role held by Charlie Kolar and join veteran Mark Andrews in the rotation. Smythe was a fourth-round pick by the Miami Dolphins in 2018 and spent his first seven seasons with Miami before joining the Bears last season. During his career, Smythe has 136 catches for 1,253 yards and three touchdowns.

items.headline: Army fullback Scott pursues dream position with Ravens after multiple college roles
items.summary: Lucas Scott, born August 25, 2003, is a fullback for the Baltimore Ravens who played college football for the Army Black Knights. He signed as an undrafted free agent following the 2025 NFL Draft. Scott is 6-foot-3 and weighs 290 pounds. At West Point, Scott played multiple positions: defensive line as a freshman, tight end as a sophomore, right guard as a junior, and right tackle as a senior. He told the Ravens at the Hula Bowl in January that he loved watching All-Pro fullback Patrick Ricard and said fullback wouldn't mind following in his footsteps, calling it his dream position. He saw playing time in the preseason but was waived on August 26 and signed to the practice squad on August 28.

items.headline: Ravens reunite with guard Simpson on three-year deal after Jets stint
items.summary: John Simpson is an offensive guard for the Ravens who played college football for the Clemson Tigers. The Ravens reunited with Simpson on a three-year, $30 million contract in March 2026, after he had previously played for them in 2023. Simpson, 28, was a top-10 starting guard in the NFL in 2024 according to Pro Football Focus and allowed just 20 quarterback pressures that entire season. He was the Ravens' starting left guard in 2023 before moving to the New York Jets for two years. Simpson was originally selected by the Las Vegas Raiders in the fourth round (109th overall) of the 2020 NFL draft.

items.headline: USC guard Vorhees overcomes ACL injury at combine, emerges as full-time starter
items.summary: Andrew Michael Vorhees is an offensive guard for the Ravens who played college football for the USC Trojans. He was selected by Baltimore in the seventh round (229th overall) of the 2023 NFL draft. During the 2023 NFL Combine, Vorhees tore his ACL during one of the drills and was slated to be a late first to early third round pick but fell to the seventh round due to the injury. He missed the 2023 season recovering from the torn ACL. Vorhees played in 15 games with 3 starts in 2024 before becoming the full-time starter in 2025. In 2025, he earned a 54.6 overall PFF grade, 60th among 81 qualified offensive guards.

items.headline: HBCU tackle Vinson becomes only draft pick from historically Black college in 2025
items.summary: Carson Vinson is an offensive tackle for the Ravens who was drafted from Alabama A&M in the fifth round (141st overall) with a pick gained after a trade with Tennessee. Standing 6-foot-7 and weighing 321 pounds, Vinson has the tools to mold into a strong backup early in his career with potential to become a down-the-road starter. He was a five-year starter at left tackle and is Alabama A&M's first draft pick since 2011 and first offensive player drafted in more than 30 years. As the only HBCU player selected in the 2025 draft, Vinson proudly carries the foundation he built at his school into the NFL. He battled two SEC teams (Vanderbilt and Auburn) and had a strong showing at the Senior Bowl, the only HBCU player invited.

items.headline: Virginia Tech DT Peebles is grandson of Hall of Famer Doug Wilkerson
items.summary: Aeneas Peebles is a defensive tackle for the Ravens who was selected in Round 6 of the 2025 NFL Draft (210th overall) out of Virginia Tech. He played college football for the Duke Blue Devils and Virginia Tech Hokies, transferring to Virginia Tech in the 2023 winter transfer portal to remain in the ACC. The Virginia Tech product is smaller than most DTs at 289 pounds and 6-feet tall, but his quickness makes him tough to block. Peebles is a gap-shooting 3-technique DT with impressive agility, quickness and get-off. He was named after Hall of Fame defensive back Aeneas Williams. His grandfather, Doug Wilkerson, was a first-round pick in 1970 who played 15 seasons in the NFL at defensive end and offensive guard, making three Pro Bowls.

items.headline: Penn State CB Oruwariye returns to football after 2021 six-interception season in Detroit
items.summary: Amani Oruwariye is a cornerback for the Ravens who played college football at Penn State. He is in his seventh NFL season and has played in 61 games with 40 starts. A fifth-round pick out of Penn State in 2019, he spent his first four seasons with the Detroit Lions and has also played for the Giants, Jaguars, Cowboys and Titans. On October 1, 2025, Oruwariye signed with the Ravens' practice squad. He had a spectacular 2021 season in Detroit when he started 14 games and made six interceptions. Over his seven-year career, Oruwariye has appeared in 61 games and made 40 starts.

items.headline: Patriots safety Hawkins joins Ravens on two-year deal to complement Hamilton and Starks
items.summary: Jaylinn Hawkins is a safety for the Ravens who played college football for the California Golden Bears and was selected by the Atlanta Falcons in the fourth round of the 2020 NFL draft. The Ravens signed the former New England Patriot Hawkins on a two-year, $10 million deal. Hawkins was a 15-game starter for AFC champion Patriots and led them with four interceptions, along with 71 tackles, six passes defensed, 1.5 sacks, and five QB hits. The Ravens sought a veteran who could complement starters Kyle Hamilton and Malaki Starks, with Hamilton having a versatile role while they needed a reliable third safety for significant snaps. In 2025, Hawkins earned an 82.4 overall PFF defensive grade, ranking 5th among 98 qualified safeties.

items.headline: Canadian OLB Robinson returns from broken foot injury after strong rookie season
items.summary: Tavius Patrick Robinson is a Canadian linebacker for the Ravens, born January 3, 1999. He played U Sports football at Guelph and college football at Ole Miss. Robinson was selected in the fourth round (124th overall) of the 2023 NFL draft by Baltimore. As a rookie, he played in all 17 games notching 26 tackles and one sack. He began the 2025 season as one of Baltimore's starting linebackers but suffered a broken foot in Week 6 against Los Angeles and was placed on injured reserve on October 22, 2025. He was later activated to the 53-man roster to face Cincinnati. At Ole Miss in 2022, Robinson started all 13 games, recording 44 tackles (17 solo), seven sacks, five forced fumbles and two fumble recoveries.

items.headline: UCLA LB Jones claimed off waivers by Ravens from Chicago Bears
items.summary: Carl Jones Jr., born August 8, 2001, is a linebacker for the Ravens who played college football for the UCLA Bruins. He is 6-0, weighs 223 pounds, and is from Bakersfield, California. On December 8, 2025, Jones was claimed off waivers by the Ravens. Jones appeared in nine games for the Bears this season, compiling 11 tackles on 148 special teams snaps before being waived. He signed with the Bears as an undrafted rookie last year, appearing in one game and spending nearly the entire season on their practice squad.

items.headline: North Carolina LB Rucker signs with Ravens as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Kaimon Rucker signed with the Ravens as an undrafted free agent on May 13, 2025. The 6-foot-2, 265-pound linebacker is 24 years old and earned second-team All-ACC honors in 2023 at North Carolina after five seasons with the team. His 2024 season was cut short with a fractured fibula, but he made 16 tackles and six sacks in eight games.

items.headline: Ravens DT Washington undergoes ACL surgery in November, status uncertain
items.summary: Broderick Washington Jr. is a defensive tackle for the Ravens who was selected in the fifth round (170th overall) of the 2020 NFL draft. He played college football at Texas Tech, appearing in 49 games over four seasons (2016-2019) with 138 total tackles, 15.5 tackles for loss, and 8.5 sacks. Standing 6-feet-2 and weighing 315 pounds, he is known for interior pass-rushing ability and run-stopping. On August 9, 2023, Washington signed a three-year, $17.5 million contract extension with Baltimore. In 2024, he appeared in 15 games with 21 tackles, 2 sacks, 2 passes defensed, and 1 forced fumble. In 2025, he appeared in the first three games with 4 tackles and 1 pass defensed before suffering an Achilles injury requiring surgery in early November.

items.headline: Syracuse DL 'Tank' Wilson signs with Ravens as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Dion 'Tank' Wilson Jr. is a defensive lineman from Syracuse who signed with the Ravens as an undrafted free agent. The 6-foot-5 defensive lineman spent the last two seasons with the Orange after transferring from New Mexico State. In 2025, he started in 11 games for Syracuse, notching 29 tackles (2.5 for loss). Wilson signed a 3-year, $3,100,000 contract with the Ravens with an average annual salary of $1,033,333.

items.headline: Iowa State cornerback drafted by Ravens in fourth round, considered draft steal
items.summary: T.J. Tampa is a cornerback for the Ravens who played college football for the Iowa State Cyclones. He was born on March 13, 2002, and drafted by the Ravens in the fourth round of the 2024 NFL draft. Tampa has great size at 6-foot-1 and 194 pounds. As a full-time starter the past two seasons, he registered 83 tackles, three interceptions, and 19 passes defensed. The Ravens got great value in the fourth round with Tampa, who was projected by many to be an early Day 2 pick. Tampa secured the Ravens' fifth-straight win with a fourth-quarter interception in one of his early season appearances.

items.headline: Arkansas cornerback Robinson undrafted rookie makes Ravens 53-man roster
items.summary: Marquise Robinson is a cornerback for the Ravens. He is 6'0", weighs 192 lbs, was born on July 16, 2002 (age 23), attended the University of Arkansas, and is a rookie with no prior NFL experience. Robinson was undrafted and signed with the Ravens in 2025. He signed a 1-year contract worth $885,000 with a base salary of $885,000 for the 2026 season. During Ravens OTA practices, Robinson demonstrated his skills as an undrafted cornerback by making a pass breakup.

items.headline: Rutgers CB Longerbeam drafted in sixth round by Ravens, placed on IR
items.summary: Robert Longerbeam is a cornerback for the Ravens born January 18, 2001. He played college football for the Rutgers Scarlet Knights and was selected by the Ravens in the sixth round (212th overall) of the 2025 NFL draft. Longerbeam was a three-year starter in the Big Ten with 42 career passes defensed. He has explosive traits (4.39 40-yard dash) but a slight frame (5-11, 175 pounds). Sixth-round rookie Robert Longerbeam (knee) was placed on injured reserve after missing the Ravens' preseason opener and Saturday's practice.

items.headline: Youngstown State CB Keyon Martin is son of former NFL cornerback Manny Martin
items.summary: Keyon Martin is a cornerback for the Ravens who was signed as an undrafted free agent in 2025. He played college football for Youngstown State and Louisiana. Martin was born May 16, 2001, and grew up in Fort Lauderdale, Florida. His father, Manny Martin, played five seasons in the NFL as a cornerback. After going unselected in the 2025 NFL draft, Martin signed with the Ravens after impressing at their rookie minicamp. He was a standout performer in preseason, posting a safety in the second preseason game and returning an interception for a touchdown in the third, making the Ravens' 53-man roster as an undrafted rookie.

items.headline: Arizona kicker Loop becomes Ravens' first-ever drafted kicker, misses game-winner vs Steelers
items.summary: Tyler Loop is a placekicker for the Ravens who played college football for the Arizona Wildcats and was selected by the Ravens in the sixth round of the 2025 NFL draft. Loop became the first kicker drafted in Ravens' history. After making 5 of 6 field goals in the preseason win over Dallas, Loop was officially designated as the Ravens' new placekicker to replace Justin Tucker. During his rookie season in 2025, Loop connected on 30 of 34 field goal attempts (88.2% conversion rate, 4th among rookie kickers). In Week 18, Loop missed a game-winning 44-yard field goal wide right against Pittsburgh that would have sent the Ravens to the playoffs. Loop explained he mishit the ball, hitting it too low on his foot.

items.headline: Scottsdale, Arizona native with college football diversity
items.summary: Kyle Allen was born and raised in Scottsdale, Arizona, and attended Desert Mountain High School there. He played college football for Texas A&M and Houston, starting his career at A&M before transferring to Houston where he was the starting quarterback for the Cougars.

items.headline: Brother of NFL running back Dalvin Cook; father passed away
items.summary: James Cook III is the younger brother of NFL running back Dalvin Cook and one of six children. His father, James Cook Sr., passed away, and Cook missed the 2021 Peach Bowl following his death. His grandmother noted that both grandsons were in the NFL, with the family channeling strength through his mother Varondria Burnett and siblings.

items.headline: Suffered cardiac arrest on field; diagnosed with commotio cordis
items.summary: On January 2, 2023, during a Monday Night Football game against the Cincinnati Bengals, Hamlin suffered cardiac arrest after making a tackle. He was later confirmed to have commotio cordis, an extremely rare condition where cardiac rhythm is disrupted by a blow to the chest during a specific 40-millisecond window in the heart's electrical cycle.

items.headline: Pro Bowl alternate in 2025 after remarkable comeback
items.summary: After recovering from cardiac arrest and being medically cleared, Hamlin made his first start in 2024 against Arizona Cardinals. He played in 13 games in 2024, recording 83 total tackles, 2 interceptions, 5 defended passes, and a fumble recovery. He was named a first alternate for the 2025 Pro Bowl.

items.headline: Philadelphia native acquired in major 2026 trade
items.summary: DJ Moore is from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, where he attended Imhotep Institute Charter High School. On March 11, 2026, the Chicago Bears traded Moore and a 2026 fifth-round pick to the Buffalo Bills in exchange for a 2026 second-round pick, bringing him to Buffalo.

items.headline: Three-time Super Bowl champion with Kansas City Chiefs
items.summary: Mecole Hardman Jr. played college football at Georgia and was drafted in 2019 Round 2, Pick 56 by Kansas City. He earned a Pro Bowl appearance as a rookie and won 3 Super Bowl rings as a key contributor for the Chiefs before time with the Jets, back to the Chiefs, and then the Packers.

items.headline: Career-saving opportunity with Bills on reserve futures contract
items.summary: On January 19, 2026, Hardman signed a reserve/futures contract with Buffalo after a season bouncing between practice squads and teams. He had scored in the Bills' Divisional Round game against Denver and contributed on kick return duties.

items.headline: Son of Hall of Fame running back Frank Gore Sr.
items.summary: Frank Gore Jr. is the son of Frank Gore Sr., a future Hall of Famer. Frank Gore Sr. ended his career in 2020 with 16,000 rushing yards (third-most in NFL history), had 10 seasons with San Francisco as the franchise rushing yards leader, earned five Pro Bowl selections and one second-team All-Pro selection. Gore Sr. will be considered for Pro Football Hall of Fame induction in 2026.

items.headline: Tennessee native who redefined Bills tight end production
items.summary: Dawson Alan Knox was born November 14, 1996, in Brentwood, Tennessee. He played college football for Ole Miss and was selected by the Bills in the third round of the 2019 NFL draft. Knox had his first NFL touchdown in Week 3 against Cincinnati, and broke the franchise touchdown record for tight ends in 2021.

items.headline: Surpassed franchise tight end touchdown record in 2025
items.summary: Knox surpassed Pete Metzelaars's team record for most career touchdowns by a tight end in 2025. He has been a major part of the Bills' passing attack and signed a three-year contract extension with Buffalo on March 10, 2026.

items.headline: Changed last name to honor stepfather Brian Johnson
items.summary: Chauncey 'C.J.' Gardner-Johnson (born December 20, 1997) legally changed his last name from Gardner Jr. to Gardner-Johnson on December 31, 2017, in honor of his stepfather, Brian Johnson. He played college football for the Florida Gators.

items.headline: Won Super Bowl with Eagles; joined Bills as free agent
items.summary: After being drafted by New Orleans in 2019, Gardner-Johnson was traded to Philadelphia in 2022 where he had a career-high 6 interceptions. He played for Detroit in 2023, returned to the Eagles in 2024, and appeared in all four postseason games en route to a Super Bowl trophy. He briefly played for Houston before signing with Buffalo on a one-year deal worth up to $6 million.

items.headline: Iowa native from small town; drafted from Northern Iowa
items.summary: Spencer Brown was born February 28, 1998, and grew up in Lenox, Iowa. He attended Lenox High School where he played football, baseball, basketball, and ran track. He played college football for Northern Iowa, redshirting his freshman year as he transitioned from tight end to offensive tackle, gaining nearly 80 pounds.

items.headline: Breakout 2023 season; started all 17 games
items.summary: After two inconsistent, injury-prone seasons with limited starts, Brown started all 17 games in 2023, earning the third highest play-based pay incentive in the league for his improved play. Standing 6'8" and weighing 311 lbs, he has established himself as the starting right tackle for the Bills.

items.headline: Houston native; consensus five-star recruit and Bill Willis Trophy winner
items.summary: Edward Oliver was born December 12, 1997, in Houston, Texas. He attended Westfield High School and was rated as a consensus five-star recruit ranked among the top players in his class. He won the Bill Willis Trophy as the first freshman ever to win the award.

items.headline: Outland Trophy winner; drafted 9th overall by Bills in 2019
items.summary: At the University of Houston, Oliver was selected to the Walter Camp All-America first team as a sophomore and won the Outland Trophy, presented to the nation's top interior lineman. The Bills selected him 9th overall in the 2019 draft. He signed a four-year, $68 million contract extension with Buffalo on June 3, 2023, and was activated from injured reserve before the 2026 playoffs.

items.headline: San Francisco native with multiple college transfers
items.summary: Ray Davis was born November 11, 1999, in San Francisco, California. He is 5'8" and weighs 220 lbs. Davis began his college career at Temple, where he ran for 936 yards and 8 touchdowns as a freshman and was named first-team Freshman All-American by PFF. He later transferred to Vanderbilt and then entered the transfer portal again for Kentucky.

items.headline: Louisiana native; three-position All-ACC honors
items.summary: Keon Coleman was born May 17, 2003, and is 6'4", 215 lbs. He attended Opelousas Catholic School in Opelousas, Louisiana, and played both football and basketball in high school. He played college football for Michigan State and Florida State.

items.headline: Exceptional multi-position honors in college; second round draft pick
items.summary: Coleman was named First Team All-ACC at wide receiver, all-purpose, and specialist positions, joining Will Shipley as the only college football players to earn first-team honors at three positions. He declared for the 2024 NFL draft after skipping Florida State's Orange Bowl. The Bills selected him 33rd overall in 2024.

items.headline: Canadian-born wide receiver from Ontario
items.summary: Joshua Palmer grew up in Brampton, Ontario, Canada. He played college football for the Tennessee Volunteers and was selected by the Los Angeles Chargers in the third round of the 2021 NFL draft before being traded to Buffalo.

items.headline: Three-year contract with Bills in March 2025
items.summary: On March 13, 2025, Palmer signed a three-year deal with the Buffalo Bills, bringing the Canadian-born receiver to Western New York.

items.headline: Texas native from La Porte; breakout 2023 season
items.summary: Terrel Bernard grew up in La Porte, Texas, and attended La Porte High School. He played college football for Baylor, redshirting his freshman year. The Bills selected him in the third round, 89th overall, in the 2022 NFL draft.

items.headline: Named team captain alongside Josh Allen; breakout defensive player
items.summary: After being named the starting inside linebacker in 2023, Bernard had a breakout season, playing all 17 games and leading the Bills in tackles. He became the first linebacker since 1991 to record at least 6 sacks, 3 interceptions, and 3 fumble recoveries in the same season. Head coach Sean McDermott named him his breakout player. He was named a team captain in 2024 and signed a four-year, $50 million contract extension on March 7, 2025.

items.headline: Son of former MLB player Steve Buechele; TV analyst for Rangers
items.summary: Shane Buechele was born January 8, 1998. His father, Steve, played in Major League Baseball from 1985 to 1995 and is currently a TV analyst for the Texas Rangers, providing his son with sports mentorship.

items.headline: Two-college quarterback; made NFL debut in 2026
items.summary: Buechele attended Lamar High School in Arlington, Texas, passing for 6,379 yards with 73 touchdowns and rushing for 1,805 yards and 21 touchdowns. He played college football for Texas and SMU, finishing with 11,660 passing yards and 87 touchdowns. He signed with KC as an undrafted free agent in 2021 and made his NFL debut on January 4, 2026, against Las Vegas, completing 7 of 14 passes for 88 yards.

items.headline: Maryland native; Villanova All-American cornerback
items.summary: Christian Benford was born in Baltimore, Maryland, and grew up in Randallstown, Maryland, where he attended Randallstown High School. He was named All-Metro and All-Baltimore County as a senior and played in the Big 33 Football Classic. He played college football for Villanova and was named an All-American by the Associated Press, Stats Perform, and Hero Sports.

items.headline: Touchdown scoop-and-score and pick-six in 2024 season
items.summary: The Bills drafted Benford in the sixth round (185th overall) of 2022. As of 2025, he has become one of the top cornerbacks in the NFL with two interceptions in each of the last three years. In 2024, he scored his first career touchdown on a scoop-and-score against the Steelers and followed with a 63-yard pick-six against the Bengals. He signed a four-year, $72 million contract extension in March 2025.

items.headline: South Carolina native; high school kicker award winner
items.summary: Tyler Royce Bass was born February 14, 1997, in Columbia, South Carolina. He is 5'10" and weighs 183 lbs. Bass attended Dutch Fork High School in Irmo, South Carolina, where he also played soccer. His grandmother convinced him to try out for football due to his strong soccer leg. He won the Chris Sailer Award, given to the best high school kicker, following his senior season.

items.headline: Broke franchise scoring record; three 50+ yard field goals in one game
items.summary: Bass was selected by the Bills in the sixth round of the 2020 draft and beat out incumbent Stephen Hauschka for the starting position. His 141 points in 2021 broke Steve Christie's 1998 franchise record of 140 points, and he broke Scott Norwood's franchise records for extra points attempted and made in a season. He became the first Bills kicker to make three field goals longer than 50 yards in one game, and just the second in NFL history to make 3 field goals longer than 54 yards in the same game.

items.headline: Maryland native; single-season yards per carry record
items.summary: Ty Johnson was born September 17, 1997, in Cumberland, Maryland. He is 5'10" and weighs 210 lbs. He attended Fort Hill High School and played college football for Maryland. In his sophomore season in 2016, Johnson set the Terrapins' single season yards per carry record, averaging 9.1 yards per rushing attempt.

items.headline: Seven-season NFL veteran; played all 17 games for Bills in 2024
items.summary: Johnson was drafted in 2019 and has played for multiple teams including the Bills, Jets, and Lions. In 2024, he played in all 17 games for Buffalo with one start, recording 41 carries for 213 yards and 18 receptions for 284 yards with 4 total touchdowns.

items.headline: Illinois native; Vanderbilt undrafted free agent
items.summary: Trent Sherfield was born February 25, 1996, in Danville, Illinois. He stands 6'1" and weighs 205 lbs. He was signed by the Cardinals as an undrafted free agent out of Vanderbilt.

items.headline: Multiple NFL stops; re-signed with Bills in 2026
items.summary: Sherfield spent time with Arizona, San Francisco, and Miami before signing with Buffalo in March 2023, catching 11 passes for 86 yards and one touchdown. He later played for Minnesota and Denver before returning to the Bills on a one-year contract on March 26, 2026.

items.headline: First Australian to score a point in an NFL game
items.summary: Mitch Wishnowsky was born March 3, 1992, in Gosnells, Western Australia, a suburb of Perth. He grew up playing Australian rules football but was forced to give up the sport at age 18 due to repeated shoulder injuries. He played college football for Santa Barbara City and Utah, where he won the Ray Guy Award and was a unanimous All-American in 2016. He is the first Australian to score a point in an NFL game.

items.headline: Super Bowl LIV participant with 49ers; re-signed with Bills
items.summary: Wishnowsky was selected by San Francisco in the fourth round of the 2019 NFL draft and reached Super Bowl LIV as a rookie, though the 49ers lost 31-20 to Kansas City. After seven seasons with the 49ers, he joined the Bills on September 30, 2025, replacing injured Australian punter Cameron Johnston. He re-signed on March 13, 2026.

items.headline: Brother of fellow NFL player Laiatu Latu
items.summary: Keleki Latu was born August 13, 2002, in Sacramento, California. He and his brother attended Jesuit High School in Carmichael, California, where they both played football and rugby union. He is the brother of fellow NFL player Laiatu Latu.

items.headline: Multiple college transfer; undrafted free agent with Bills
items.summary: Latu stands 6'7" and weighs 244 lbs. He played college football for California, Nevada, and Washington. After going unselected in the 2025 draft, he signed with the Bills as an undrafted free agent, was waived on August 26, re-signed to the practice squad on August 27, and was elevated for the Week 11 game against Tampa Bay.

items.headline: Mississippi native with underdog mentality
items.summary: Dee Alford was born in Hernando, Mississippi, a small town with a population under 20,000. He played college football for Tusculum. On March 12, 2026, Alford signed a three-year contract with the Buffalo Bills worth up to $21 million, bringing his underdog mentality to Buffalo's defense.

items.headline: Georgia native and Bills' longest-tenured player
items.summary: Reid Ferguson was born March 24, 1994, in Buford, Georgia. He stands 6'2" and weighs 235 lbs. He graduated early from Buford High School and enrolled at LSU in spring 2012, where he developed as an accurate and powerful long snapper with strong blocking ability.

items.headline: Highest-paid long snapper in NFL; longest-tenured Bill
items.summary: Ferguson signed with the Bills on May 6, 2016, as an undrafted free agent and has been with the team ever since, making him the team's longest-tenured player as of 2026. He signed a four-year contract that made him the highest average annual value long snapper in the NFL and awarded him the largest signing bonus ever for a long snapper.

items.headline: Brown University defensive end; strong college production
items.summary: Michael Hoecht played college football for the Brown Bears and finished his collegiate career with 174 tackles, 29.5 tackles for loss, and 16.5 sacks. He was signed by the Los Angeles Rams as an undrafted free agent following the 2020 NFL draft.

items.headline: Multi-year contract with Bills in 2025
items.summary: On March 14, 2025, Hoecht signed a three-year, $21 million contract with the Buffalo Bills, coming off several seasons with the Los Angeles Rams.

items.headline: Multi-college transfer; Jacksonville State to UCF
items.summary: Tylan Grable played college football for Jacksonville State and UCF. He arrived at Jacksonville State in 2018 and redshirted that year, then stayed through 2021 before transferring to UCF. At UCF, he was a full-time starter at left tackle and earned Honorable Mention All-Big 12 in 2023.

items.headline: 2024 NFL draft pick; sixth round selection
items.summary: The Buffalo Bills selected UCF offensive tackle Tylan Grable in Round 6 of the 2024 NFL Draft with the 204th overall pick, addressing depth on the offensive line.

items.headline: Michigan native and first-round draft pick in 2025
items.summary: Maxwell Hairston was born August 6, 2003, in West Bloomfield, Michigan. He played college football for the Kentucky Wildcats and was selected by the Buffalo Bills in the first round of the 2025 NFL draft with the 30th overall pick.

items.headline: Overcame pre-season injury; made NFL debut in Week 8
items.summary: After suffering an LCL sprain in pre-season practice, Hairston was activated for his NFL debut on October 25, 2025, ahead of Buffalo's Week 8 matchup against the Carolina Panthers.

items.headline: Multi-college transfer; LSU to Arkansas
items.summary: Landon Jackson played college football for LSU and Arkansas. He originally committed to Louisiana State University but played in only five games his freshman year at LSU, seeing action solely on special teams. He later transferred to Arkansas, where he became a prominent defensive end prospect.

items.headline: Third round selection in 2025 NFL draft
items.summary: Jackson was selected by the Buffalo Bills in the third round of the 2025 NFL draft, bringing his defensive end talents to Buffalo's defense.

items.headline: Georgia Bulldogs defensive tackle; drafted by Falcons
items.summary: Zion Logue played college football for the Georgia Bulldogs. During his college career at Georgia, he recorded 11 tackles, 1 TFL and 0.5 sacks in 2021, played in 14 of 15 games in 2022 with 16 total tackles, and in 2023 appeared in 14 games with 17 tackles, 2.5 TFLs and 0.5 sacks. He was selected by Atlanta in the sixth round of the 2024 NFL draft.

items.headline: Career turnaround after joining Bills practice squad
items.summary: On October 1, 2024, Logue was signed by the Buffalo Bills after initially being drafted by and spending time with the Atlanta Falcons, giving him a career-saving opportunity.

items.headline: Alabama Crimson Tide; 2021 College Football Playoff Champion
items.summary: Phidarian Mathis played college football for the Alabama Crimson Tide. During his college career, he had 31 tackles, five tackles for loss and 1.5 sacks with a forced fumble as the Crimson Tide won the 2021 College Football Playoff National Championship. He was selected by the Washington Commanders in the second round of the 2022 NFL draft.

items.headline: Career-saving opportunity with Bills on practice squad
items.summary: On September 10, 2025, Mathis signed with the Buffalo Bills' practice squad in what he described as a career-saving opportunity. He re-signed with the Bills on a one-year contract.

items.headline: Georgia native; four-year high school starter
items.summary: Cole Bishop is from Peachtree City, Georgia, and attended Starr's Mill High School in Fayetteville, Georgia. He was a starter all four years in high school. He played college football for the University of Utah Utes.

items.headline: Second round draft pick in 2024
items.summary: Cole Bishop was selected by the Buffalo Bills with the 60th overall pick in the second round of the 2024 NFL draft, making him an early investment in the Bills' secondary.

items.headline: Two-college transfer; Louisiana to Florida
items.summary: O'Cyrus Torrence played for the Louisiana Ragin' Cajuns from 2019-21, earning first-team All-Sun Belt Conference honors in 2021. He followed coach Billy Napier to Florida, transferring to the Gators for the 2022 season where he started 11 games at guard.

items.headline: Consensus All-American at Florida; second round draft pick
items.summary: Torrence became the first Gators offensive guard to be named a consensus All-American. The Buffalo Bills selected him with the 59th overall selection in the second round of the 2023 NFL Draft.

items.headline: Kentucky Wildcat; strong 2023-2024 season production
items.summary: Deone Walker played college football for the Kentucky Wildcats starting in 2022. He is 6'7" and weighs 331 lbs, born March 11, 2004. During his excellent 2023-2024 season, he totaled 55 tackles, 12.5 tackles for loss, and 7.5 sacks.

items.headline: 2025 NFL draft selection; ESPN All-Rookie team
items.summary: Walker was selected by the Bills in the fourth round of the 2025 NFL draft. ESPN recognized Walker by naming him to the ESPN All-Rookie team for his solid 2025 season as a defensive tackle.

items.headline: Long college career spanning six seasons
items.summary: Desmond Reid began his college career at Western Carolina, where he won SoCon Freshman of the Year with 826 rushing yards. He later made First-team All-Southern Conference with 897 rushing yards and 13 touchdowns before transferring to Pitt, where he played for the Panthers.

items.headline: Breakout 2024 season at Pitt; undrafted free agent signing
items.summary: Reid totaled 1,240 rushing yards, 896 receiving yards and 13 total touchdowns over his Pitt career. His breakout 2024 season included 962 rushing yards, 579 receiving yards and nine total touchdowns, earning second-team All-America recognition. He signed with the Bills as an undrafted free agent following the 2026 NFL Draft.

items.headline: Florida native from Tallahassee; Troy Trojans pass rusher
items.summary: Javon Solomon played college football for the Troy Trojans and attended Amos P. Godby High School in Tallahassee, Florida. He stands 6'1" and weighs 247 lbs, born January 17, 2001 (age 25).

items.headline: Fifth round draft pick in 2024
items.summary: Solomon was selected by the Bills in the fifth round of the 2024 NFL draft. Wearing #56, he has recorded 1.0 sacks in the 2025 season and 3.0 sacks over his NFL career.

items.headline: Louisiana Ragin' Cajuns outside linebacker; long college career
items.summary: Andre Jones Jr. played college football as an outside linebacker for the Louisiana Ragin' Cajuns at the University of Louisiana at Lafayette from 2017 to 2022. He was selected by the Washington Commanders in the seventh round (233rd overall) of the 2023 NFL draft.

items.headline: Transitioned to defensive end with Buffalo
items.summary: After being drafted by Washington, Jones Jr. joined the Buffalo Bills and currently plays defensive end wearing number 75.

items.headline: Appalachian State receiver; six-season college career
items.summary: Jalen Virgil played college football for the Appalachian State Mountaineers, where he was a member for six seasons, redshirting his true freshman year. He had 98 receptions for 1,436 yards and 11 touchdowns in five playing seasons. He stands 6'1" and weighs 210 lbs.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent who made active roster
items.summary: Virgil went undrafted in 2022 and was signed to the Bills' practice squad on August 28, 2024. He was promoted to the active roster on November 8, 2024, and signed a reserve/futures contract on February 10, 2026.

items.headline: Penn State Nittany Lion; early enrollee success
items.summary: Connor McGovern played college football for the Penn State Nittany Lions. He enrolled a semester early and earned a starting job at guard one month into his true freshman season, helping Penn State win the 2016 Big Ten title and reach the Rose Bowl.

items.headline: Named starting center in 2024; four-year extension in 2026
items.summary: McGovern was selected by Dallas in the third round of the 2019 draft. He signed a three-year contract with Buffalo on March 16, 2023, and was named the starting center in 2024 after Mitch Morse was released. The Bills re-signed him to a four-year deal in March 2026.

items.headline: Nevada Wolf Pack four-year starter
items.summary: Austin Corbett played college football for the Nevada Wolf Pack and was a four-year starter from 2014-17. He stands 6'4" and weighs 305 lbs.

items.headline: Super Bowl LVI champion; joined Bills in 2026
items.summary: Corbett was selected by Cleveland in the second round (33rd overall) of the 2018 NFL draft. He played in Super Bowl LVI with the Los Angeles Rams, winning 23-20 against Cincinnati. He signed with Buffalo on a one-year contract on March 26, 2026.

items.headline: Multi-college transfer; North Carolina to Virginia Tech
items.summary: Stephen Gosnell played college football for the North Carolina Tar Heels from 2020 to 2021 and the Virginia Tech Hokies from 2022 to 2024. At Virginia Tech, he caught 28 passes for 506 yards, both career-highs, and one touchdown in his final college season in 2024.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent; became team favorite in training camp
items.summary: After not being selected in the 2025 NFL draft, Gosnell signed with the Buffalo Bills as an undrafted free agent and quickly became a team favorite in training camp, earning the nickname from coaches and teammates.

items.headline: Won Johnny Unitas Golden Arm Award in 2021 at Pitt
items.summary: Kenny Pickett won the Johnny Unitas Golden Arm Award in 2021 while playing college football for the University of Pittsburgh Panthers.

items.headline: Won Super Bowl LIX with Philadelphia Eagles
items.summary: Pickett was a member of the Philadelphia Eagles team that won Super Bowl LIX, providing backup quarterback depth for the champions.

items.headline: Led Pittsburgh Steelers to 2023 playoff appearance as primary starter
items.summary: Pickett spent two seasons as the Pittsburgh Steelers' primary starter, helping lead the team to a playoff appearance in 2023.

items.headline: Utah Utes linebacker with only Power Five offer
items.summary: Devin Lloyd was a three-star recruit who chose Utah over other colleges with just one Power Five offer, showing determination and resilience.

items.headline: Selected in first round of 2022 NFL Draft by Jacksonville Jaguars
items.summary: Lloyd was drafted in the first round of the 2022 NFL Draft by the Jacksonville Jaguars, validating his development at Utah.

items.headline: Returned interception for touchdown in college career
items.summary: During his college career at Utah, Lloyd returned an interception for a touchdown, showcasing his defensive versatility.

items.headline: First-round 2022 pick as top offensive tackle prospect
items.summary: Ikem Ekwonu was drafted in the first round of the 2022 NFL Draft by the Carolina Panthers as a high-priority offensive tackle.

items.headline: Elite offensive tackle protecting Panthers QBs
items.summary: Taylor Moton has been a cornerstone of the Panthers offensive line for multiple seasons.

items.summary: Xavier Legette was drafted by the Panthers from South Carolina in the 2024 NFL Draft.

items.summary: Evan McPherson was the placekicker for the University of Florida Gators.

items.summary: Ryan Rehkow is an Australian punter in the NFL.

items.headline: Long snapper in NFL
items.summary: William Wagner is a long snapper on an NFL roster.

items.headline: Son of Pro Football Hall of Famer Deion Sanders
items.summary: Shedeur Sanders was born February 7, 2002, to former NFL player Deion Sanders and Pilar Sanders. His father is a Pro and College Football Hall of Famer who won 2 Super Bowls, made 8 Pro Bowls, and won the Jim Thorpe Award. Deion coached Shedeur at both Jackson State and Colorado.

items.headline: 2026 Pro Bowl selection as rookie QB
items.summary: Shedeur Sanders was added to the 2026 Pro Bowl roster as a replacement, making him the first Cleveland Browns quarterback to be selected to the Pro Bowl since Derek Anderson in 2008. He finished 2025 with a 3-4 record in 7 starts.

items.headline: Brother of QB Shedeur Sanders
items.summary: Raheim Sanders is a running back for the Cleveland Browns and is the brother of QB Shedeur Sanders. Born June 8, 2002, he played college football for Arkansas and South Carolina. The Browns claimed him from the LA Chargers on August 27, 2025.

items.headline: First NFL QB to make debut outside the United States
items.summary: Dillon Gabriel made his first career start on October 5, 2025, in a game against the Minnesota Vikings held in London, England, making him the first quarterback to have his initial NFL start outside the United States. Born December 28, 2000, in Mililani, Hawaii.

items.headline: One of few left-handed quarterbacks in the NFL
items.summary: Gabriel is one of the few left-handed quarterbacks currently active in the NFL. He tied the FBS record for passing touchdowns with 155, matching the mark previously set by Case Keenum.

items.headline: Two-time Pro Bowl selection with Green Bay
items.summary: Elgton Jenkins signed with the Cleveland Browns on a two-year, $24 million deal with $20 million guaranteed in March 2026. He was a two-time Pro Bowler (2020, 2022) with the Green Bay Packers after 7 seasons. Moved from guard to center in 2025 season.

items.headline: Brother Elgton Jenkins joined him on Browns in 2026
items.summary: Teven Jenkins plays guard for the Cleveland Browns. His brother Elgton Jenkins, who previously played for Green Bay, signed with the Browns as center on a $24 million deal in March 2026, reuniting the brothers on the same team.

items.headline: Son of former NFL player Cedric Tillman Sr.
items.summary: Cedric Jashon Tillman was born April 19, 2000. His father, also named Cedric Tillman, played in the NFL for the Denver Broncos and Jacksonville Jaguars. His older brother Jamir played wide receiver at Navy (2013-16).

items.headline: Suffered fractured fibula and dislocated ankle in Week 16
items.summary: In Cleveland's Week 16 loss to the Buffalo Bills on December 21, Judkins suffered a fractured fibula and dislocated ankle, ending his season. Browns coach Todd Monken stated he should be full go for training camp.

items.headline: Two-year contract with Cleveland worth $20 million
items.summary: Maliek Collins signed a two-year, $20 million contract with the Cleveland Browns on March 13, 2025, with $13 million guaranteed. He spent 2024 with the San Francisco 49ers before joining the Browns in free agency.

items.headline: Brother of former NFL player Romeo Okwara
items.summary: Julian Okwara was born in London, England to Nigerian parents. His brother Romeo Okwara played in the NFL for the New York Giants (2016-17) and Detroit Lions (2018-23). The brothers were teammates on the Lions from 2020-2023.

items.headline: Three-year, $36 million contract extension
items.summary: Grant Delpit signed a three-year contract extension worth $36 million with $23.6 million guaranteed on December 10, 2023. Extension signed one day after he suffered a groin injury in a win over Jacksonville. In 2023, had 80 tackles, 1.5 sacks, interception, and 3 passes defended through 13 games.

items.headline: Placed on reserve/PUP list due to neck injury
items.summary: On May 12, 2025, the Browns placed Jeremiah Owusu-Koramoah on the reserve/PUP list due to a neck injury suffered in Week 8 of the 2024 season, making him ineligible to play for the entirety of the 2025 NFL season.

items.headline: Three-year, $39 million contract extension
items.summary: Owusu-Koramoah signed a three-year, $39 million deal with $25 million guaranteed on August 14, 2024, making him the NFL's sixth-highest paid linebacker at $13 million per year. Originally drafted 52nd overall in 2021 by the Browns.

items.headline: Signed with Browns after 5 seasons with Ravens
items.summary: Tylan Wallace was originally drafted by the Baltimore Ravens in the fourth round (131st overall) of the 2021 NFL Draft. After five seasons in Baltimore, he signed with the Cleveland Browns in 2026. He had 11 catches on 12 targets in 2024, his most productive season.

items.headline: Traded from Texans for three-year, $63 million extension
items.summary: The Browns acquired Tytus Howard from Houston in exchange for a fifth-round draft pick and signed him to a three-year, $63 million extension. He spent his entire 7-year career with the Texans, starting 93 games. Can play right tackle or guard positions.

items.headline: Three-year, $49.5 million contract with Browns
items.summary: Zion Johnson signed a three-year, $49.5 million deal with $32.4 million guaranteed with the Browns in March 2026. Johnson was originally drafted 17th overall by the Los Angeles Chargers in the first round of 2022. Played 100% of Chargers snaps in 2025 except Week 18.

items.headline: Fifth overall pick in 2025 NFL Draft from Michigan
items.summary: Mason Graham was selected by the Cleveland Browns with the 5th overall pick in the 2025 NFL Draft. A two-time All-American from Michigan (including unanimous All-American in 2024), he led Michigan DL with 45 tackles including 7.0 for loss with 3.5 sacks in 2024.

items.headline: Domestic violence arrest and charges in 2024
items.summary: Browns rookie DT Mike Hall Jr. was arrested August 13 on a first-degree misdemeanor domestic violence charge. He was accused of threatening his fiancée with a gun during a fight. His fiancée later recanted the most serious accusation, claiming she was mistaken about the gun threat.

items.headline: Suspended 5 games for violating personal conduct policy
items.summary: Mike Hall Jr. pleaded no contest in September to a reduced charge of disorderly conduct, received a 30-day suspended jail sentence, $250 fine, and two years probation. He was suspended 5 games without pay for violating the NFL's personal conduct policy.

items.headline: Two-year, $17 million contract with Browns
items.summary: Quincy Williams signed a two-year, $17 million contract with the Cleveland Browns on March 11, 2026, with $9 million guaranteed and an $4.64 million signing bonus. He reunites with Browns defensive coordinator Mike Rutenberg, who was his linebackers coach with the Jets for four seasons.

items.headline: Re-signed on one-year, $2 million deal in March 2026
items.summary: On March 12, 2026, Bojorquez re-signed with the Browns on a one-year, $2 million contract. He had previously signed a two-year extension on March 15, 2024. Finished 2024 with 89 punts for 4,387 yards (career high) and made an 84-yard punt in Week 4 vs. Las Vegas Raiders.

items.headline: Signed from Houston Texans in April 2026
items.summary: The Browns officially signed veteran cornerback Myles Bryant to an undisclosed contract in April 2026. Bryant is a six-year veteran with 77 career games played: 4 seasons with New England Patriots (2020-23) and 2 seasons with Houston Texans (2024-25). Career totals: 173 tackles, 2.0 sacks, 4 interceptions, 17 passes defensed.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent from Tulane
items.summary: The Cleveland Browns signed undrafted edge rusher Adin Huntington to a rookie free agent contract. At 6-foot and 260 pounds, he is considered too short for the position. College career: Kent State (2020-22), Louisiana-Monroe (2023), and Tulane (2024). Had 63 tackles, 16 TFL, and 8.5 sacks at Louisiana-Monroe in 2023.

items.headline: Waived by Jets, joined Browns practice squad then active roster
items.summary: Malachi Corley was drafted by the New York Jets in the third round (65th overall) of the 2024 NFL Draft from Western Kentucky. Waived by Jets on August 26, 2025, he signed with Browns practice squad on August 27, 2025, and was elevated to active roster on September 30, 2025.

items.headline: Missed entire 2024 season with ankle injury
items.summary: Luke Wypler, drafted 190th overall in the sixth round of the 2023 NFL Draft from Ohio State, missed the entire 2024 season with an ankle injury. In early 2024 preseason, his ankle was rolled up on. He was a two-year starter at center with 26 consecutive starts in 2021-2022.

items.headline: One-year, $3 million deal with Browns in March 2026
items.summary: Kalia Davis signed a one-year deal with the Cleveland Browns in March 2026. Originally drafted by San Francisco in the sixth round (220th overall) of the 2022 NFL Draft, he spent three seasons with the 49ers before joining Cleveland. Played college football for UCF Knights.

items.headline: Won 2018 Lou Groza Award at Syracuse
items.summary: Andre Szmyt signed a reserve/futures contract with the Browns on January 6, 2025. He won the 2018 Lou Groza Award as the nation's top kicker at Syracuse. Made 805 of 105 field goals with 54-yard career long at Syracuse over five seasons.

items.headline: Right of first refusal tender worth $3.52 million
items.summary: Ronnie Hickman, a third-year safety, started all 17 games in 2025. Browns retained him with a right-of-first-refusal tender worth approximately $3.52 million. Original deal was a three-year, $2.715 million contract as an undrafted free agent in 2023.

items.headline: PFWA All-Rookie Team selection in 2023
items.summary: Dawand Jones, an offensive tackle from Ohio State (not Michigan), was selected by the Cleveland Browns in the fourth round, 111th overall, of the 2023 NFL Draft. He was named to the PFWA All-Rookie Team in his first season with the Browns.

items.headline: Drafted in fifth round of 2024 NFL Draft by Jets
items.summary: Jamari Thrash was drafted by the New York Jets in the fifth round (156th overall) of the 2024 NFL Draft from Louisville Cardinals and Georgia State Panthers. Made NFL debut October 20, 2024, vs Cincinnati. Appeared in 9 games in 2024 with 3 receptions for 22 yards.

items.headline: 2024 SEC Offensive Player of the Year from Tennessee
items.summary: Dylan Sampson was selected by the Browns in the fourth round (126th overall) of the 2025 NFL Draft from Tennessee. Named 2024 SEC Offensive Player of the Year after rushing for 22 touchdowns with 1,400 yards. College: 2,492 rushing yards, 35 TDs in 3 years at Tennessee.

items.headline: One-year free agent deal after five seasons in NFL
items.summary: Jack Stoll, an undrafted free agent from Nebraska (2021), played five seasons with Philadelphia Eagles (2021-24), Miami Dolphins, and New Orleans Saints. 2025 with Saints: 6 receptions for 46 yards, 1 TD in 15 games. Signed one-year deal with Browns in 2026.

items.headline: Two-year contract worth $1.845 million
items.summary: Christopher Edmonds has a 2-year contract with the Cleveland Browns for $1,845,000 with $0 guaranteed. He signed as undrafted free agent after going undrafted in 2024. Signed to Browns active roster on January 3, 2025.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent from Pittsburgh Panthers
items.summary: Donovan McMillon signed with the Cleveland Browns as an undrafted free agent on May 9, 2025. Played for Pittsburgh Panthers and Florida Gators. Led Panthers with 115 tackles in 2024, first Pitt player with consecutive 100-tackle seasons since 2008. Made 53-player active roster after training camp.

items.headline: Signed from Jets practice squad to Browns active roster
items.summary: Brenden Bates, an undrafted free agent from Kentucky, signed with Chicago Bears on May 9, 2024. Waived by Bears on August 27, claimed by Jets on August 28, 2024. On December 18, 2024, signed off Jets practice squad to Browns active roster.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent from Alabama and Texas
items.summary: Isaiah Bond was signed by Cleveland Browns as an undrafted free agent on August 18, 2025. Played for Alabama Crimson Tide (2022-23) and Texas Longhorns (2024). College: 99 receptions for 1,428 yards and 10 touchdowns. 2025 NFL: 18 receptions for 338 yards.

items.headline: Signed with Browns as free agent after Titans stint
items.summary: Tre Avery signed with Tennessee Titans as an undrafted free agent on May 13, 2022. Spent 3 seasons with Titans (2022-24) in 38 games with 5 starts. Signed with Browns practice squad September 16, 2025, and active roster October 14, 2025. Re-signed by Browns in 2026 free agency.

items.headline: Re-signed by Browns in March 2026 free agency
items.summary: D'Angelo Ross was signed off Texans practice squad by Browns on November 19, 2025. Appeared in final 7 games of 2025 season with 123 snaps. Originally signed by New England Patriots as undrafted free agent in 2019. Spent 3 seasons with Texans (2023-25) before Browns signing.

items.headline: UFL All-honors standout before signing with Browns
items.summary: Salvatore Cannella played college football for Auburn Tigers. Signed with Browns on June 19, 2025. After 16 weeks on practice squad, elevated to active roster for Week 17 vs Pittsburgh Steelers. Made debut: 2 receptions for 11 yards. Previously: Arlington Renegades (UFL) starter, 44 receptions for 474 yards, 1 TD.

items.headline: Two-year, $3 million contract with Browns
items.summary: Daniel Thomas signed a two-year, $3 million contract with the Cleveland Browns on March 20, 2026. Played college football for Auburn Tigers. Originally drafted 5th round by Jacksonville in 2020, appeared in 83 games with Jaguars (2020-24) and Lions (2025).

items.headline: Undrafted from Ole Miss, signed to Browns practice squad
items.summary: Caden Prieskorn went undrafted in 2025, initially signed by Detroit Lions as undrafted free agent. Signed to Browns practice squad on October 27, 2025. Played for Ole Miss Rebels and Memphis Tigers. 6-5, 255 lbs, in rookie season.

items.headline: Sixth round pick in 2026 NFL Draft from Arkansas
items.summary: Taylen Green was drafted by Cleveland Browns in the 6th round (182nd overall) of the 2026 NFL Draft from Arkansas Razorbacks. Transferred to Arkansas from Boise State in December 2023. 2025 stats: 2,714 passing yards, 19 TDs, 11 INTs; 777 rushing yards, 8 rushing TDs.

items.headline: Set QB vertical and broad jump records at 2026 NFL Combine
items.summary: At 6-foot-5, 227 pounds, Green posted a 4.36-second 40-yard dash at the 2026 NFL Combine. He set the record for highest vertical jump (43.5 inches) and longest broad jump (11 feet, 2 inches) by a quarterback at the event.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent from Appalachian State
items.summary: Ahmani Marshall was originally signed by Cleveland Browns as undrafted free agent on May 9, 2025. From Appalachian State: 36 games, 14 starts, 1,167 rushing yards, 13 TDs. Appeared in preseason: 24 rushes for 88 yards, 1 TD. Waived August 26, signed practice squad August 27, released October 27.

items.headline: Signed from Bills practice squad on December 24, 2025
items.summary: Kendrick Green, fifth-year center from Illinois, signed by Browns off Bills practice squad on December 24, 2025. Originally drafted 3rd round by Pittsburgh Steelers (2021), moved to guard in second season. Career: Steelers, Texans (traded 2023), Bills (signed March 2025), Browns.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent from Weber State
items.summary: Winston Reid signed with Cleveland Browns as undrafted free agent on May 10, 2024. From Weber State, 6-foot, 225 lbs, born August 3, 1999. 2024 stats: 16 games (3 starts), 15 tackles, 0.5 sacks, 2 passes defensed, 1 fumble recovery, 8 special teams tackles.

items.headline: 2025 Defensive Rookie of the Year
items.summary: Carson Schwesinger won Defensive Rookie of the Year for the 2025 NFL season. Selected 33rd overall in the 2nd round of 2025 NFL Draft from UCLA. Started 16 games as rookie with 156 tackles (6th in league), 2.5 sacks, 3 passes defensed, 2 interceptions.

items.headline: Placed on season-ending injured reserve with quad injury
items.summary: On January 3, 2026, Carson Schwesinger was placed on season-ending injured reserve due to a quad injury.

items.headline: Two-year, $1.755 million contract with Browns
items.summary: Rex Sunahara signed a 2-year, $1,755,000 contract with the Cleveland Browns on August 8, 2024, with average annual salary of $877,500. Appeared in 12 games in 2024 and posted 2 special teams tackles.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent from LSU and Alabama
items.summary: Aaron Anderson signed UDFA deal with Cleveland Browns after 2026 NFL Draft. Played for Alabama Crimson Tide and LSU Tigers. 5-foot-8, 198 pounds. College: 41 games, 99 receptions for 1,428 yards, 10 TDs. Strong prospect: 61 catches for 884 yards at LSU in sophomore season.

items.headline: Pittsburgh multi-sport athlete
items.summary: Abanikanda was a versatile athlete at Pittsburgh who competed in both football and track & field before focusing on football.

items.summary: Howell had an excellent 2021 season at North Carolina, leading the Tar Heels to a 13-1 record and establishing himself as an elite college QB prospect.

items.summary: Dallen Bentley played college football at the University of Wyoming.

items.summary: Pat Bryant attended Clemson University and played college football.

items.summary: Kage Casey played college football at North Carolina State University.

items.headline: Offensive lineman from Southeastern Louisiana

items.summary: Frank Crum played college football for the University of Alabama Crimson Tide.

items.summary: J.K. Dobbins is a running back who played college football at Ohio State University.
