items.summary: Sam Ehlinger was a standout quarterback for the Texas Longhorns.

items.headline: Center from University of Georgia
items.summary: Alex Forsyth played college football at the University of Georgia.

items.headline: Wide receiver from University of Oregon
items.summary: Troy Franklin played college football for the University of Oregon Ducks.

items.headline: Guard from Stanford
items.summary: Nick Gargiulo played college football at Stanford University.

items.headline: Running back from UNLV

items.headline: Ohio State standout, 2023 #2 overall pick for Texans
items.summary: C.J. Stroud was drafted 2nd overall by the Houston Texans in 2023 after a stellar college career at Ohio State. He won the Heisman Trophy runner-up in 2022 and led the Buckeyes to the College Football Playoff.

items.headline: Former Chicago Bears RB, battled through serious injuries
items.summary: David Montgomery was drafted by the Chicago Bears and became one of their offensive cornerstones. He has dealt with multiple injuries including ACL tears but has shown resilience in his NFL career.

items.headline: Houston Texans 2023 draft pick from Houston area
items.summary: Tank Dell was drafted by the Houston Texans in 2023 and represents a local success story, coming from the Houston area to play for his hometown NFL team.

items.headline: Texas A&M defensive star, 2022 Texans 1st rounder
items.summary: Jalen Pitre was a standout safety for Texas A&M and was selected in the first round (12th overall) by the Houston Texans in 2022. Known for his aggressive defensive play and leadership on the field.

items.headline: Elite guard, multiple All-Pro selections
items.summary: Wyatt Teller has been one of the NFL's premier guards, earning All-Pro honors in multiple seasons. He transitioned from the Cleveland Browns to the Houston Texans and remains one of the league's top interior offensive linemen.

items.headline: Texas Tech center, 2023 UDFA addition to Texans
items.summary: Jake Andrews went undrafted in 2023 and signed with the Houston Texans as an undrafted free agent. He was a four-year starter at Texas Tech where he anchored the offensive line.

items.headline: Pro Bowl offensive tackle, multiple Super Bowl appearances
items.summary: Trent Brown is an elite left tackle who has made the Pro Bowl and played in Super Bowls with the New England Patriots and Las Vegas Raiders before joining the Houston Texans.

items.headline: Former first-round pick, career journey with multiple teams
items.summary: Josh Kelly was drafted in the first round (18th overall) by the Los Angeles Chargers in 2019. After stops with multiple NFL teams, he joined the Houston Texans as a wide receiver.

items.headline: LSU tight end, drafted by Las Vegas Raiders
items.summary: Foster Moreau was drafted by the Las Vegas Raiders in 2020 out of LSU. He has developed into a reliable tight end in the NFL and joined the Houston Texans to continue his career.

items.headline: Louisville defensive tackle, 1st rounder by Saints
items.summary: Sheldon Rankins was drafted in the first round (12th overall) by the New Orleans Saints in 2016 from the University of Louisville. He has been a consistent interior defensive lineman throughout his NFL career.

items.headline: Son of former NFL RB Michael Jordan (not the basketball player)
items.summary: Brevin Jordan is a tight end who carries on his family's football legacy. His father played running back in the NFL, continuing the Jordan football family tradition.

items.headline: Wisconsin Badgers star QB, third-round pick 2023
items.summary: Graham Mertz was a standout quarterback at the University of Wisconsin and was drafted by the Houston Texans in the third round of the 2023 NFL Draft. He serves as a backup/developmental QB for the franchise.

items.headline: Stanford QB, 3rd round 2021 pick, competed for starting role
items.summary: Davis Mills was drafted by the Houston Texans in the third round (67th overall) of the 2021 NFL Draft from Stanford. He has been in competition for the starting quarterback position during his tenure with the team.

items.headline: Son Anthony Jr. born March 17, 2024
items.summary: Richardson became a father when his fiancee Jada gave birth to their son Anthony Richardson Jr. in March 2024, less than a month after they got engaged in February. Richardson was present for the birth and spent time with his newborn in the delivery room.

items.headline: Engaged to fiancee Jada Richardson in February 2024
items.summary: Richardson proposed to his high school sweetheart Jada in February 2024. Jada attended Florida A&M where she majored in nursing and currently runs a doula service for expecting mothers. The couple has been together since high school.

items.headline: Mother LaShawnda Cleare overcame hardship to raise him
items.summary: Richardson's mother LaShawnda Cleare worked multiple jobs with little sleep to provide for her family. She struggled financially to pay for clothes, food, rent and bills. After Richardson was drafted, he immediately bought his mother a new house as his first major purchase.

items.headline: Uncle Tanka Lane taught him football and served as father figure
items.summary: Richardson's uncle Tanka Lane was a crucial male role model who taught him football at a young age. Lane died when Richardson was 11 years old, which motivated him to ensure nobody could stop him when he was at his best. Richardson described his uncle as a 'comedian-type' who was serious about football.

items.headline: Younger brother Corey Carter idolizes him as role model
items.summary: Richardson has one brother, Corey Carter, who is eight years younger. Carter credits Richardson as his father figure and biggest role model. Carter appeared at the 2023 NFL Draft at age 13 to support his brother and deliver a message of support.

items.headline: Married to Olympic gymnast Simone Biles
items.summary: Owens is married to Simone Biles, one of the most decorated gymnasts in Olympic history. The couple married in 2023. Biles is a 7-time Olympic medalist and returned to gymnastics for the 2024 Olympic Games.

items.headline: Youngest of 39 cousins in competitive sports family
items.summary: Nelson was raised in an extremely competitive family environment. His father Craig was the youngest of six siblings, and his mother Maryellen was also the youngest of six. The extended family had 39 nieces and nephews total, with Nelson being the youngest of all of them. The family regularly held intense 3-on-3 basketball games.

items.headline: Mother broke wrist during competitive family basketball game
items.summary: During a heated 3-on-3 basketball game between the Nelson family members, Nelson's mother took a charging foul and broke her wrist. His father recalled they were bickering about whether her feet were moving while heading to the hospital.

items.headline: Played Pop Warner two grades above his age
items.summary: Nelson joined his older brother Connor's Pop Warner team and played two grades above his age. He competed in offensive line and linebacker roles while his father was the coach. The league required him to lose 20 pounds each year as he aged up.

items.headline: Older brother Connor is now a software engineer
items.summary: Nelson's six-year-old brother Connor became a college football player but is now a software engineer. In his free time, Connor edits highlight reels of Quenton's games and has been impressed by his pancake block numbers.

items.headline: Austrian-born, from Steinbrunn
items.summary: Raimann was born September 23, 1997 in Steinbrunn, Austria. He began playing American football at age 14 for the Vienna Vikings youth team. He attended Ballsportgymnasium Wien, an elite school for competitive athletes.

items.headline: Completed Austrian military service before college
items.summary: After graduating from Ballsportgymnasium Wien, Raimann completed Austria's compulsory military service before committing to play college football at Central Michigan. He was an exchange student at Delton-Kellogg High School in Michigan.

items.headline: Switched from tight end to offensive tackle at Central Michigan
items.summary: Raimann played tight end during his first two seasons at Central Michigan (2018-2019) before moving to offensive tackle after his sophomore year. He caught 20 passes for 164 yards total in his first two seasons.

items.headline: Signed four-year, $100 million contract extension July 28, 2025
items.summary: Raimann signed a four-year contract extension worth $100 million with the Indianapolis Colts on July 28, 2025, ensuring he remains a cornerstone of the offensive line.

items.headline: Married high school sweetheart Molly Walding in March 2026
items.summary: Leonard proposed to his high school sweetheart Molly Walding on July 10, 2025. The couple married on March 28, 2026 in Fairhope, Alabama, their hometown.

items.headline: Identifies as Christian
items.summary: Leonard is a practicing Christian, which has influenced his personal values and approach to life.

items.headline: Two-sport athlete in high school, played football and basketball
items.summary: In high school at Fairhope, Alabama, Leonard played both football and basketball. As a senior, he passed for 1,900 yards and 25 touchdowns with 500 rushing yards. He received college basketball offers from Saint Mary's, North Alabama, Samford and Missouri State.

items.headline: Transferred from Duke to Notre Dame in December 2023
items.summary: Leonard announced he was entering the transfer portal on November 29, 2023, then committed to Notre Dame on December 12, 2023 after starting his career at Duke.

items.headline: Made NFL debut in Week 8 2025 against Tennessee Titans
items.summary: Leonard made his NFL debut in Week 8 of the 2025 season against the Tennessee Titans as an injury replacement, completing neither of his two passes and rushing for one yard.

items.headline: Father was college football player at Oklahoma State
items.summary: Treadwell's father played college football, providing him with mentorship and athletic guidance from a young age. His father's experience in football helped shape Laquon's path to the NFL.

items.headline: Spent time incarcerated before pursuing football professionally
items.summary: Alie-Cox spent time in prison before being signed by the Indianapolis Colts. His football career represents a redemption story from his troubled past.

items.headline: Both parents were athletes at Northwestern University
items.summary: Pierce's father Greg was a tight end who played collegiately at Northwestern. His mother also played volleyball at Northwestern. The Pierce family has a strong athletic tradition.

items.headline: Two brothers also pursuing athletic careers
items.summary: Pierce's younger brother Caden plays college basketball for Purdue. Another brother Justin played college basketball at William & Mary and North Carolina and currently plays professionally in Europe.

items.headline: Reached 1,000 receiving yards in 2025 with fewest receptions since 2010
items.summary: Pierce had 47 receptions for 1,003 yards in the 2025 season, making his performance notably efficient. His 47 receptions were the fewest to clear 1,000 yards since DeSean Jackson did so in 2010.

items.headline: Father Gary Downs played college football at NC State and is now an assistant coach
items.summary: Josh's father Gary played college football for NC State and has become an assistant coach. His uncle Dré Bly played for North Carolina and had an 11-year NFL career.

items.headline: Younger brother Caleb Downs drafted by Dallas Cowboys
items.summary: Josh's younger brother Caleb is a safety who played his freshman season at Alabama before transferring to Ohio State. Caleb was later drafted by the Dallas Cowboys.

items.headline: Broke multiple University of North Carolina receiving records
items.summary: Downs holds North Carolina records for career receiving yards (2,483), career receptions (202 at 3rd most), and had record-breaking seasons with 101 catches for 1,335 yards in 2021.

items.headline: Grandfather Bill Mallory was legendary college football head coach
items.summary: Will's grandfather Bill Mallory served as head coach at Miami of Ohio, Colorado, Northern Illinois, and Indiana. His father Mike Mallory is an assistant special teams coach for the Denver Broncos.

items.headline: Two uncles are also football coaches
items.summary: Will's uncles Curt Mallory and Doug Mallory are both football coaches, continuing the family coaching tradition.

items.headline: Father Dave Henigan was head coach at Billy Ryan High School
items.summary: Seth's father Dave served as head coach at Denton Ryan High School in Texas. Seth led the school to a 39-2 record from 2018 to 2020 and threw for 7,234 yards with 79 touchdowns and only 14 interceptions.

items.headline: Extended 41-game touchdown pass streak tied for second-longest in FBS history
items.summary: In 2024, Henigan extended his streak of throwing a touchdown pass in every game to 41 consecutive games, tying him with Heisman Trophy winner Marcus Mariota for the second-longest streak in FBS history.

items.headline: Memphis all-time touchdown leader with 104 career touchdowns
items.summary: Henigan surpassed Brady White's previous Memphis record of 90 touchdown passes to become the program's all-time leader. He finished his college career with 104 passing touchdowns and 31 interceptions.

items.headline: Played college football at Albany State Golden Rams
items.summary: Stewart played college football for the Albany State Golden Rams from 2013 to 2016. He was selected by the Indianapolis Colts in the fourth round, 144th overall, in the 2017 NFL draft.

items.headline: Suspended six games in 2023 for violating NFL performance-enhancing drug policy
items.summary: On October 17, 2023, Stewart was suspended six games for violating the NFL policy on performance-enhancing drugs. He finished the 2023 season with 41 tackles and 0.5 sacks through 11 starts.

items.headline: Received Black College Football Pro Player of the Year Award in 2021
items.summary: Stewart received the Black College Football Pro Player of the Year Award in 2021, recognizing his professional achievements and representation of Black college football.

items.headline: Two-time first-team All-SEC selection at LSU
items.summary: Key was a standout defensive end at LSU from 2015-2017. As a sophomore in 2016, he set a single-season school record with 12 sacks. He earned first-team All-Southeastern Conference honors from the Associated Press in both 2016 and 2017.

items.headline: Successfully appealed and overturned 6-game PED suspension in 2024
items.summary: On July 30, 2024, Key was suspended 6 games for violating the NFL's policy against performance-enhancing drugs while with the Tennessee Titans. However, he successfully appealed the ban, and his suspension was overturned on August 17, 2024.

items.headline: Signed two-year, $20 million contract with Indianapolis Colts
items.summary: On March 12, 2026, Key signed with the Indianapolis Colts on a two-year, $20 million contract with $11 million guaranteed, adding depth to their defensive end rotation.

items.headline: Helped lead Ohio State to College Football Playoff National Championship in 2024
items.summary: Tuimoloau played a key role in Ohio State's dominant 2024 season, helping the Buckeyes capture the College Football Playoff National Championship. He was a consistent three-time first-team All-Big Ten selection from 2022-2024.

items.headline: Recorded two interceptions against Penn State in 2022, first FBS player to do so since 2000
items.summary: Against Penn State in 2022, Tuimoloau had an historic performance with two interceptions, two sacks, a forced fumble, and a fumble recovery plus touchdown, becoming the first FBS player to accomplish that since 2000.

items.headline: Selected 45th overall by Indianapolis Colts in 2025 NFL Draft
items.summary: Tuimoloau was selected in the second round with the 45th pick in the 2025 NFL Draft by the Indianapolis Colts. He made his first NFL game action on September 14, 2025 in Week 2 against the Denver Broncos.

items.headline: Named first-team All-Pac-12 cornerback in 2022 season at USC
items.summary: After transferring from Colorado, Blackmon had an outstanding 2022 season at USC with 66 tackles, 2 tackles for loss, 3 interceptions, and 12 pass deflections. He was named first-team All-Pac-12 Conference for his performance.

items.headline: Drafted by Minnesota Vikings in third round, 102nd overall, 2023 NFL Draft
items.summary: Blackmon was drafted by the Minnesota Vikings in the third round of the 2023 NFL Draft. In his rookie season, he recorded his first career interception in Week 10 against the New Orleans Saints.

items.headline: Traded to Indianapolis Colts from Minnesota Vikings in August 2025
items.summary: On August 25, 2025, the Vikings traded Blackmon to the Indianapolis Colts in exchange for a 2026 NFL draft sixth-round pick. He has since become a starter for the Colts.

items.headline: Played college football for Notre Dame Fighting Irish from 2015 to 2018
items.summary: Tillery was originally going to play offensive tackle as a freshman at Notre Dame but was moved to defensive tackle. He started all 13 games in 2017 with 56 tackles and 4.5 sacks. In his senior year 2018, he posted a career-high seven sacks.

items.headline: Selected 28th overall in first round of 2019 NFL Draft by Los Angeles Chargers
items.summary: Tillery was drafted by the Los Angeles Chargers in the first round of the 2019 NFL Draft. As a rookie, he appeared in 15 games with three starts and recorded two sacks and 17 total tackles.

items.headline: Signed one-year contract with Indianapolis Colts on March 17, 2026
items.summary: After playing for the Chargers, Raiders, Vikings, and Chiefs, Tillery signed with the Indianapolis Colts on March 17, 2026 on a one-year, $1.49 million contract.

items.headline: Played college football for Penn State Nittany Lions from 2020 to 2024
items.summary: Warren played tight end for Penn State and had an outstanding final season in 2024 with 104 receptions for 1,233 yards and 8 touchdowns. He was a first-team All-American in 2024 and first-team All-Big Ten.

items.headline: Won John Mackey Award in 2024 as nation's top tight end
items.summary: Warren won the John Mackey Award in 2024, recognizing him as the best tight end in college football. This award is the highest honor for tight ends in college sports.

items.headline: Selected 14th overall in first round of 2025 NFL Draft by Indianapolis Colts
items.summary: Warren was drafted by the Indianapolis Colts with the 14th overall pick in the 2025 NFL Draft. He had a strong rookie season with 76 receptions for 817 yards and 4 touchdowns.

items.headline: Named to 2026 Pro Bowl in rookie season
items.summary: Warren was named to the 2026 Pro Bowl as a rookie tight end, becoming the 18th rookie tight end in NFL history to achieve this honor and just the second in Colts history after John Mackey in 1963.

items.headline: Played college football at Arkansas State and Notre Dame
items.summary: Grupe spent four years at Arkansas State (2017-2021) as a kicker before transferring to Notre Dame for his final season in 2022. He was named first-team All-Sun Belt in 2019 and second-team All-Sun Belt in 2021.

items.headline: Signed as undrafted free agent by New Orleans Saints in 2023
items.summary: After going undrafted in the 2023 NFL Draft, Grupe was signed by the New Orleans Saints as an undrafted free agent on May 1, 2023. In his rookie season, he converted all 40 extra point attempts and 30 of 37 field goal attempts.

items.headline: Kicked 60-yard field goal in Week 15 against Seattle Seahawks in 2025
items.summary: Playing for the Indianapolis Colts in Week 15 against the Seattle Seahawks, Grupe kicked a go-ahead 60-yard field goal with 40 seconds left. This is the longest field goal in Colts history, breaking the previous record of 58 yards set by Dan Miller in 1982.

items.headline: Played college football at Malone University
items.summary: Dulin was a four-year starter for the Malone Pioneers and finished his collegiate career with 189 receptions for 3,188 yards and 28 touchdowns. He was named first-team All-G-MAC in both his junior and senior seasons.

items.headline: Signed as undrafted free agent by Indianapolis Colts in 2019
items.summary: Dulin signed with the Indianapolis Colts as an undrafted free agent on April 27, 2019. He made his NFL debut on September 29, 2019 against the Oakland Raiders, becoming the first and only Malone football player to appear in an NFL game.

items.headline: Named second-team All-Pro in 2021 for special teams
items.summary: In 2021, Dulin saw action in all 17 games and finished second in the NFL with 17 special teams tackles. He set career highs in receptions (13), receiving yards (173) and receiving touchdowns (2), and was named Associated Press second-team All-Pro.

items.headline: Played college football for UCLA Bruins from 2022 to 2023
items.summary: After transferring to UCLA following medical retirement from Washington, Latu had a standout two-year career with 23.5 sacks, 34 tackles for loss, 85 tackles, five forced fumbles, two interceptions and five passes defended. He was a consensus All-American in 2023.

items.headline: Selected 15th overall in first round of 2024 NFL Draft by Indianapolis Colts
items.summary: Latu was selected by the Indianapolis Colts with the 15th overall pick in the 2024 NFL Draft as the draft's first defensive player. He was the most skilled pass rusher available in the draft.

items.headline: Recorded 8.5 sacks in 2025 rookie season
items.summary: In his rookie 2025 season, Latu recorded 8.5 sacks, 27 solo tackles, and 3 interceptions. He has been making his move as a pass rusher and is expected to contribute significantly to the Colts' defense.

items.headline: Played college football at SMU and Ole Miss from 2019 to 2024
items.summary: Bentley started his college career at SMU from 2019-2021, where he was named AAC Co-Rookie of the Year and First Team All-AAC in 2020 with 11 rushing touchdowns. He then transferred to Ole Miss from 2022-2024 and finished with 540 rushing yards and four touchdowns in 2023.

items.headline: Signed as undrafted free agent by Indianapolis Colts on May 9, 2025
items.summary: After going undrafted in the 2025 NFL Draft, Bentley signed with the Indianapolis Colts as an undrafted free agent and was later elevated to the active roster. He was eventually placed on the practice squad and signed a reserve/future contract on January 5, 2026.

items.headline: Born December 30, 2000 in Houston, Texas
items.summary: Bentley is a native of Houston, Texas and attended C.E. King High School where he was a three-star recruit who recorded 50 carries for 510 yards in 2018 as a senior.

items.headline: Played college football at Wisconsin from 2020 to 2023
items.summary: Bortolini played for the Wisconsin Badgers, starting at multiple offensive line positions including guard and center. In his final season 2023, he started all 12 games at center and earned third-team All-Big Ten honors with a strong Pro Football Focus grade.

items.headline: Selected 117th overall in fourth round of 2024 NFL Draft by Indianapolis Colts
items.summary: Bortolini was drafted by the Indianapolis Colts in the fourth round of the 2024 NFL Draft. He made his first start in Week 4 against Pittsburgh and had an excited reaction slapping high-fives down the sideline after the team scored a touchdown.

items.headline: Born June 17, 2002 in Kewaunee, Wisconsin
items.summary: Bortolini is from Kewaunee, Wisconsin and attended Kewaunee High School where he was a four-year starter on the offensive line and gained offers from schools including Harvard, Yale, and Iowa.

items.headline: Played college football at Purdue and Oklahoma from 2021 to 2025
items.summary: Burks was Purdue's leading receiver in 2023 with 47 receptions for 629 yards and seven touchdowns. He was named second-team All-Big Ten Conference and later transferred to Oklahoma for the 2024-2025 seasons.

items.headline: Selected 254th overall in seventh round of 2026 NFL Draft by Indianapolis Colts
items.summary: Burks was drafted by the Indianapolis Colts with the 254th overall pick in the seventh round of the 2026 NFL Draft, making him an early prospect for the franchise's wide receiver rotation.

items.headline: Born January 8, 2003 in Belleville, Michigan
items.summary: Burks is from Belleville, Michigan and attended Belleville High School. At 5-10 and 180 pounds, he was one of Michigan's top wide receiver prospects and recorded impressive combine measurements with a 4.30-second 40-yard dash.

items.headline: Played college football at Florida and Kentucky from 2022 to 2025
items.summary: Farmer started at Florida in 2022 as a true freshman but only appeared in two games during 2023 due to an upper body injury. He transferred to Kentucky and started every game at right guard for the Wildcats in both 2024 and 2025.

items.headline: Selected 113th overall in fourth round of 2026 NFL Draft by Indianapolis Colts
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts selected Farmer with the 113th overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft. ESPN analyst Field Yates praised him as one of the best value picks of the entire draft, noting his mean streak and starting-caliber upside.

items.headline: Born July 27, 2004 in Atlanta, Georgia; grew up in Covington
items.summary: Farmer was born in Atlanta and grew up in Covington, Georgia where he attended Eastside High School. He was named Region 8-AAAAA Lineman of the Year and all-state, and was a three-star recruit ranked as one of the top-35 offensive line recruits nationally.

items.headline: Played college football for BYU from 2019 to 2022
items.summary: Freeland was a standout offensive tackle for the BYU Cougars, starting at left tackle and right tackle. In 2022, he was named a Third Team All-American and earned Phil Steele All-Independent First Team honors.

items.headline: Selected 106th overall in fourth round of 2023 NFL Draft by Indianapolis Colts
items.summary: Freeland was drafted by the Indianapolis Colts in the fourth round of the 2023 NFL Draft. He started his first NFL game on October 1, 2023 at left tackle and later transitioned to right tackle, starting nine games in his rookie season.

items.headline: Holds NFL Combine record for offensive lineman with 37-inch vertical jump
items.summary: Freeland set an NFL Combine record for an offensive lineman with a 37-inch vertical jump at the 2023 Combine, showcasing exceptional athleticism for his position at 6-8 and 302 pounds.

items.headline: Played college football at University of Delaware (2015-2018)
items.summary: Adderley played four seasons at Delaware, initially as a cornerback for his first two years before switching to safety in 2017. During his college career he recorded 226 tackles and 10 interceptions, earning First-team All-CAA honors in 2017 and 2018.

items.headline: Selected by Los Angeles Chargers in 2nd round (60th overall) of 2019 NFL Draft
items.summary: Adderley was drafted by the Chargers in 2019 and spent four seasons with the team from 2019-2022. He appeared in 50 games with 44 starts, recording 232 total tackles, 3 interceptions, 12 pass deflections, 2 forced fumbles and 2 fumble recoveries.

items.headline: Announced retirement from NFL in March 2023 at age 25
items.summary: Adderley announced his retirement from football on March 16, 2023, at age 25 after four seasons with the Chargers. He cited a desire to focus on health, family and entrepreneurship.

items.headline: Announced NFL comeback and signed with Colts in 2026
items.summary: After three years away from football, Adderley announced on Instagram on February 9, 2026 that he intended to return to the NFL. On April 14, 2026, the Colts signed him to a veteran minimum contract, making him the third safety Indianapolis brought in during free agency.

items.headline: Played college football at University of Chattanooga (2020-2024)
items.summary: Lowery appeared in 46 games with 29 starts at Chattanooga, compiling 166 tackles, 19.0 tackles for loss, 15 pass breakups, and three interceptions including two pick-sixes. He earned First-team All-SoCon honors in 2024 and Second-team in 2022. He graduated in May 2023 with Latin Honors in Mechanical Engineering and contributed to the UTC Rocket Mocs rocketry team.

items.headline: Signed by Baltimore Ravens as undrafted free agent in 2025
items.summary: Lowery was not selected in the 2025 NFL Draft but signed with the Ravens as an undrafted free agent. He made the Ravens initial 53-man roster and played in three games with one start in Week 5 before being waived on October 11, 2025.

items.headline: Claimed by Colts off waivers from Baltimore on October 13, 2025
items.summary: Lowery was claimed off waivers by the Indianapolis Colts from the Baltimore Ravens on October 13, 2025, giving him another opportunity at the NFL level with his new team.

items.summary: Nikkel was drafted by the Colts in 2026 and has appeared in games for the team during the 2025 season.

items.headline: Son of former Georgia linebacker; football legacy runs deep
items.summary: Davis Allen is the son of former Georgia linebacker John Allen and continues his family's football tradition. His brother played defensive end at Air Force, establishing the Allen family as a football dynasty.

items.headline: Five-time Academic All-Big Ten standout with dual degrees
items.summary: AJ Arcuri earned a bachelor's degree in packaging science in December 2020 and completed a master's degree in the same field the following year. He was one of only five Spartans to earn Academic All-Big Ten honors five times and was a semifinalist for the prestigious William V. Campbell Trophy.

items.headline: Rookie consensus All-American did not allow single sack at TCU
items.summary: Steve Avila was a consensus All-American guard at TCU who did not allow a single sack during his final season. He was named to the PFWA All-Rookie Team after starting all 17 regular season games in his first NFL year.

items.headline: Two-time national championship QB; elite company of consecutive champions
items.summary: Stetson Bennett won consecutive NCAA national championships in 2021 and 2022 with Georgia, joining only A.J. McCarty, Tommie Frazier, Jerry Tagge, and Steve Davis as the only quarterbacks to achieve this feat.

items.headline: Michigan's all-time rushing TD leader; unanimous All-American
items.summary: Blake Corum became Michigan's all-time leader in rushing touchdowns with 58. As a unanimous All-American and 2022 Big Ten MVP, he won a national championship and was named offensive MVP in 2023, setting single-season TD record with 27.

items.headline: Longest-tenured Rams player; growing family with wife Malea
items.summary: With Rob Havenstein's February 2026 retirement, Tyler Higbee is now the longest-tenured player on the Rams' roster after eight years with the team. He is married to Malea and has a daughter Iyla Ty (born October 2022) plus a second child born in 2025.

items.headline: Five-star recruit from Oaks Christian; Stanford record holder
items.summary: Colby Parkinson was a five-star recruit at prestigious Oaks Christian School in Westlake Village, California. At Stanford, he set a school record for tight ends with four TDs in a single game (vs. Oregon State, Nov 10, 2018) and caught 48 passes as a junior before going pro.

items.headline: Super Bowl LVI champion with Rams; three-time All-Big Ten offensive tackle
items.summary: Alaric Jackson, a Canadian-American from Iowa, won Super Bowl LVI with the Rams when they defeated the Cincinnati Bengals 23-20. He started all 42 games for Iowa at left tackle and earned All-Big Ten honors in three consecutive seasons.

items.headline: Career year in 2025; elite run defense grading at 87.9 PFF
items.summary: Kam Curl had a breakout 2025 season with 122 tackles (second-most among DBs), two regular season interceptions plus a crucial playoff pick against Caleb Williams in OT win vs Bears. Re-signed to three-year, $36M deal; PFF's highest-graded safety in run defense.

items.headline: 2024 PFWA All-Rookie DE; versatile defender across five techniques
items.summary: Braden Fiske, drafted 39th overall in 2024, played all 17 games (8 starts) recording 44 tackles, 8.5 sacks, two forced fumbles and two fumble recoveries as a rookie. Named to PFWA All-Rookie Team; plays three-, four-, and five-techniques with pass-rush versatility.

items.headline: FBS record holder: six defensive TDs; slimmest CB since 2000 at combine
items.summary: Emmanuel Forbes Jr. holds the FBS record for most career interceptions returned for a touchdown with six. At the NFL Combine, he was the only cornerback drafted since 2000 to weigh under 170 pounds (at 5'10"). Thriving with Rams after waiver claim from Commanders.

items.headline: Nicknamed 'The Conductor'; plays guitar, organ, clarinet; 2026 commencement speaker
items.summary: Kobie Turner, a two-time team captain, plays multiple instruments including guitar, organ, and clarinet. He sang baritone for Richmond's Schola Cantorum and will serve as the 2026 commencement speaker at University of Richmond, addressing ~840 graduates on May 10, 2026.

items.headline: Zimbabwe native; undrafted gem from Colorado; elite run defense
items.summary: Nate Landman is a Zimbabwe-born inside linebacker who went undrafted out of Colorado. In 2023 with Atlanta, he posted 110 tackles, two sacks, three forced fumbles; now PFF's eighth-graded off-ball LB with 80.5 run grade after signing with Rams.

items.headline: From small-town Cuero, Texas; battled injuries at UT before NFL success
items.summary: Jordan Whittington hails from Cuero, Texas (population ~7,000) and attended Cuero High School. Initially a running back at Texas, he battled injuries before finishing as a receiver with 42 catches for 501 yards. Named 2024 PFWA All-Rookie Team returner after 16 kickoff returns.

items.headline: Fresno State record holder; undrafted journey through four teams
items.summary: Ronnie Rivers finished his Fresno State career with team records in scoring (52 TDs), rushing TDs (40), receiving TDs by RB (12), and receiving yards by RB (1,417). Went undrafted but has spent all years with Rams after free agent signings; special teams contributor.

items.headline: Chiefs reunion with Trent McDuffie; two-time Super Bowl champion
items.summary: Jaylen Watson is a two-time Super Bowl champion who played four seasons with Kansas City, starting 29 of 53 games with 24 passes defensed and three interceptions. Re-joined the Rams on a three-year deal (up to $51M) alongside former Chiefs teammate Trent McDuffie.

items.headline: OSU all-time receptions leader; 100-yard kickoff return TD in Bedlam 2021
items.summary: Brennan Presley is Oklahoma State's all-time receptions leader with 315 catches. In the 2021 Bedlam win, he made a 100-yard kickoff return for a TD, defining the Cowboys' 12-2 season. Signed with Rams as UDFA; earns second-team All-Big 12 honors as returner.

items.headline: 3rd-graded guard in NFL by PFF (86.4 grade); Steelers trade target
items.summary: Kevin Dotson earned 86.4 PFF grade in 2025, 3rd among 81 qualified guards. Acquired from Pittsburgh in 2023 trade (Steelers got 4th, Rams gave 5th + Dotson). Re-signed to three-year, $48M deal; returned from ankle injury for divisional playoff game vs Bears.

items.headline: Clemson defensive starts record; All-ACC four consecutive years
items.summary: Tyler Davis broke Clemson's record for career defensive starts with 52 games. As a senior in 2023, he set the Clemson defensive tackles starts record. Named second-team All-American in 2022 and was Clemson's first four-time All-ACC selection at his position.

items.headline: Shortest DL in NFL at 5'11"; 6'8" wingspan; Texas Big 12 DPOY
items.summary: Poona Ford is the shortest defensive lineman in the NFL at 5-foot-11 5/8 inches but has an impressive 6-foot-8 wingspan (80.25 inches). A Texas Longhorns Big 12 Defensive Lineman of the Year (2017), he signed with Rams on three-year, $27.6M deal in March 2025.

items.headline: FAMU undrafted gem; overcame adversity to reach NFL without offers
items.summary: Xavier Smith (5'9", 155 lbs) from Florida A&M received no college football offers despite creating highlight films and contacting 100+ coaches. After quitting football to work at Amazon, he signed UDFA with Rams and debuted vs 49ers, setting up game-winning FG with 38-yard punt return.

items.headline: John Mackey Award watchlist; blocking-first TE from Newport Beach
items.summary: Mark Redman is a tight end from Newport Beach, California who transferred through Washington, San Diego State, and Louisville. Named to John Mackey Award watchlist twice for best TE in nation. Blocking is his strong suit despite good hands; signed UDFA with Rams in 2025.

items.headline: All-CUSA First Team; led Louisiana Tech in receptions, yards, TDs
items.summary: Tru Edwards (6'3") played three seasons at Louisiana Tech after stints at Hawaii and Navarro College. In his redshirt senior season, led team with 85 receptions for 989 yards and six TDs; named All-CUSA First-Team. Signed UDFA with Rams in April 2025.

items.headline: Auburn RB from Philadelphia, Mississippi (pop. 7,000); self-described country-boy
items.summary: Jarquez Hunter hails from rural Philadelphia, Mississippi (pop. ~7,000). The Auburn Tigers running back was drafted 117th overall in 2025 (traded up 10 spots) after earning his bachelor's in natural resource management. Self-described 'country-boy' excited to start NFL career with Rams.

items.headline: Outside linebacker from Tennessee/Georgia Military; humbling football journey to Hollywood
items.summary: Byron Young played for Georgia Military Bulldogs and Tennessee Volunteers before being drafted 77th overall by Rams in 2023. His humbling football journey brought him to Hollywood. Two-time team captain voted by peers; taken his game 'to next level' both mentally and physically.

items.headline: Defensive tackle for Philadelphia Eagles
items.summary: Byron Young is a defensive tackle on the Philadelphia Eagles defense.

items.headline: Undrafted rookie wide receiver out of South Carolina
items.summary: Jack Bech signed as an undrafted free agent with the Raiders. He played college football at the University of South Carolina and earned attention for his receiving ability despite going undrafted in the 2025 NFL Draft.

items.headline: Willis signs 3-year, $67.5M deal with Dolphins after Tua exit
items.summary: On March 12, 2026, Malik Willis signed a three-year, $67.5 million contract with the Miami Dolphins following the team's decision to part ways with Tua Tagovailoa. Willis was drafted in the third round (86th overall) by Tennessee in 2022.

items.headline: Ewers drafted by Dolphins in 7th round out of Texas
items.summary: Quinn Ewers was selected by the Miami Dolphins in the seventh round (231st overall) of the 2025 NFL draft. Ewers transferred from Ohio State to Texas in December 2021 and started three seasons for the Longhorns under Steve Sarkisian.

items.headline: Wright enters 3rd season with opportunity behind De'Von Achane
items.summary: Jaylen Wright, drafted 4th round in 2024 from Tennessee, finished 2025 with 288 rushing yards (4.1 avg) and 2 TDs across 10 games. Born 4/1/2003 at 5'10", 208 lbs, he faces competition from Ollie Gordon II but has a shot at complementary role in 2026.

items.headline: Marshall Jr. signs futures contract with Dolphins as lottery ticket
items.summary: Former second-round pick (2021) by Panthers from LSU, Terrace Marshall Jr. (6-2, 200 lbs) signed a futures contract with Miami on February 4, 2026. Big-bodied receiver who can win contested catches, Marshall has bounced through multiple teams including 49ers, Raiders, and Eagles.

items.headline: Dulcich re-signs with Dolphins after strong late-season run
items.summary: Greg Dulcich (born 3/26/2000), third-round pick (2022) by Denver from UCLA, re-signed with Miami on March 11, 2026. GM Jon-Eric Sullivan identified Dulcich as a 2026 riser after a strong back end of 2025 season, marking his return to the Dolphins.

items.headline: Uche signs one-year deal with Dolphins after Eagles stint
items.summary: Joshua Uche, drafted 2nd round (2020) by Patriots from Michigan, signed a one-year contract with Miami on March 12, 2026. After breakout 2022 (11.5 sacks), Uche played for Eagles in 2025 before joining Miami. Had recorded 3 sacks in 2023 and 2 in 2024.

items.headline: Brooks leads Dolphins with 183 tackles, seeks raise amid rookie class
items.summary: Jordyn Brooks, first-round pick (2020) by Seattle from Texas Tech, signed three-year, $26.3M deal with Miami on March 14, 2024. In 2025, Brooks recorded league-leading 183 tackles (99 solo) and became fifth player since 2000 to reach 800 tackles in first six NFL seasons.

items.headline: Wilson signs one-year deal after stints with Cardinals, Patriots, Bengals
items.summary: Marco Wilson, fourth-round pick (2021) by Arizona from Florida, signed one-year contract with Dolphins on March 13, 2026. 6'0", 191 lbs cornerback from Fort Lauderdale recorded 178 tackles, 3 INTs, and 20 pass defenses across five seasons with Arizona, New England, and Cincinnati.

items.headline: Gordon won 2023 Doak Walker Award before 6th-round selection by Dolphins
items.summary: Ollie Gordon II, sixth-round pick (2025) from Oklahoma State, won Doak Walker Award in 2023 after rushing for 1,600+ yards with 20 TDs. Over three college seasons, Gordon compiled 3,500+ career rushing yards from scrimmage with 40 total touchdowns for the Cowboys.

items.headline: Tolbert joins Dolphins via free agency, has connection with Malik Willis
items.summary: Jalen Tolbert, third-round pick (2022) by Cowboys from South Alabama, signed one-year contract with Miami on March 16, 2026. Caught 91 passes for 1,093 yards and 10 TDs in Dallas. Tolbert reportedly has close relationship with new Dolphins QB Malik Willis, influencing his Miami decision.

items.headline: Henning suspended by NFL for PED violation after Dolphins UDFA signing
items.summary: Antiwan Henning Jr. (born 9/9/2001), undrafted in 2025 from Northwestern (transferred from Michigan), signed with Dolphins on May 9, 2025. On December 4, 2025, Henning was suspended by NFL for violating performance-enhancing substances policy. Spent entire 2025 season on practice squad.

items.headline: Jackson's grandfather Melvin also played offensive line at USC and NFL with Packers
items.summary: Austin Jackson, first-round pick (2020) by Miami from USC, started all games protecting Tua Tagovailoa before signing three-year, $36M extension. His grandfather Melvin Jackson played offensive line at USC and for Green Bay Packers, establishing family NFL legacy.

items.headline: Paul drafted 2nd round (2024) by Dolphins from Houston with All-Conference honors
items.summary: Patrick Paul, second-round pick (55th overall) in 2024 NFL draft from University of Houston, earned All-Conference honors in 2023. Now left tackle for Dolphins offensive line alongside Aaron Brewer at center and Austin Jackson at right tackle.

items.headline: Edwards won national championship at Michigan, signed off Commanders practice squad
items.summary: Donovan Edwards from West Bloomfield, Michigan (6-1, 212 lbs, age 23) won national championship with Michigan in 2023. UDFA signed to Jets, then claimed by Commanders practice squad, finally signed by Dolphins on January 2, 2026. At Michigan, he recorded 422 rushing attempts for 2,251 yards with 19 TDs.

items.headline: Pinion signs with Dolphins after 11 seasons with 49ers and other teams
items.summary: Bradley Pinion (born 6/1/1994), fifth-round pick (2015) by San Francisco from Clemson, signed one-year, $1.48M deal with Dolphins in March 2026. Two-time Walter Payton Man of the Year nominee with 44.5 career punt average; last season with Atlanta averaged 45.1 yards on 64 punts.

items.headline: Dodson achieves first 100-tackle season, re-signed by Dolphins
items.summary: Tyrel Dodson, former Texas A&M linebacker, was claimed by Dolphins November 12, 2024, and re-signed March 12, 2025. In 2025, the 27-year-old recorded first 100-tackle season of his career while joining Jordyn Brooks as only every-down starter linebacker duo. Career best sack total as well.

items.headline: Sieler signs $64M extension, first Dolphins to record back-to-back double-digit sack seasons since Cameron Wake
items.summary: Zach Sieler, seventh-round pick (2018) from Ferris State via Baltimore, signed three-year, $64M extension with Dolphins in 2025 ($44M guaranteed). Recorded 55 tackles, 10 sacks, 1 INT in 2025—second consecutive double-digit sack season, first Dolphin since Cameron Wake (2016-17).

items.headline: Rookie DT Phillips (2004) drafted 5th round from Maryland, best run-defender on team
items.summary: Jordan Phillips (born 2004), fifth-round pick in 2025 from Maryland, 6-3, 320 lbs. Despite being day-3 pick, quickly became best run-defending DT on Dolphins with 321 defensive snaps. Maryland product is showing rapid development in third year of NFL eligibility.

items.headline: Robinson nicknamed 'Pork-Chop' after weighing 11-12 pounds at birth
items.summary: Demeioun 'Chop' Robinson (born 1/2/2003), first-round pick (21st overall, 2024) by Dolphins from Maryland/Penn State, earned NFL All-Rookie Team honors with 6 sacks in 2024. Nicknamed 'Pork-chop' because he weighed 11 or 12 pounds at birth; nickname was shortened to 'Chop'.

items.headline: Gay Jr., two-time Super Bowl champion, re-signs one-year deal with Dolphins
items.summary: Willie Gay Jr. (born 2/15/1998) from Mississippi State, drafted 2020 by Kansas City, is two-time Super Bowl champion (LVII, LVIII). Signed one-year deal March 19, 2025, re-signed March 12, 2026. In six NFL seasons: 253 tackles (164 solo), 7.0 sacks, 4 INTs, 22 pass defenses.

items.headline: Brents claimed off waivers from Colts, suffered season-ending foot injury
items.summary: JuJu Brents (born 1/18/2000), second-round pick (44th overall, 2023) from Iowa/Kansas State by Colts, claimed off waivers by Dolphins August 27, 2025. Suffered season-ending foot surgery after leaving Dolphins' Week 11 win vs Washington Commanders. Recorded 60 tackles, 1 INT in 18 games.

items.headline: Ojabo reunites with mentor Josh Uche at Michigan after Ravens stint
items.summary: David Ojabo (Nigerian-Scottish) was All-American for Michigan but suffered torn Achilles on Pro Day. Second-round pick (45th, 2022) by Ravens, spent four seasons accumulating 4.5 sacks. Signed March 16, 2026 with Dolphins to reunite with Josh Uche, his Michigan mentor.

items.headline: Turner signed futures contract after years on practice squads
items.summary: Cole Turner (born 3/16/2000), fifth-round pick (149th, 2022) by Washington from Nevada, switched from WR to TE his senior year. After practice squad stints with Washington and Tennessee, signed reserve/futures contract with Dolphins January 9, 2026. Hasn't played regular season since Week 16, 2023.

items.headline: Daniels moves between teams, signs futures deal after Cowboys waiver wire
items.summary: Braeden Daniels (born 8/22/2000, Carrollton, Texas), fourth-round pick (2023) by Washington from Utah, signed to Miami futures contract January 9, 2026. At 6-4, 305 lbs with 2 NFL seasons, Daniels bounced from Cowboys practice squad; Dolphins signed him twice with practice squad assignments.

items.headline: Johnson Jr., former Texans 2nd-rounder, brings versatility to Dolphins secondary
items.summary: Lonnie Johnson Jr., second-round pick (2018) by Houston from Kentucky, signed one-year deal March 12, 2026. Seven-year NFL veteran at age 30 with experience at post safety, wide corner, slot, and box. In 92 career games: 224 tackles, 4 INTs, 17 pass defenses. Last season with Raiders as free safety.

items.headline: Green III signed off Rams practice squad, re-signed by Dolphins
items.summary: A.J. Green III (born 6/9/1998), UDFA signed by Cleveland from Oklahoma State (2020), signed by Miami off Rams practice squad November 19, 2025. Re-signed with Dolphins March 11, 2026. 6'2", 198 lbs cornerback wearing #24 competing for depth role.

items.headline: Brewer earns Second-Team All-Pro, ranked 2nd at center with 87.4 PFF grade
items.summary: Aaron Brewer from Texas State, UDFA by Tennessee (2020), signed three-year, $21M contract with Dolphins March 15, 2024. In 2025, earned Second-Team All-Pro with 87.4 PFF grade (2nd among centers). Run-blocking grade of 91.5 ranked 1st at position; allowed only 1 sack all season.

items.headline: Heck's father Andy is KC Chiefs OL coach, former Notre Dame All-American
items.summary: Charlie Heck (born 11/20/1996), fourth-round pick (2020) by Houston from North Carolina, signed one-year, $1.2M deal March 16, 2026. At 6-8, 311 lbs, he's very large lineman. His father Andy Heck coached OL for KC Chiefs, former Notre Dame All-American, 12-year NFL player.

items.headline: Conyers signed as UDFA from Texas Tech, placed on injured reserve
items.summary: Jalin Conyers (born 7/25/2001), undrafted from Texas Tech (via Oklahoma and Arizona State), signed with Dolphins May 9, 2025. In three seasons at Arizona State (2021-23), recorded 74 receptions for 846 yards and 6 TDs. Placed on injured reserve with foot injury.

items.headline: Harrison Jr., Alabama star, signs one-year deal after Falcons stint
items.summary: Ronnie Harrison Jr., third-round pick (2018) by Jacksonville from Alabama, signed one-year contract March 25, 2026. Eight-year NFL veteran has played LB and S: 274 tackles, 8.5 sacks, 7 INTs, 27 pass defenses. Recent Falcons (2025) performance: 29 tackles, 2 sacks in 10 games.

items.headline: Butler claimed off waivers from Las Vegas, re-signed by Dolphins
items.summary: Matthew Butler (born 6/10/1999) from Tennessee, fifth-round pick (175th, 2022) by Las Vegas, claimed off waivers by Dolphins May 2025 and re-signed March 2026. DT recorded 15 tackles (5 solo), 0.5 sacks in 15 games with Raiders. At Tennessee: 152 tackles, 9.5 sacks in 53 games.

items.headline: Washington led nation with 110 receptions at Virginia before 6th-round draft
items.summary: Malik Washington (WR/return specialist), sixth-round pick (184th, 2024) from Northwestern/Virginia, signed four-year, $4.2M contract with Dolphins. In 2023, led nation with 110 receptions (school record) for 1,426 yards, 9 TDs. Made 14 appearances (3 starts) in 2024 rookie season: 26 catches, 223 yards.

items.headline: Washington missed 2024 but impressed in preseason debut for Dolphins
items.summary: Tahj Washington (born 5/16/2001), seventh-round pick (241st, 2024) from Memphis/USC, 5-10 WR for Dolphins. Missed entire 2024 season due to injury. In preseason 2025 debut, recorded 3 catches for 53 yards. At USC: 59 catches, 1,062 yards, 8 TDs in final season.

items.headline: Wease undrafted, promoted to active roster after practice squad stint
items.summary: Theo Wease Jr., undrafted in 2025 from Missouri/Oklahoma, signed by Dolphins May 9, 2025. Spent final two seasons at Missouri as team captain with 109 receptions for 1,566 yards, 10 TDs (26 starts). Waived August 26, re-signed to practice squad August 27, promoted to active roster December 27. 6 catches, 139 yards, 1 TD in 2025.

items.headline: Beal Jr. signed by Dolphins after limited snaps with 49ers
items.summary: Robert Beal Jr. (born 8/18/1999), fifth-round pick (173rd, 2023) by San Francisco from Georgia, signed with Dolphins March 12, 2026. DE appeared in 14 games with 49ers, contributed 14 tackles on defense/special teams. At 247 lbs with 4.48 40-yard dash (99th percentile), elite athleticism for edge rusher.

items.headline: Grant drafted 13th overall 2025 from Michigan national champions, All-American
items.summary: Kenneth Grant, first-round pick (13th overall, 2025) by Dolphins from Michigan, 6-4, 331 lbs DT. Won national championship 2023, earned All-American honors 2024. Produced 27-23 pressures last two seasons (10.4% pressure rate). Second DT taken in draft after Michigan teammate Mason Graham.

items.headline: Biggers blocked more FGs than any college player last 4 years, drafted 7th round
items.summary: Zeek Biggers, seventh-round pick (253rd, 2025) from Georgia Tech, 320 lbs DT. Blocked more field goals in college football last four seasons (one per season average) than any other player. 85-1/8" wingspan in 98th percentile at Combine. Two-year starter at Georgia Tech.

items.headline: Priebe UDFA from Michigan, third-team All-Big Ten consecutive seasons
items.summary: Josh Priebe (born 7/30/2001), undrafted from Northwestern/Michigan, signed with Dolphins as UDFA April 26, 2025. Waived August 26, re-signed to practice squad August 27. Started all 13 games at LG for Michigan in 2024, third-team All-Big Ten selection for consecutive season.

items.headline: Salyer signed as free agent, started 40 games at guard/tackle for Chargers
items.summary: Jamaree Salyer, sixth-round pick (2022) from Georgia by Los Angeles, signed unrestricted free agent deal March 13, 2026. Started 40 games at G/T over four Chargers seasons. At 2022 Combine: 80th percentile weight, 94th percentile bench (31 reps), 68th percentile short-shuttle (4.70).

items.headline: Savaiinaea drafted 2nd round from Arizona, struggling rookie moving to right side
items.summary: Jonah Savaiinaea, second-round pick (37th overall, 2025) from Arizona, born in Tafuna, American Samoa, attended Saint Louis School in Honolulu. Earned 28.4 PFF grade in 2025 (81st of 81 guards), allowed most pressures (45) and sacks (8) of any qualified guard. Dolphins moving him to right side.

items.headline: Punter from college football
items.summary: Brett Thorson is a punter who joined the Vikings special teams unit.

items.headline: From Giants backup to Patriots QB with Italian heritage pride
items.summary: DeVito became known as 'Tommy Cutlets' due to his Italian heritage and familiarity with chicken cutlets from living with his parents. He lived at home during his rookie season with the Giants, leveraging proximity to their facilities. His rise to prominence earned him Super Bowl commercials, allowing him to move out before the 2024 season. Known for being openly proud of his Italian-American background.

items.headline: Louisiana native starred at LSU with breakout single-game performance
items.summary: Boutte played at LSU and set a Southeastern Conference record with 14 receptions for 308 yards and 3 touchdowns in a single game against Ole Miss in 2020. From Westgate High School in New Iberia, Louisiana, he was a standout in the 2020 Under Armour All-American Game. 6th round draft pick in 2023.

items.headline: Offensive tackle married with four children
items.summary: Morgan Moses married Jessica Morgan in May 2018 and is a father of four: Isaiah, Natalia, Jeremiah, and Kyrie. His family is central to his story, with tattoos representing his marriage and children. Jessica provides unwavering support and is frequently present at his games and team events.

items.headline: Three-time All-Pro safety with two children, married since 2018
items.summary: Byard married Clarke Conner in 2018. The couple has two children: daughter Eliana Rose (born August 22, 2019) and son Kevin IV (born August 23, 2020). Byard is a practicing Christian. His parents divorced when he was 14, after which he moved to Atlanta with his mother and siblings.

sources.name: Boston Globe - Kevin Byard Four Things

items.headline: Alabama defensive star overcome blood clots issue
items.summary: First defensive tackle selected in 2021 (38th overall). Won Defensive MVP of 2021 College Football Playoff National Championship. In July 2024, diagnosed with blood clots and placed on reserve/non-football injury list. Signed $92M extension in April 2024, making him highest-paid Patriots player (non-Brady).

items.headline: Undersized slot cornerback and elite punt returner
items.summary: At 5'8", 188 lbs, Jones is among the shortest players in the NFL. Named first-team All-Pro as punt returner (362 yards, 12.5 avg). Team captain. Signed 3-year, $36M extension in October 2024. From Troy and Houston, drafted 2022 3rd round.

items.headline: Liberty University receiver 'Pop' found his element in slot role
items.summary: Called 'Pop', Douglas transferred from Mandarin High School (Jacksonville, FL). Drafted 210th overall 2023 (6th round). Averaged 12.3 air yards per target at Liberty as senior. Team-best 561 receiving yards as rookie. Thriving in new slot role as vertical receiver this season.

items.headline: Super Bowl LV champion cornerback with Patriots since 2024
items.summary: Won Super Bowl LV with Buccaneers (5 combined tackles in game). Tampa Bay drafted him 2nd round 2018. Spent 6 seasons with Bucs before 2024 season with Detroit. Patriots signed him to 3-year, $60M deal. Started all 17 regular season games plus both playoff games.

items.headline: Los Angeles native and Packers receiver signs massive Patriots deal
items.summary: Born April 13, 2000 in Los Angeles. Played at Nevada (225 receptions, 3,322 yards, 26 TDs). Drafted 4th round 2022 by Green Bay. 6'2", 204 lbs. Signed 4-year, $68M deal with Patriots in March 2026.

items.headline: Talented but injury-prone guard with extensive versatility
items.summary: 14th overall Jets pick 2021. One of top 10 guards when healthy (2024). Missed all 2024 season with triceps injury. Torn Achilles 2023, season-ending triceps 2022. Missed 42 of 85 possible games since draft. Patriots signed 3-year, $42M deal with $250k/game bonuses. Can play all OL positions except center.

items.headline: Maryland native with close-knit family and blended household
items.summary: Born September 16, 1993 in Rockville, Maryland. Middle of three sons to Richard and Karyn Hollins. Brother Brian raised $60M+ in funding for company; brother Andrew served 4+ years in U.S. Marine Corps. Partner Lamorna and daughter Mason; couple added second child. Mother Karyn sports team merch at all games.

items.headline: Center with Super Bowl legacy through grandfather
items.summary: Grandfather Allen Brown was reserve tight end for Green Bay Packers 1965-67, winning two league championships and Super Bowl I & II rings. Ben went undrafted 2022 after injury (Ole Miss). Played for Bengals, Seahawks, Raiders before Patriots signed him October 2024. Started all 10 games in 2024. Signed 2-year, $6.6M extension December 2025.

items.headline: Veteran edge rusher pursuing first playoff appearance with Patriots
items.summary: 8-season veteran signed 3-year, $39.5M deal March 2026. Played for Seahawks, Titans, Ravens. Career-high 7 sacks last season. 6'3", 281 lbs. Former Mike Vrabel recruit at Ohio State. Goal: make playoffs for first time in NFL career. Fits Vrabel's preferred defensive brand.

items.headline: Linebacker recovering from knee injury, signed major deal
items.summary: Boston College product. 2nd round 2018 Titans pick. 3-year, $43.5M deal March 2025 with Patriots. Had 49 tackles, 8.5 sacks, 1 FF in 15 games 2025 before knee injury. Frequently attending Patriots facility for rehabilitation and recovery work this offseason.

items.headline: Familiar face: cornerback reunites with Vrabel and Davis
items.summary: Georgia Southern 2020 5th round pick by Bears. Played 1.5 seasons with Lions under Terrell Williams (now Patriots assistant) and alongside Carlton Davis. Spent last season with Buccaneers (15.7% defensive snaps). Signed 1-year deal March 2026. Competes with Woods and Minor for 3rd CB spot.

items.headline: Linebacker grandson of 1953 Heisman Trophy winner Johnny Lattner
items.summary: Born and raised in Oak Park, Illinois. Grandson of 1953 Heisman Trophy winner Johnny Lattner. Western Michigan product. Undrafted 2018, signed Titans (2 games), then Steelers 4 seasons. Raiders 2023. Patriots signed 3-year, $33M deal March 2025. Played running back and linebacker at Fenwick High.

items.headline: Highest-paid Patriot ever—Alabama defensive lineman dominates
items.summary: Born April 6, 1999. Louisiana Tech product. 3rd round 2021 Eagles pick. Signed 4-year, $104M deal March 2025 (highest-paid Patriot ever). 2025: ranked 4th in points allowed (18.8 ppg), 8th in total yards (295.2 ypg). Wild Card: 2 sacks vs Chargers sealing 16-3 victory.

items.headline: 3rd round pick never played NFL game due to injuries
items.summary: 3rd round 2019 pick by San Francisco (67th overall). Baylor WR, transferred from Tennessee where he played RB. Missed first two NFL seasons (2019-2020) due to injuries. Started 2021 on IR, released November 2021. Signed Patriots July 2023 but announced retirement one week later.

items.headline: Rookie RB modeling game after Patriots legend James White
items.summary: 2nd round 2025 (38th overall) Ohio State RB. Models his game after James White, praising his receiving ability. Reached 22.01 mph top speed on 55-yard TD. Already considered best RB on roster by analysts. Coach Vrabel addressed Henderson's social media comments about personal beliefs vs team representation.

items.headline: First-round left tackle and SEC's top 2024 blocker
items.summary: 4th overall 2025 (first OL in Patriots top 10 since 1974). 19 years old, broke upperclassman's job in freshman year and never relinquished it. Won 2024 Jacobs Blocking Trophy (top SEC lineman). Consensus All-American. Father Brian 'Bull' Campbell played OL, farms. Changed culture at LSU from Day 1.

items.headline: Wyoming linebacker signed off Colts practice squad for playoff push
items.summary: 3rd round 2022 Jaguars pick. 2021 second-team All-American, Butkus Award finalist (one of six). Waived Jaguars August 2025. Colts signed him, waived November. Patriots signed off Colts practice squad December 16, 2025. Made debut Baltimore 12/21.

items.headline: Transfer punter set team record with 49.8 yard average
items.summary: Illinois 2018 walk-on. Sat bench, transferred home to Michigan State as student. 2024: 70 punts, 3,486 yards, 49.8 average (team record, 4th in NFL). Four 70+ yard punts (most since 2000). 6th round 2023 (192nd overall). Holder on FGs/XPs. Undisputed roster lock.

items.headline: Versatile lineman with year 2 All-Pro honors
items.summary: Michigan 6th round 2020. Named PFWA All-Rookie Team (started all 16 games). Played RG, LG, RT—most flexible O-lineman. March 2024: 3-year, $57M extension. 2024-25: started all 17 games. Projects to RG alongside Morgan Moses in 2026.

items.headline: Tight end depth option with Dolphins background
items.summary: Campbell undrafted free agent 2023 (6'4", 251 lbs). 3 NFL seasons with Miami: 45 games, 26 starts, 33 receptions, 288 yards. Patriots signed 3-year deal March 2026 ($7.5M guaranteed). Backup/blocker behind Hunter Henry. Born July 9, 2000.

items.headline: Auburn linebacker with five NFL seasons
items.summary: Born June 4, 1999, Oxford, AL. Auburn product. 5th round 2021 Buccaneers pick. 5 NFL seasons: Bucs 2021-24, Dolphins 2025. Patriots signed 1-year deal March 2026. Solid run defender but coverage limitations. Plug-and-play in middle of defense.

items.headline: Venezuelan-born kicker first from country to play Super Bowl
items.summary: First Venezuelan player to suit up for Super Bowl. Miami first kicker drafted 2025 (6th round, 182nd overall). 2025: 27/32 FGs (84%), 53/55 XPs (96%). AFC Championship: only FG in 10-7 win over Denver. Super Bowl LX: converted only XP in 29-13 loss to Seattle.

items.headline: Baltimore native schools record-holder leads rookie receivers
items.summary: 3rd round 2025 (69th). Washington State record: 14 TD receptions 2024. UNLV transfer. 70 receptions, 1,198 yards, 14 TDs in 2024 (4th in WSU single-season history). Born Baltimore, raised Inglewood, CA. Appeared all 17 games 2025 (10 rec, 209 yds, 3 TDs rookie).

items.headline: Troy undrafted free agent became third safety option
items.summary: Born June 2, 2001. Troy Trojan. UDFA signed May 2024. Developed from roster bubble player to 3rd safety. Plays special teams (FGs, XPs, punts, kickoffs) and rotational safety. 5'11", 200 lbs. Key contributor on coverage units.

items.headline: Rookie cornerback tears ACL in training camp
items.summary: 6th round 2024 (180th). South Carolina product. Played Georgia Military College. 2024: 17 games, 1 start (special teams). Summer 2025: tore ACL during training camp practice. Missed all 2025 season recovering. Has high hopes for 2026 comeback.

items.headline: FAMU running back sidelined from Super Bowl with concussion
items.summary: Born March 1, 2001. Florida A&M product (1,860 yards, 24 TDs on 281 carries, 44 games). UDFA 2024. 2 seasons, 36 carries, 106 yards, 1 catch, 9 yards, 1 TD. Concussed vs Giants (12/1), IR after bye. Returned practice (1/14). 21-day window closed, not active for Super Bowl LX.

items.headline: Rookie safety leads SEC in turnovers, stolen from Dolphins
items.summary: 6'2", 211 lbs. Miami RedHawks and Ole Miss. 2024: led SEC with 3 INTs, 4 forced fumbles. UDFA Dolphins 2025. Patriots signed off Dolphins practice squad. Versatile: free safety, strong safety, slot, cornerback. Finished 2025 on Patriots practice squad (11/19).

items.headline: Special teams All-Pro with crossover defensive skill set
items.summary: 2022 UDFA. Oregon and Texas product (safety + WR). Signed May 2022. PFWA All-Rookie. NFLPA All-Pro 2023-24. October 2024: 3-year, $9M extension. Week 10 2025: first sack (Caleb Williams). 2024: 13 tackles, 2 sacks. Elite on special teams (punts, KOs, FGs).

items.headline: Slot corner projects from SMU through Rams to Patriots
items.summary: Born June 17, 2000. Illinois State, West Virginia, SMU. 2024 Senior: 27 tackles, 11 pass breakups, 3rd-team All-AAC. Undrafted 2024, signed Rams UDFA, made 53-man. Patriots claimed waivers (8/27/25). 5'11", 185 lbs. Slot corner projection.

items.headline: Chicago native's European detour leads to Patriots contract
items.summary: Born May 17, 2001, Chicago. South Dakota/South Alabama (36 rec, 649 yards, 5 TDs final season). Briefly Berlin Thunder (European League), then Patriots signed 3-year, $2.967M deal (4/28/25). Preseason: 6 rec, 80 yards, 1 TD. Released 8/26, re-signed practice squad 8/28.

items.headline: Eastern Washington receiver broke Cooper Kupp's school record
items.summary: Eastern Washington UDFA 2025. Career: 346 receptions, 3,852 yards, 37 TDs. Senior: 120 catches (school record), 1,311 yards, 13 scores. Signed 5/9/25 citing Vrabel/McDaniels' work with undersized WRs. Preseason: 12 catches, 121 yards, 2 TDs. Made 53-man roster. Season: 3 rec, 75 yards, 1 TD, 16 KR.

items.headline: Mr. Irrelevant 2025: last pick makes roster by January
items.summary: 7th round 2025 (257th/last) Patriots. Texas Tech, Indiana, Memphis. 'Mr. Irrelevant' 2025. 5'11", 182 lbs, 4.56 40. Released 8/26, practice squad 8/27, active roster 1/1/26. Fluid, versatile defender with good instincts, willing ST contributor.

items.headline: Rookie safety earns 10th highest performance bonus payout
items.summary: 4th round 2025 from California. Played all 17 games, started 15. Wild Card: 16-3 vs Chargers. Super Bowl LX: led team with 10 tackles in 29-13 loss to Seattle. Highest performance-based pay distribution (10th in NFL): $1.149M bonus plus salary/incentives. Expected 2026 starter alongside Byard.

items.headline: Texas A&M tackle converts from tight end, projects as developmental swing piece
items.summary: 6th round 2026 (196th). Texas A&M. Switched from TE to OT 2022. Right tackle mainstay all 13 games 2024-25 (25 starts over 2 years). 6'7", 319 lbs, 36-inch arms. Pass protection strong suit. Learning left side—goal to master both positions. Patriots project him as backup swing tackle.

sources.name: Boston.com - Four things to know about Crownover

items.headline: TE-to-fullback convert emerges as key Super Bowl contributor
items.summary: Born June 9, 1999. Washington Husky TE → FB conversion. Original Seahawks UDFA (5/3/24). Patriots practice squad 10/2/24, active 1/4/25. 2025: all 17 games, 2 starts. Super Bowl LX: 5 offensive snaps, 22 ST snaps vs Seattle. 1-year tender 2026 ($1.075M, non-guaranteed).

items.headline: SMU cornerback bounces back with futures deal after training camp cut
items.summary: SMU/Colorado State CB. 25 years old. 66 apps (146 tackles, 6 INTs, return TDs). 2024 SMU senior: 13 starts, 35 tackles, 1 INT, All-ACC honorable mention. UDFA 2025, released training camp. Signed practice squad mid-November, released after regular season. Futures contract 2026 competing for roster spot.
