items.headline: Historic family milestone: T.J. Watt surpasses brother J.J. in career sacks
items.summary: T.J. Watt passed his older brother J.J. Watt in career sacks, becoming the player with the most sacks in the Watt family. The brothers are the only pair to each record 100+ career sacks since 1982. T.J. also has a third brother, Derek, a former NFL fullback.

items.headline: Dating history: Steelers QB Mason Rudolph previously linked to tennis star Eugenie Bouchard
items.summary: Mason Rudolph was in a relationship with Canadian tennis star and Wimbledon finalist Eugenie Bouchard from 2020-2022. They went Instagram official in 2021 before breaking up in 2022 on good terms. Rudolph is currently single as of 2025.

items.headline: Steelers RB Jaylen Warren grew up in Salt Lake City after birth in North Carolina
items.summary: Running back Jaylen Warren was born November 1, 1998, in Clinton, North Carolina, but grew up in Salt Lake City, Utah. His full name is Derrell Jaylen Warren.

items.headline: Steelers CB Asante Samuel Jr. is son of Hall-caliber cornerback Asante Samuel Sr.
items.summary: Cornerback Asante Samuel Jr., born October 3, 1999, is the son of former NFL cornerback Asante Samuel, who had a stellar career with the Philadelphia Eagles and Atlanta Falcons as a top interception cornerback. Samuel Jr. was drafted by the Chargers in 2021 before joining the Steelers.

items.headline: Steelers CB Joey Porter Jr. follows father into Pittsburgh defense
items.summary: Cornerback Joey Porter Jr. was drafted by the Steelers in the 2023 NFL draft, becoming a Pro Bowl All-Rookie selection. He is the son of legendary outside linebacker Joey Porter Sr., who played 13 seasons for the Steelers, earning four Pro Bowl selections and winning Super Bowl XL. Joey Porter Sr. was inducted into the Steelers Hall of Honor in 2025.

items.headline: Steelers TE Pat Freiermuth holds NFL tight end franchise records
items.summary: Tight end Pat Freiermuth, born October 25, 1998, holds the Steelers record for most receptions by a TE in their first four years (220) and most receiving yards (2,190). He attended Penn State and was drafted in the second round of the 2021 NFL draft, wearing jersey #88.

items.headline: Steelers DT Keeanu Benton has father figure Troy Rodriguez and strong Wisconsin bond
items.summary: Defensive tackle Keeanu Benton, born July 17, 2001, was raised primarily by mother La'Tasha Benton and father figure Troy Rodriguez, who wasn't his biological father but was deeply influential on his football path. Benton considers Wisconsin teammate Keeanu Benton a brother. Benton played for Wisconsin and was drafted by Pittsburgh.

items.headline: Steelers TE Darnell Washington overcame childhood homelessness to reach NFL
items.summary: Tight end Darnell Washington, born March 28, 2001, experienced homelessness throughout his childhood in Las Vegas. He had an older brother in the U.S. Marines and is himself a young father. Despite these challenges, he achieved his NFL dream after playing college football at Georgia.

items.headline: Steelers WR Roman Wilson won National Championship at Michigan before ankle injuries
items.summary: Wide receiver Roman Wilson, born June 19, 2001, played for the Michigan Wolverines, winning three consecutive Big Ten titles and the national championship in 2023. Drafted third round in 2024, injuries limited his rookie year to one game, though he recorded 166 yards and two TDs in 13 games in 2025.

items.headline: Steelers S Jaquan Brisker returns home to Pittsburgh after Chicago Bears stint
items.summary: Safety Jaquan Brisker, born April 20, 1999, is from Wilkinsburg, Pennsylvania, just outside Pittsburgh. He attended Gateway High School and played college football at Penn State before being drafted by the Bears. In March 2026, he signed with his hometown Steelers on a one-year, $5.5M deal.

items.headline: Steelers CB Jamel Dean was last remaining Bucs Super Bowl LV champion
items.summary: Cornerback Jamel Dean, drafted by Tampa Bay in 2019, played 7 years with the Buccaneers before signing with Pittsburgh in March 2026. He was the last remaining player from Tampa's Super Bowl LV championship team, recording four tackles and one pass defense against the Chiefs. In 2025, he had career-best three interceptions.

items.headline: Steelers LB Nick Herbig reunited with brother Nate in Pittsburgh
items.summary: Linebacker Nick Herbig is the youngest of three brothers on the island of Kauai, Hawaii. His older brother Nate Herbig is a guard for the Steelers, allowing the brothers to play together. Parents Robyn and Bruce Herbig both have sports backgrounds. Third brother Jake is a computer interface designer in Dallas.

items.headline: Steelers LB Alex Highsmith drafted from University of Charlotte
items.summary: Outside linebacker Alex Highsmith was drafted by the Steelers in the third round, 102nd overall, of the 2020 NFL draft. He attended the University of Charlotte before becoming a key defensive contributor for Pittsburgh.

items.headline: Steelers LB Cole Holcomb married Casey DiNardo in 2022
items.summary: Linebacker Cole Holcomb married Casey DiNardo on June 18, 2022. Casey (born April 22, 1994) is the daughter of Kathy and Jimmy DiNardo, whose father played football at William & Mary. Cole's parents, Jack and Beth, co-own auto dealerships in New Smyrna Beach. The couple welcomed daughter Morgan James Holcomb on June 28, 2024.

items.headline: Steelers LB Malik Harrison signed two-year deal after Baltimore Ravens tenure
items.summary: Linebacker Malik Harrison, born March 5, 1998, played college football for Ohio State and was drafted by the Baltimore Ravens in the third round of 2020. He appeared in 76 games for Baltimore, starting 34. In March 2025, he signed a two-year, $10M contract with Pittsburgh to compete at inside linebacker.

items.headline: Steelers CB Brandin Echols improved production after Jets stint
items.summary: Cornerback Brandin Echols, born October 16, 1997, was drafted by the New York Jets in the sixth round of 2021. After four seasons with New York, he signed a two-year, $6M contract with Pittsburgh in March 2025, and saw an uptick in production with six passes defensed (two INTs) in 2025.

items.headline: Steelers S DeShon Elliott fulfilled childhood dream joining Pittsburgh
items.summary: Safety DeShon Elliott, born April 21, 1997, grew up in Dallas where he played on a youth football team named the Steelers. This early connection to the franchise led him to sign with the Pittsburgh Steelers in March 2024 on a two-year, $6M deal, fulfilling a childhood connection formed through youth football.

items.headline: Steelers OT Troy Fautanu won Morris Trophy at Washington before early NFL success
items.summary: Offensive tackle Troy Fautanu, born October 11, 2000, won the 2023 Morris Trophy at the University of Washington. Drafted first round in 2024, he started as right tackle in 2025, allowing zero sacks on 225 snaps. He is a longtime Steelers fan from Nevada.

items.headline: Steelers C Zach Frazier learns sign language; 16 family members deaf
items.summary: Center Zach Frazier learned sign language as a child because 16 family members, including an aunt, uncle, and cousins on his mother's side, are deaf. His mother's side has state champion wrestlers; his father played football for Fairmont State. He married Stephanie and was drafted by Pittsburgh in 2024 as rookie offensive player of year.

items.headline: Steelers OT Broderick Jones won back-to-back CFP National Championships at Georgia
items.summary: Offensive tackle Broderick Jones, born May 16, 2001, played for Georgia where he won the CFP national championship twice. Drafted 14th overall in 2023, he made his first start in Week 5 against Baltimore with no sacks allowed. Started all 17 games in 2024. Currently recovering from neck surgery.

items.headline: Steelers RB Rico Dowdle reunites with Coach Mike McCarthy
items.summary: Running back Rico Dowdle signed a two-year, $12.25M contract with Pittsburgh in March 2026. He reunited with Steelers head coach Mike McCarthy, who was his coach in Dallas during his breakout 2024 season. Dowdle had back-to-back 1,000-yard rushing seasons with Dallas and Carolina.

items.headline: Steelers QB Will Howard won National Championship at Ohio State
items.summary: Quarterback Will Howard transferred from Kansas State to Ohio State for his final season, leading the Buckeyes to the 2024 national championship. Drafted in the sixth round (185th overall) by Pittsburgh in 2025, he mentored under Aaron Rodgers in 2025. McCarthy has spoken positively about developing Howard.

items.headline: Steelers QB Drew Allar was first Penn State quarterback drafted by Pittsburgh
items.summary: Quarterback Drew Allar played college football at Penn State, appearing in 45 games with 35 starts. He holds Penn State career records in completion percentage (63.2%) and interception percentage (1.3%). A season-ending ankle injury in October 2025 impacted his draft stock but Pittsburgh selected him 76th overall in 2026, viewed as a steal.

items.headline: Steelers WR Ben Skowronek earned Pro Bowl honors on special teams
items.summary: Wide receiver Ben Skowronek, born June 27, 1997, played college football at Northwestern and Notre Dame before being drafted by the Rams in 2021. Signed to Pittsburgh's practice squad in August 2024 and promoted to active roster. Earned Pro Bowl honors for special teams in 2025 and caught the team's first TD pass from Aaron Rodgers.

items.headline: Steelers LB Carson Bruener is son of former Steelers tight end Mark Bruener
items.summary: Linebacker Carson Bruener, born June 5, 2001, is the son of former Steelers tight end and current scout Mark Bruener. Drafted by Pittsburgh in the seventh round of 2025 (226th overall) from the University of Washington, Bruener recorded his first professional tackle in the Week 1 victory over the Jets.

items.headline: Steelers LB Payton Wilson won Bednalik and Butkus Awards at NC State
items.summary: Linebacker Payton Wilson, from Hillsborough, North Carolina, won the Chuck Bednalik Award and Butkus Award in 2023 while playing for NC State. Drafted by Pittsburgh in the third round (98th overall) in 2024, he recorded 64 tackles in 2024 and 126 tackles in 2025 with two sacks and an interception.

items.headline: Steelers LB Jack Sawyer won 2025 National Championship at Ohio State
items.summary: Outside linebacker Jack Sawyer was drafted by Pittsburgh in the fourth round (123rd overall) of 2025. At Ohio State, he recorded 23 career sacks including 9 in 2024, winning the 2025 CFP National Championship against Notre Dame. He had a strip-sack fumble return TD in the Cotton Bowl.

items.headline: Steelers CB Cory Trice Jr. battled ACL injuries at Purdue and in NFL
items.summary: Cornerback Cory Trice Jr., born May 30, 2000, played for Purdue where he was moved to cornerback in 2019 after starting as safety. He recorded five interceptions and 20 pass breakups before tearing his ACL in 2021. Drafted seventh round in 2023, injuries continued to plague him in the NFL.

items.headline: Steelers CB D'Shawn Jamison's family displaced by Hurricane Katrina
items.summary: Cornerback D'Shawn Jamison was born and lived in New Orleans until his family was displaced by Hurricane Katrina in 2005 and subsequently settled in Houston, Texas. This early life challenge shaped his development before his NFL career with the Steelers.

items.headline: Steelers LS Cal Adomitis is Pittsburgh native and All-American from Pitt
items.summary: Long snapper Cal Adomitis, born July 9, 1998, is a Pittsburgh native who attended Pittsburgh Central Catholic High School and played five seasons at the University of Pittsburgh. Named first-team All-American and won the Patrick Mannelly Award as the nation's top long snapper. Signed undrafted with Bengals in 2022 before returning to Pittsburgh.

items.headline: Steelers K Chris Boswell most accurate 50+ yard field goal kicker in NFL history
items.summary: Kicker Chris Boswell, born March 16, 1991, is a Fort Worth, Texas native who played college football at Rice. He has been with the Steelers since October 2015 and holds the record for most accurate field goal kicker in NFL history on 50+ yard attempts. He has 358 career points.

items.headline: Steelers DT Derrick Harmon drafted first round 2025, won Joe Greene Award
items.summary: Defensive tackle Derrick Harmon, born August 3, 2003, was drafted 21st overall by the Steelers in the 2025 NFL draft. He transferred from Michigan State to Oregon for his final season where he earned second-team All-American honors. Won the Joe Greene Great Performance Award as the team's top rookie.

items.headline: Steelers DT Logan Lee drafted 2024 from University of Iowa
items.summary: Defensive tackle Logan Lee, born June 12, 2000, played college football for the Iowa Hawkeyes, recording 158 tackles with 18.5 for loss and nine sacks in 43 games. Drafted by Pittsburgh in the sixth round (178th overall) in 2024. Made the 2025 roster after recovering from preseason calf injury.

items.headline: Steelers DE Esezi Otomewo signed after Jacksonville and Minnesota stints
items.summary: Defensive end Esezi Otomewo, born in Indianapolis, is 6'5" and 282 lbs. Fifth-round pick by Minnesota in 2022 from the University of Minnesota. Spent time with Jacksonville before signing with Pittsburgh in March 2025. Appeared in career-high 12 games for the Steelers in 2025.

items.headline: Steelers DE Yahya Black is fifth-round 2025 draft pick from Iowa
items.summary: Defensive end Yahya Black, born April 21, 2002, stands 6'5" and weighs 340 lbs. Drafted by Pittsburgh in the fifth round (164th overall) of 2025 from Iowa. Became a fan favorite in 2025 with 28 tackles and 2 forced fumbles. Started all 13 games in 2024 with 34 tackles and 1 sack.

items.headline: Steelers DE KJ Henry won Super Bowl LIX with Eagles
items.summary: Defensive end KJ Henry, full name Keith Jeremiah Henry, played college football for the Clemson Tigers and was drafted by Washington in the fifth round of 2023. Won a Super Bowl championship when the Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in Super Bowl LIX. Signed reserve/futures with Pittsburgh in January 2026.

items.headline: Steelers P Cameron Johnston is Australian punter from Melbourne Football Club
items.summary: Punter Cameron Johnston, from Geelong, Victoria, Australia, played college football at Ohio State. He previously played Australian rules football for the Melbourne Football Club. Signed with Pittsburgh on a one-year deal in March 2026, after spending time with Philadelphia, Houston, and other teams over his seven-year NFL career.

items.headline: Steelers LS Christian Kuntz played linebacker at Duquesne before long snapper role
items.summary: Long snapper Christian Kuntz, born April 13, 1994, played college football at Duquesne where he recorded a school record 30.5 sacks as a linebacker. Signed undrafted with the Patriots in 2017, then finally secured NFL job with Pittsburgh starting in 2021. Signed three-year contract with Steelers in March 2024.

items.headline: Steelers CB Donte Kent drafted seventh round 2025 from Central Michigan
items.summary: Cornerback Donte Kent, from Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, was drafted 229th overall (seventh round) by Pittsburgh in 2025 from Central Michigan. Played five seasons as cornerback and return specialist, recording 234 tackles and 47 pass breakups in 53 games. First Central Michigan cornerback drafted since 2019.

items.headline: Steelers DT Sebastian Joseph-Day signed two-year deal after Titans
items.summary: Defensive tackle Sebastian Joseph-Day, a Rutgers product, was drafted by Los Angeles in 2018 (195th overall). After eight NFL seasons with Rams, Chargers, 49ers, and Titans, he signed a two-year, $11M contract with Pittsburgh in March 2026. Career totals: 105 games, 315 tackles, 15.5 sacks.

items.headline: Steelers LB Julius Welschof is German international player from Michigan
items.summary: Linebacker Julius Welschof, born March 12, 1997, is a German professional player from Munich who played college football at Michigan and Charlotte. Signed as undrafted free agent using Pittsburgh's International Player Pathway Program spot. Worked hard to fund his journey to American football and earned scholarship.

items.headline: Steelers K Laith Marjan from Raleigh, two-time Lou Groza semifinalist
items.summary: Kicker Laith Marjan, from Raleigh, North Carolina, was signed undrafted as free agent following 2026 NFL Draft. Finished college at Kansas making 14 of 17 FGs and all 40 extra points, with school-record 14 consecutive FGs. Two-time Lou Groza semifinalist who made 42 of 44 extra points at South Alabama.

items.headline: Steelers DE Jahvaree Ritzie from North Carolina signed reserve/future
items.summary: Defensive end Jahvaree Ritzie played college football for North Carolina (not Georgia), appearing in 51 games with 138 tackles, 10.5 TFLs and 9 sacks. Signed as undrafted free agent by New England Patriots after 2025 draft, spent time on practice squad before signing reserve/future with Pittsburgh in January 2026.

items.headline: Steelers DE Gabriel Rubio is son of former Steelers nose tackle Angel Rubio
items.summary: Defensive end Gabriel Rubio, 6'0" 321 lbs, was drafted 210th overall in sixth round of 2026 NFL draft from Notre Dame. Son of former Steelers nose tackle Angel Rubio. Played in 39 games for Fighting Irish with 66 tackles, 1.5 sacks, and three passes defensed before suffering injury in final season.

items.headline: Steelers DE Anthony Goodlow signed future contract after Cardinals stint
items.summary: Defensive end Anthony Goodlow, born July 25, 2000, played college football at Tulsa and Oklahoma State. Signed as undrafted free agent with Rams in 2024 after going unselected in draft. Signed reserve/future with Pittsburgh in January 2026 after time with Arizona Cardinals.

items.headline: Steelers CB Daequan Hardy is local product from Penn Hills High School
items.summary: Cornerback Daequan Hardy, born June 13, 2001, played college football at Penn State in 48 games with 60 tackles, 20 passes defended and five interceptions. Drafted sixth round by Buffalo in 2024. Attended Penn Hills High School where he played on same 7-on-7 team as Steelers CB Joey Porter Jr. Signed to Pittsburgh practice squad November 2025.

items.headline: Steelers CB Doneiko Slaughter signed reserve/future after Jacksonville stint
items.summary: Cornerback Doneiko Slaughter, born November 8, 2001, played college football at Tennessee before transferring to Arkansas for his final season. Signed as undrafted free agent by Jacksonville in 2025 but was waived at final cutdowns. Pittsburgh signed him to reserve/future contract January 20, 2026.

items.headline: Steelers S Robert Spears-Jennings drafted seventh round from Oklahoma
items.summary: Safety Robert Spears-Jennings, born January 25, 2004, played college football for the Oklahoma Sooners with 178 tackles, 5 forced fumbles and 2 interceptions. Drafted 224th overall (seventh round) by Pittsburgh in 2026. Ran 4.32-second 40-yard dash with exceptional athleticism and hard-hitting run-stopping abilities.

items.headline: Steelers CB Daylen Everette drafted third round from Georgia
items.summary: Cornerback Daylen Everette, born May 6, 2004, was a three-year starter at Georgia, selected 85th overall (third round) in 2026. Led Georgia with 10 pass breakups and 50 tackles as third-team All-SEC honoree. Stands 6'1" 196 lbs with press-man coverage skills praised by scouts.

items.headline: Steelers S Jack Henderson transferred from Southeastern Louisiana to Minnesota
items.summary: Safety Jack Henderson, from Mandeville, Louisiana, played three seasons at Southeastern Louisiana before transferring to Minnesota. Started seven games for Gophers with 59 tackles, 5 TFLs, 2 interceptions. Drafted by Carolina, played for Tampa Bay and Miami before signing reserve/future with Pittsburgh January 2026.

items.headline: Steelers DB Devan Boykin won 2026 National Championship with Indiana
items.summary: Defensive back Devan Boykin, 5'10" 192 lbs, won the 2026 College Football Playoff National Championship with Indiana, recording 4 tackles in the 27-21 victory over Miami. Played for NC State and Indiana, versatile covering slot cornerback and safety. Signed as undrafted free agent by Pittsburgh in 2026.

items.headline: Seahawks Backup Quarterback
items.summary: Drew Lock joined the Seattle Seahawks as a backup quarterback in 2024, bringing NFL experience from previous stops with Denver, Chicago, and Kansas City.

items.headline: Father Was University of Missouri Football Player
items.summary: Drew Lock comes from a football family with his father having played collegiate football at the University of Missouri.

items.headline: Alabama Football Legacy
items.summary: Jalen Milroe was a starting quarterback for the University of Alabama Crimson Tide before joining the NFL, playing in the 2024 season as a rookie.

items.headline: University of Michigan RB
items.summary: Zach Charbonnet was a running back for the University of Michigan before being drafted by the Seattle Seahawks, competing for carries in the backfield.

items.headline: 2024 NFL Draft Selection
items.summary: Zach Charbonnet was selected by the Seahawks in the 2024 NFL Draft after a standout college career at Michigan.

items.headline: Seahawks Starting Left Tackle
items.summary: Charles Cross is the Seahawks' primary left tackle, a key offensive lineman protecting the quarterback's blind side as a first-round pick from Mississippi State.

items.headline: Mississippi State Offensive Tackle
items.summary: Charles Cross developed his offensive line skills at Mississippi State before being drafted by Seattle as a top pick.

items.headline: Young Cornerback Prospect
items.summary: Devon Witherspoon is a cornerback for the Seattle Seahawks selected early in the 2023 NFL Draft from the University of Illinois, part of the team's secondary rebuild.

items.headline: Illini Cornerback Development
items.summary: Devon Witherspoon developed his craft at the University of Illinois before being selected by Seattle in the early draft rounds.

items.headline: Seahawks Edge Rusher
items.summary: Uchenna Nwosu signed with the Seattle Seahawks as a pass rusher, bringing NFL experience from his previous tenure with the Los Angeles Chargers.

items.headline: NFL Free Agent Signing
items.summary: Uchenna Nwosu signed with Seattle after his tenure with the Los Angeles Chargers, adding pass rush help to the defense.

items.headline: Veteran Defensive Tackle
items.summary: Leonard Williams is a veteran defensive tackle anchoring the Seahawks' defensive line, with extensive NFL experience across multiple teams including the Giants and 49ers.

items.headline: Former San Francisco 49ers Defensive Tackle
items.summary: Leonard Williams spent time with the San Francisco 49ers before joining the Seahawks, strengthening Seattle's defensive interior.

items.headline: Cowboys Legacy Pass Rusher
items.summary: DeMarcus Lawrence signed with the Seahawks after a long tenure with the Dallas Cowboys as a premiere pass rusher, bringing star power to Seattle's defense.

items.headline: Multiple-Time Pro Bowler
items.summary: DeMarcus Lawrence was a multi-time Pro Bowler with the Dallas Cowboys before joining Seattle to add veteran pass rush expertise.

items.headline: First Australian-Born Punter in NFL
items.summary: Michael Dickson is a standout punter from Australia who made his mark with the Seattle Seahawks, becoming one of the league's premier special teams performers.

items.headline: Signed Long-Term Extension with Seattle
items.summary: Michael Dickson signed a long-term extension with the Seahawks, securing his role as the team's premier special teams asset.

items.headline: Reliable Seahawks Kicker
items.summary: Jason Myers is the Seahawks' placekicker, handling field goals and extra points for the team in recent seasons after signing with Seattle.

items.headline: 1st round 2024 draft pick: Center Graham Barton selected 26th overall
items.summary: Graham Barton was drafted 26th overall in the first round of the 2024 NFL draft by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, becoming the first center selected that year. Born June 1, 2002, in Brentwood, Tennessee, he majored in Public Policy and signed a four-year fully guaranteed contract worth $14.02 million with a $7.02 million signing bonus.

items.headline: Re-signed to 1-year deal by Buccaneers in March 2026
items.summary: Connor Bazelak signed a one-year contract worth approximately $1 million with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers on March 4, 2026. He was elevated from the practice squad multiple times during the 2025 season but did not appear in a game. Bazelak competed at the University of Missouri, Indiana University, and Bowling Green State University during his college career.

items.headline: Signed 3-year, $22M extension with Buccaneers in 2025
items.summary: Ben Bredeson signed a three-year, $22 million contract extension with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in 2025. Standing 6'5" and weighing 315 pounds, he started all 17 games as left guard in his first season with the team in 2024. Originally drafted 143rd overall in 2020 by the Baltimore Ravens, he spent time with the New York Giants before joining Tampa Bay.

items.headline: Signed to 1-year deal as backup QB in March 2026
items.summary: Jake Browning signed a one-year, $1.3 million contract with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers on March 13, 2026, to serve as backup to starting quarterback Baker Mayfield. The former Washington Huskies quarterback spent five seasons with the Cincinnati Bengals behind Joe Burrow before joining Tampa Bay.

items.headline: Nigerian-born OT never played football before moving to US at age 17
items.summary: Benjamin Chukwuma was born in Nigeria and moved to the United States at age 17 in 2017, having never played football before. After getting a spot on Georgia State's team through an open tryout in 2020, he worked his way up to become a starting left tackle by 2024. Signed by the Buccaneers as an undrafted free agent in May 2025 with $300,000 guaranteed, the largest for any 2025 UDFA, he made the 53-man roster and made his first career start in Week 9.

items.headline: 7th round 2024 draft pick from University of Washington
items.summary: Devin Culp was selected by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the seventh round (246th overall) of the 2024 NFL draft. The tight end from Spokane, Washington, played college football at the University of Washington where he shared an offense with highly-drafted wide receivers. He ran the fourth-fastest 40-yard dash by a tight end since 2003 at the NFL Combine with a 4.47-second time.

items.headline: Kansas QB signed as undrafted free agent in April 2026
items.summary: Jalon Daniels signed with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers as an undrafted free agent following the 2026 NFL draft, securing $247K guaranteed and a $25K signing bonus. The Kansas Jayhawks QB earned second-team All-Big 12 honors in 2022 and compiled 9,282 passing yards and 67 touchdowns with 23 rushing touchdowns across his college career, showcasing true dual-threat ability.

items.headline: 5th round 2023 draft pick from Purdue
items.summary: Payne Durham was selected by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the fifth round (171st overall) of the 2023 NFL draft from Purdue University. At 6-5 and 253 pounds, he can line up inline or split out in the slot at tight end position. In his rookie season, he appeared in 13 games with two starts, recording five receptions for 58 yards.

items.headline: 1st round 2025 draft pick: 19th overall WR from Ohio State
items.summary: Emeka Egbuka was selected 19th overall in the first round by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the 2025 NFL draft. Born October 14, 2002, at 6'1" and 205 lbs, he finished as Ohio State's career receptions leader with 205 catches during his 2024 senior season. Made an immediate impact as a rookie, earning NFL Offensive Rookie of the Month for September after scoring game-winning TD in Week 1.

items.headline: 9-year veteran re-signed to one-year deal in March 2026
items.summary: Dan Feeney re-signed with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers on March 16, 2026. The nine-year NFL veteran was originally drafted 143rd overall by the Los Angeles Chargers in 2017. He played in 12 games during the 2025 season and started the final 10 games at right guard. Feeney has also played for the Jets, Dolphins, Bears, and Vikings before returning to Tampa Bay.

items.headline: Signed 2-year deal with Buccaneers as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Marshall Foerner, a 6-6, 308-pound offensive tackle, signed a 2-year contract with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers worth $1.935 million with an average annual salary of $967,500. The undrafted prospect from Minnesota State-Mankato, born December 31, 2000, was signed by the Colts before joining Tampa Bay as a developmental camp body.

items.headline: Signed 2-year, $14M deal in March 2026 free agency
items.summary: Kenneth Gainwell signed a two-year, $14 million contract with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers on March 13, 2026, after earning team MVP honors with the Pittsburgh Steelers. The Memphis Tigers product led the Steelers with 73 receptions on 85 targets for 1,023 yards from scrimmage with 8 touchdowns. He joins Bucky Irving in the Buccaneers' running back room.

items.headline: Changed jersey name to 'Godwin Jr.' in June 2025 to honor late father
items.summary: On June 12, 2025, Chris Godwin announced he would change his jersey name to 'Godwin Jr.' to honor his late father. The Super Bowl LV champion signed a three-year, $66 million extension with the Buccaneers in March 2025 after 8 seasons with the team. Godwin finished the 2025 season with 33 receptions for 360 yards and two touchdowns.

items.headline: Signed 4-year, $90M extension in 2026 (became highest-paid OT)
items.summary: Luke Goedeke agreed to a four-year, $90 million extension with the Buccaneers with $50 million guaranteed, making him one of the highest-paid offensive tackles in the NFL. Originally drafted 57th overall in 2022, the Wisconsin-Stevens Point/Central Michigan product earned a 75.8 PFF grade in 2025, ranked 23rd among qualified offensive tackles.

items.headline: College QB switched to WR as pro, 5'10" 202 lbs
items.summary: Garrett Greene, a 5'10" 202-pound former West Virginia quarterback, signed with the Buccaneers as an undrafted free agent on May 9, 2025, to try converting to wide receiver. His undersized frame for QB position led to the position change. He was waived before the season but re-signed to the practice squad, catching 3 targets for 18 yards and a touchdown in preseason.

items.headline: First Sonoma County player to suit up in NFL since 2017
items.summary: Luke Haggard, born January 3, 2000, from Petaluma, California, became the first Sonoma County player to suit up in an NFL game since 2017. After going unselected in 2023, he signed with the Buccaneers and spent the entire 2024 season on the practice squad before finally making his NFL debut in Week 3, 2026, as the starting right guard. He has a 2-year contract worth $1.845 million.

items.headline: UDFA who bounced between teams before landing with Bucs in 2024
items.summary: Dennis Houston, born April 24, 1999, signed with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers' practice squad on October 23, 2024. The Houston Baptist/Fullerton/Western Illinois product initially signed with Dallas as an UDFA in 2022, then moved to NY Giants practice squad in 2023 before joining Tampa Bay. Spent the 2025 season on Bucs practice squad without appearing in games.

items.headline: 3rd round 2026 draft pick (84th overall) from Georgia State
items.summary: Ted Morris Hurst III (born July 2, 2004) was selected by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the third round (84th overall) of the 2026 NFL draft. The 6-4, 210-pound wide receiver from Georgia State ran a 4.42-second 40-yard dash at the Scouting Combine. As a senior, he had 71 receptions for 1,004 yards and 6 touchdowns after earning school records for receiving yards and TDs.

items.headline: First player from Barton College ever to sign with NFL team
items.summary: Kameron Johnson, born March 17, 2002, became the first-ever player from Barton College to sign with an NFL team when he joined the Buccaneers as an undrafted free agent in 2024. The undersized WR/return specialist had a 54-yard punt return for a TD setup in Week 1 of 2025 and caught a 34-yard TD pass from Baker Mayfield against SF in Week 6.

items.headline: 7th round 2025 draft pick from Oregon (235th overall)
items.summary: Tez Johnson was drafted by the Buccaneers in the seventh round (235th overall) of the 2025 NFL draft. The former Troy/Oregon wide receiver finished his college career with 3,889 receiving yards, 28 TDs and 310 receptions. Named Big Ten Championship game MVP and Second-team All-Big Ten in his final year at Oregon. Scored first career TD on 45-yard reception in Week 6 vs SF.

items.headline: Placed on injured reserve with season-ending leg injury in Week 3
items.summary: Ko Kieft, a 5th-year tight end from Minnesota drafted in 6th round (218th overall) in 2022, was placed on injured reserve following a season-ending leg injury in Week 3 against the Jets. The blocking TE was re-signed in 2026. Over his first 3 seasons, he appeared in 53 of 54 possible regular-season games with just 8 receptions but was a special teams stalwart on approximately 70% of snaps.

items.headline: 6th round 2024 draft pick from UTEP (220th overall)
items.summary: Elijah Blue Klein was selected in the sixth round (220th overall) of the 2024 NFL draft from UTEP. Named first-team All-Conference USA in 2023 after starting 12 games at right guard. Can play both guard and tackle positions. The Buccaneers value his versatility and ability to contribute at multiple positions on the offensive line.

items.headline: Suffered season-ending torn meniscus in Week 2 (2025)
items.summary: Cody Mauch, drafted 48th overall in 2023 from North Dakota State, started all 17 games in 2023 and 2024 seasons at right guard before suffering a torn meniscus in Week 2 of 2025. Mauch confirmed the rehab is progressing well and expects to be ready for 2026 offseason activities. He remains an important part of the Buccaneers' offensive line.

items.headline: Suffered broken neck injury in October 2025, activated from IR December 2025
items.summary: Jalen McMillan, drafted 92nd overall in 2024 from Washington, suffered a broken neck as a result of a fall in late October 2025. Head coach Todd Bowles expressed confidence in McMillan's future, saying there is bright football ahead. McMillan was activated from injured reserve on December 10, 2025, after showing strong rookie form with 37 catches for 461 yards and 8 TDs in 2024.

items.headline: Son of SMU/Arkansas/Clemson coach Chad Morris
items.summary: Chandler Morris, 6'0" 190 lbs, signed as an undrafted free agent with the Buccaneers following the 2026 draft. Son of current Clemson offensive coordinator Chad Morris (former SMU and Arkansas head coach), Chandler is a journeyman who played for Oklahoma, TCU, North Texas, and Virginia. Had 9,207 passing yards, 63 TDs and 15 rushing TDs across 6 college seasons.

items.headline: Georgia Tech WR with elite 4.35 40-time, converted from DB
items.summary: Eric Rivers signed with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers as an undrafted free agent after the 2026 draft. The Georgia Tech WR originally played defensive back at Memphis before converting to WR at FIU, where he had 1,172 yards and 12 TDs in 2024 and became the school's first All-American. At 4.35 40-time, he has blazing speed. Contract: 3 years, $3.125M with $25K signing bonus.

items.headline: 6'10" Frankfurt-born UCF tackle with rare athleticism
items.summary: Paul Rubelt, a 6'10" 313-pound German-born offensive tackle from UCF, signed with the Buccaneers as an undrafted free agent. The Frankfurt-Oder native spent six seasons at UCF, appearing in 60 total games with 60 starts. His 4.99 40-yard dash time is incredible for his size, and his 9'8" broad jump was the second-best among linemen at the Combine. Contract: 3 years, $3.13M.

items.headline: 5th round 2026 draft pick from Notre Dame (160th overall)
items.summary: Billy Schrauth, 6'5" 310-pound guard from Notre Dame, was drafted by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the fifth round (160th overall) of the 2026 NFL draft. Served as team captain at Notre Dame with a powerful upper body and strong hands. However, missed rest of 2025 season after starting first 7 games with a left knee injury. The Bucs value his road-grading blocking and believe he can compete for backup roles.

items.headline: Nebraska center signed as college free agent in May 2025
items.summary: Ben Scott, 6'5" 310 lbs, signed with the Buccaneers as a college free agent on May 9, 2025. The Nebraska center appeared in 51 career games (50 starts) between UMass (2018-21) and Arizona State (2020-22), primarily playing center. Started all 13 games at center for Nebraska in 2024. Signed 3-year contract worth $2.98M but was waived and later signed to practice squad.

items.headline: 6th round 2026 draft pick from LSU (185th overall), former QB
items.summary: Bauer Sharp (born May 3, 2003), 6'5" 249-pound tight end from LSU, was drafted by the Buccaneers in the 6th round (185th overall) of the 2026 draft after trading up 10 spots from 195. Former quarterback at Southeastern Louisiana and Oklahoma before transferring to LSU. In his lone LSU season, he caught 24 passes for 252 yards and 2 TDs, bringing speed and pass-catching skills to the tight end room.

items.headline: Army WR/dual-threat, first from Army to sign with NFL, 1,000+ rush AND receiving yards
items.summary: Noah Short, an Army West Point slotback, became the first player in Army history to log career totals of 1,000+ rushing yards (1,148 at 6.5 avg) and 1,000+ receiving yards (1,008 at 15.0 avg). Signed with Buccaneers as UDFA following 2026 draft. Dual-threat weapon with 10 total TDs. 6'0" 195 lbs. Contract: 3 years, $3.1M with $1K signing bonus.

items.headline: Former Falcons WR converted from QB, signed by Bucs in April 2026
items.summary: David Sills V, 6'3" 211-pound wide receiver, signed a one-year contract with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in April 2026. Most recently with the Atlanta Falcons in 2025, appearing in all 17 games with 4 starts, recording 18 receptions for 191 yards and 2 TDs. Converted from quarterback at West Virginia. OC Zac Robinson worked with him at Atlanta and knows his skillset.

items.headline: 6'6" 315 lb OT returns to Buccaneers on 1-year deal in 2026
items.summary: Justin Skule, 6'6" 315-pound offensive tackle from Vanderbilt, re-signed with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in 2026 after previously playing for the team from 2022-2024. Originally drafted 6th round by San Francisco in 2019. Played all 17 games for Tampa Bay in 2024 as a swing tackle. A familiar face brought back to shore up offensive line depth.

items.headline: Re-signed on 1-year, $3.52M deal in April 2026 after 8 TDs in 2025
items.summary: Sean Tucker, undrafted free agent signing from Syracuse in 2023, was officially re-signed to a one-year, $3.52 million contract with the Buccaneers on April 20, 2026. Produced team-leading 8 touchdowns in 2025. In Week 6 vs New Orleans in 2024, rushed for 136 yards + TD plus 3 catches for 56 yards + TD, earning NFC Offensive Player of the Week.

items.headline: Arizona RB bounced between practice squads before brief Bucs stint
items.summary: Michael Wiley, who played college football for Arizona Wildcats, signed with the Washington Commanders as an undrafted free agent in 2024. Spent time on practice squads for Washington and later the Tampa Bay Buccaneers (signed October 14, 2025). Released December 30, 2025. Never elevated to active roster during his brief time in Tampa Bay.

items.headline: LSU RB suspended 6 games for PED violation, signed futures contract
items.summary: Josh Williams (born June 9, 2001), LSU running back and member of school's 2019 national championship team, was suspended 6 games without pay for violating NFL's performance-enhancing substances policy. Signed as college free agent May 9, 2025. Career: 314 carries for 1,494 yards and 17 TDs at LSU across 60 games. Signed futures contract January 8, 2026.

items.headline: William & Mary/Monmouth RB promoted to 53-man roster during backfield injuries
items.summary: Owen Wright, who played college football at William & Mary and Monmouth, was originally signed as an undrafted free agent by Baltimore Ravens in 2023. Signed reserve/future contract with Tampa Bay January 29, 2024, then re-signed July 31, 2025. Promoted to active roster while the team dealt with backfield absences after Josh Williams was placed on reserve/suspended.

items.headline: Signed 2-year, $17M deal in March 2026 after 5 years in Detroit
items.summary: Alex Anzalone (born September 22, 1994), Florida Gators linebacker, signed a two-year, $17 million contract with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers on March 12, 2026. Spent previous 5 years with Detroit Lions, accumulating 490 tackles, 9 sacks, 33 passes defensed and 3 INTs. Originally drafted 3rd round by New Orleans in 2017. Returns to Tampa where he spends every offseason with his family.

items.headline: 1st round 2026 pick (15th overall) from Miami, coached by NFL Hall of Famer
items.summary: Rueben Ellis Bain Jr. (born September 8, 2004), Miami Hurricanes linebacker, was selected 15th overall by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the 2026 NFL draft. Earned first-team All-ACC honors in 2025. Trained by NFL Hall of Famer Jason Taylor, who served as his defensive line coach at Miami. Buccaneers graded him as a top-5 overall player. Wears number 3.

items.headline: Safety from Alabama/Mississippi State signed to practice squad in 2025
items.summary: Marcus Banks, Alabama/Mississippi State safety, signed with Tampa Bay Buccaneers as undrafted free agent but was released before 2024 season and signed to practice squad. Remained on practice squad throughout 2024 season and re-signed on futures contract. Elevated multiple times in November-December 2025, playing special teams against Rams and Cardinals.

items.headline: 2nd round 2024 draft pick from Alabama (57th overall)
items.summary: Chris Braswell, Alabama linebacker (born October 25, 2001), was selected 57th overall in the 2024 NFL draft. As a junior at Alabama, had career highs with 42 tackles, 8.0 sacks, and 3 forced fumbles. Led SEC in QB pressures and forced fumbles. As a rookie in 2025, started in limited capacity and showed promise after offseason weight loss improved his quickness.

items.headline: 5th round 2026 pick from Clemson (155th overall), profiles as Week 1 contributor
items.summary: DeMonte Capehart, 6'5" 313-pound defensive tackle from Clemson, drafted 155th overall in 5th round of 2026 by Buccaneers. Spent 6 years at Clemson with 72 combined tackles, 13.5 TFLs, 3 sacks over 57 games. Compared to Will Gholston, 9th most athletic DT since 1987. VP Mike Biehl sees him as run stopper to strengthen already strong Bucs run defense.

items.headline: Returns from shoulder surgery in 2026 after missing 2024 season
items.summary: SirVocea Levitticus Dennis (born March 9, 2000), Pittsburgh linebacker, was selected 153rd overall in 5th round of 2023 draft. Missed most of 2024 after shoulder surgery, correcting long-standing injury. Resumed practice as MIKE linebacker in 2025 offseason. Team captain at Pitt with 94 tackles and 9 sacks as senior, named 3rd-team All-American. Now healthy competing at Mike position.

items.headline: LB with unique path: no scholarship, worked at Atlanta airport before NFL
items.summary: Yaya Diaby (6'4" 270 lbs), 3rd round 2023 pick (82nd overall), worked as wheelchair assistant at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport due to lack of scholarship offers coming out of high school. Started all 17 games for Bucs in 2024. In 2025, recorded 50 tackles (29 solo), 7.0 sacks, 1 forced fumble in 17 games.

items.headline: NC State LB signed as UDFA after leading nation in 2025 tackles
items.summary: Caden Fordham, NC State linebacker, signed as an undrafted free agent with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers following the 2026 draft. Finished with 143 tackles in 2025 — 2nd-highest in the nation and most among Power Four players. Added 10 TFLs, 3.5 sacks, 3 PBUs, 2 INTs. Named Third Team AP All-American. Just fifth linebacker in NC State history to earn All-American honors.

items.headline: Georgia Tech CB signed as UDFA, sticky coverage player
items.summary: Jy Gilmore signed as an undrafted free agent with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in April 2026. Former Georgia Tech cornerback from Georgia State, became starting nickelback for Yellow Jackets. PFF gave him 66.0 overall grade in 414 snaps including 77.5 pass rushing grade. Sticky coverage with good timing on pass rush. Joined former teammates Eric Rivers and Dean Patterson in Tampa Bay.

items.headline: Signed 1-year deal April 2026, veteran with special teams experience
items.summary: Kemon Hall (5'11" 202 lbs), North Texas cornerback, signed a one-year contract with the Buccaneers on April 10, 2026. Undrafted free agent by LA Chargers in 2019, he has played for Chargers, Vikings, Saints, Cowboys, 49ers, and Titans from 2019-2025. More of a nickel CB than outside corner. Built reputation on special teams with 500+ snaps and 14 tackles.

items.headline: Nephew of former Buccaneers LB Geno Hayes, nephew connection
items.summary: Josh Hayes, originally from Lakeland, Florida, was selected 181st overall in 6th round of 2023 draft from North Dakota State. As a junior at NDSU, started all 16 games recording 59 tackles, 10 PDs, 2 INTs, 1 forced fumble. Won FCS National Championship in 2019. Is the nephew of former Buccaneers linebacker Geno Hayes. Led NFL in special teams tackles in 2023 with 14 (most by Buccaneer since 2014).

items.headline: Nebraska DT signed as UDFA, four-time Academic All-Big Ten
items.summary: Nash Hutmacher, defensive tackle from Nebraska/Chamberlain, South Dakota, signed with Buccaneers as UDFA. Majored in management and was four-time Academic All-Big Ten selection and eight-time Nebraska Scholar-Athlete Honor Roll member. Also competed in wrestling after 2023, qualifying for NCAA Championships at heavyweight with 7-8 record, earning Academic All-America honors. 2-year contract: $1,935,000.

items.headline: Former Iowa LB with NCAA record 73 games played, most tackles by current Buccaneer
items.summary: Nick Jackson (born January 5, 2001), Virginia/Iowa linebacker, signed with Buccaneers as UDFA after 2025 draft. 73 career games played — NCAA record, 555 career tackles — 2nd-most in FBS history. After preseason sack and interception, became first Buccaneer since 2017 to record both in same preseason game. Signed futures contract January 8, 2026.

items.headline: Auburn DT signed as UDFA, true nose tackle with run-stuffing ability
items.summary: Jayson Jones (born September 21, 2001), 6'6" 325-pound Auburn defensive tackle, signed with Buccaneers as UDFA. True nose tackle with 13.7% stop rate in preseason but limited pass rush (1.5 career sacks). Spent most of 2025 on practice squad injured list. Developmental defensive line prospect looking to make 2026 roster.

items.headline: Colorado State LB, 5th round 2024 pick by Dolphins, signed to Bucs practice squad
items.summary: Mohamed Kamara (6'1" 253 lbs, age 25), Colorado State linebacker, selected 5th round by Miami Dolphins in 2024 draft. Signed to Buccaneers practice squad August 27, 2025, and later signed reserve/futures contract January 8, 2026. Younger brother of former Temple linebacker Amara Kamara.

items.headline: 1st round 2023 pick, suffered torn pectoral in Week 2, 5-year option exercised
items.summary: Calijah Kancey (born March 1, 2001), Pittsburgh 2022 ACC DPOY, selected 19th overall in 2023 draft. Named Defensive Rookie of the Month November 2023. Injuries plagued his tenure: calf issues (twice) and torn pectoral muscle. Through 3 seasons, 29 games (22 missed). Buccaneers exercised his fifth-year option, keeping him through 2027. Career: 11.5 sacks, 93 pressures.

items.headline: All-Pro special teamer tore ACL in Week 6 2025, re-signed March 2026
items.summary: Miles Killebrew, 10-year NFL veteran (Detroit Lions, Pittsburgh Steelers), signed a one-year, $1.79 million contract with Buccaneers on March 13, 2026. 2023-24 consecutive Pro Bowl selections as special teamer, 2023 AP First-Team All-Pro special teamer. Leads all active players with 4 career blocked punts. Tore ACL Week 6 2025 but expected to be ready for 2026 season. Reunites with ST coordinator Danny Smith.

items.headline: Ex-49ers CB signed to 1-year deal April 2026 for defensive depth
items.summary: Chase Lucas, Arizona State cornerback, signed a one-year contract with the Buccaneers on April 10, 2026. Originally drafted 7th round (237th overall) by Detroit Lions in 2022. Played for 49ers in 2025 after becoming restricted free agent who went untendered. In 15 games, had 9 tackles, 1 PD, 1 QB hit. Played 53% special teams snaps (204) and 10% defensive snaps (98).

items.headline: Signed 3-year, $48M extension September 2025, had difficult 2025 season
items.summary: Zyon McCollum (born May 3, 1999), Sam Houston State cornerback, selected 5th round (140th overall) of 2022 draft. Signed three-year, $48 million extension with $35.4M guaranteed on September 5, 2025. Exceptionally strong 2024 season: 82 combined tackles (55 solo), career-high 17 PDs, 2 INTs, 1 fumble recovery. However, struggled in 2025: missed 5 games, ended season on injured reserve.

items.headline: Arizona DT signed as UDFA, undersized but good pass-rush ability
items.summary: Deshawn McKnight, Arizona defensive tackle, signed with Buccaneers as UDFA following 2026 draft. 6'3" 285 lbs with good twitch and pass-rush ability per VP Mike Biehl. In 2025 at Arizona, had 26 tackles with team-leading 11.0 TFLs and 2.0 sacks. Undersized but will compete for defensive line depth role. Former Sumter High standout (South Carolina).

items.headline: 2nd round 2025 pick from Notre Dame (53rd overall), father played in NFL
items.summary: Benjamin James Morrison (born March 11, 2004), Notre Dame cornerback, drafted 53rd overall in 2nd round by Buccaneers in 2025. Named preseason All-American by NBCSports, FWAA, ESPN. Son of Darryl Morrison, who played in the NFL. 2025 season: 47.0 overall PFF grade (110th among 114 qualified CBs), 50.0 coverage grade (100th).

items.headline: Signed 1-year, $6M deal March 2026 after 11 sacks with Lions in 2025
items.summary: Al-Quadin Muhammad, Miami Hurricanes edge rusher, signed a one-year contract worth up to $6 million with Buccaneers on March 12, 2026. Originally drafted 6th round by Saints in 2017. Had career-best 2025 season with Detroit Lions: 11 sacks, 9 TFLs, 20 QB hits. Teams up with former Lions teammate Alex Anzalone on the Buccaneers defense.

items.headline: Nigerian-born nose tackle via International Player Pathway program
items.summary: Haggai Chisom Ndubuisi, born October 15, 2000, in Lagos, Nigeria, signed a one-year contract with Buccaneers on April 10, 2026. Never played college football, discovered NFL through YouTube video at age 18. Joined NFL through International Player Pathway and has played for Cardinals, Broncos, Commanders, Patriots, and San Antonio Brahmas before Texans practice squad stint.

items.headline: Named NFC Defensive Player of Week for 2 sacks, INT TD vs New Orleans
items.summary: Anthony Nelson, Iowa edge rusher, drafted 4th round (107th overall) in 2019 by Buccaneers. Super Bowl LV champion with 4 tackles in postseason. Signed 2-year, $10 million contract March 11, 2025. In Week 8 vs New Orleans 2025, named NFC Defensive Player of Week with 2 sacks, interception returned for TD, forced fumble. 2024 was arguably his best season with 6 starts, 4 sacks, 6 TFLs, career-high 13 QB hits.

items.headline: Belize DT returns to Buccaneers after 3 years away
items.summary: Rakeem Nathan Nuñez-Roches (born July 3, 1993), Belizean/Southern Mississippi defensive end, signed a one-year contract with Buccaneers on April 7, 2026. Re-signed after playing 5 seasons in Tampa and starting in Super Bowl LV. Spent past 3 years with NY Giants (9 games). Across first 5 Buccaneers seasons: 68 games, 22 starts, 81 tackles, 2 sacks, 1 FF, 1 FR, 9 TFLs, 8 QB hits.

items.headline: Rookie 2025: Made All-Rookie Team, one of two Bucs earned selection
items.summary: Jacob Parrish, Kansas State cornerback, drafted 84th overall in 3rd round of 2025 by Buccaneers. Made 2025 All-Rookie Team alongside one other Buccaneer. Owned nickel role in 2025 with 615 snaps in slot and 127 snaps outside. Career stat line: 76 combined tackles, 7 PDs, 2 INTs, 2 sacks, 7 TFLs, 3 QB hits.

items.headline: LSU DE signed as UDFA after one season (transferred from Florida)
items.summary: Jack Pyburn, former Florida/LSU defensive end, signed with Buccaneers as UDFA following 2026 draft. Dane Brugler gave him a sixth-round grade. Good run defender with LSU, playing one season in Baton Rouge after transferring from Florida. 3-year contract: $3.115M with $15K signing bonus, $115K guaranteed, $1.038M average annual salary.

items.headline: 5th round 2025 pick from SMU/Miami, shifted to interior line
items.summary: Elijah Roberts, Miami/SMU defensive end, selected 5th round (157th overall) in 2025 draft. Played DE in college at SMU (2023-24) with 111 pressures and 17 sacks. Buccaneers project him as interior DT at pro level. When starter Calijah Kancey went down Week 2 with torn pectoral, Roberts stepped up with 63 pass-rushing grade, 2 sacks, 27 pressures. Struggled against the run (111th of 134 DLs).

items.headline: Promising UDFA safety suffered knee injury in training camp 2025
items.summary: J.J. Roberts (born September 22, 2001), Wake Forest/Marshall safety, signed as UDFA with Buccaneers May 9, 2025. In 2024, led all college players with 14 passes defensed (tied), adding 94 tackles. Suffered knee injury during joint practice with Steelers in Pittsburgh, missing entire rookie season. Received $300K guaranteed as UDFA, indicating high team valuation.

items.headline: Signed 1-year, $10M deal March 2026 after career year with Panthers
items.summary: A'Shawn Robinson, Alabama DT, signed a one-year, $10 million contract with Buccaneers on March 11, 2026. 2nd round 2016 draft pick by Detroit Lions. Super Bowl LVI winner with LA Rams. Had career year in 2024 with 80 tackles, 5.5 sacks. In 2025: 65 tackles, 2.5 sacks. Also played for Giants and Panthers before Tampa Bay.

items.headline: Signed 1-year, $1.55M deal March 2026 after 122 tackles with Panthers
items.summary: Christian Rozeboom (born January 30, 1997), South Dakota State linebacker, signed one-year, $1.55 million contract with Buccaneers on March 24, 2026. Super Bowl LVI champion with LA Rams. In 2025 with Carolina Panthers (his best season), started 15 games with team-leading 122 tackles, 2 sacks, 1 FF, 3 PD, 1 INT.

items.headline: 4th round 2026 pick from Miami, pressure player at nickel position
items.summary: Keionte Scott, Miami cornerback, drafted 4th round (116th overall) by Buccaneers in 2026. Snow Badgers captain, twice named junior college All-American. At Miami in 2025, recorded 64 tackles (13 TFL), 5 sacks, 2 TDs (both returned for TDs), 2 FFs, 5 PBU in 14 games. Earned All-ACC Second Team honors. Described as 'pressure player from nickel position' by Licht.

items.headline: Tennessee DT signed from Cardinals practice squad September 2025
items.summary: Elijah Simmons (born July 26, 2001), 6'1" 334-pound Tennessee nose tackle, signed to Buccaneers active roster on September 17, 2025, from Arizona Cardinals practice squad. Undrafted UDFA signing by Arizona April 28, 2025. In 2024 at Tennessee, appeared in all 13 games with 2 starts, 11 tackles, 1.5 TFLs. Brings size, strength and run-stopping power to Bucs defense.

items.headline: Transitioned from slot CB to full-time safety in 2025 after strong rookie year
items.summary: Tykee Smith (born December 2002), West Virginia/Georgia safety, selected 3rd round (89th overall) of 2024 draft. As rookie, competed at slot corner and won job with 2 INTs, 3 FF, 7 PD, 4 TFLs, 2 QB hits. In 2025, moved to full-time deep safety opposite Antoine Winfield Jr., playing 893 snaps. Led NFL safeties with 13 PD and ranked 5th with 26.3 hawk percentage.

items.headline: Son of former Eagles LB Jeremiah Trotter, 2nd round 2026 pick (46th overall)
items.summary: Josiah Trotter (born April 15, 2005), West Virginia/Missouri linebacker, drafted 2nd round (46th overall) by Buccaneers in 2026. Son of NFL Hall of Famer Jeremiah Trotter (11-year Eagles career, 4 Pro Bowls). Transferred to Missouri for 2025 and earned first-team All-SEC honors with 84 tackles, 13 TFLs, 2 sacks. Brother of current NFL LB Jeremiah Trotter Jr.

items.headline: Central Arkansas OLB suffered torn ACL in camp 2025, activated February 2026
items.summary: David Walker (born 1995), Central Arkansas outside linebacker, selected 4th round (121st overall) in 2025 draft. Three-time consensus All-American with 191 career tackles and 31 sacks. 2024 Buck Buchanan Award winner (top FCS defensive player). Suffered torn ACL during Friday training camp practice 2025, missing entire season. Activated from IR February 15, 2026.

items.headline: Holy Cross LB bounced between teams before joining Bucs practice squad 2025
items.summary: Benton Whitley (born February 12, 1999), 6'4" 255-pound Holy Cross linebacker, signed to Buccaneers practice squad November 12, 2025. Springfield, Massachusetts native. Career: undrafted by LA Rams 2022, moved to Vikings, Giants, Cardinals, then Buccaneers. Through 4 NFL seasons (2022-2025), has appeared in just 6 games with no starts or statistical production.

items.headline: Houston CB bounced to practice squads before joining Bucs September 2025
items.summary: Damarion Williams (5'10" 183 lbs), Highland CC/Houston cornerback, selected 4th round (141st overall) by Baltimore Ravens in 2022. Transferred from Highland CC to Houston after NJCAA All-America First Team honors. Signed Seahawks reserve/future January 6, 2025, then signed to Buccaneers practice squad September 30, 2025.

items.headline: Won 2026 Pro Bowl MVP, highest-paid defensive back after $84M contract
items.summary: Antoine Duane Winfield Jr. (born August 16, 1998), Minnesota Gophers safety, selected 2nd round (45th overall) by Buccaneers in 2020. Super Bowl LV starter with 6 tackles and interception off Patrick Mahomes. Signed 4-year, $84 million deal making him highest-paid defensive back in NFL history. Most sacks by DB since 2020. 2026 Pro Bowl MVP after NFC defeated AFC 66-52.

items.headline: UTSA safety signed as UDFA 2024, made 53-man roster before injury
items.summary: Rashad Wisdom, UTSA Roadrunners safety, signed with Buccaneers as UDFA on May 10, 2024. Made the team's 53-man roster for 2025 season, impressing with fast, attacking physical style. However, placed on injured reserve September 30 with quadriceps injury. Activated December 17 ahead of Week 16 vs Carolina Panthers. Recorded 2 tackles and 25 special teams snaps before injury.

items.headline: Nebraska LB overcame recurring blood clots to sign as UDFA
items.summary: Javin Wright (6'5" 230 lbs), Nebraska linebacker, signed as UDFA with Buccaneers following 2026 draft. Seven-season college career (2019-25) at Nebraska. In 2025, recorded 86 tackles (9 TFL), 3 sacks, 4 PBU. Overcame injury history including recurring blood clots that were discussed during pre-draft process. From Chandler, Arizona.

items.headline: Duke/UMass long snapper signed as UDFA 2023, beat out competition
items.summary: Evan Deckers (born February 23, 1999), UMass/Duke long snapper from Avon, Connecticut, signed with Buccaneers as UDFA 2023. Appeared in 53 career games (50 starts) between UMass (2018-21) and Duke (2022). Beat out Triner for team's long snapper job in 2024. Suffered hamstring injury Week 3 and placed on IR. Buccaneers re-signed him as key special teams contributor.

items.headline: Signed 2-year, $6M deal March 2025, rookie 40.7 avg NFL record
items.summary: Riley Dixon, Syracuse punter, signed a two-year, $6 million contract with Buccaneers on March 13, 2025. Drafted 7th round (2016) by Denver Broncos. In 2016 rookie year, punted 89 times for 41.3 net average — highest by an NFL rookie ever — named All-Rookie Team. 2025 season: earned NFC Special Teams Player of Week 13 with strong performance. 2026 contract option picked up.

items.headline: Set NFL record with 11 straight 50+ yard FG makes in 2025 season
items.summary: Chase McLaughlin, kicker drafted 7th round 2018, signed with Buccaneers March 30, 2023. Became Bucs kicker in 2023 after Rodrigo Blankenship release. Extended March 11, 2024 (3 years, $12.3M). Made franchise record 65-yard FG in Week 4 vs Eagles. First player in NFL history to make 11 consecutive 50+ yard FG attempts in a single season. 26 successful 50+ yard FGs (Bucs team record by far).

items.headline: 2022 SEC Player of the Year
items.summary: Hendon Hooker was named SEC Player of the Year in 2022 while playing for Tennessee Volunteers. He led the team to a College Football Playoff appearance.

items.headline: 2024 NFL Draft top QB prospect
items.summary: Cam Ward was a top quarterback prospect in the 2024 NFL Draft, known for his athletic ability and arm strength.

items.headline: 2nd overall pick in 2017 NFL Draft
items.summary: Mitchell Trubisky was drafted 2nd overall by the Chicago Bears in 2017 and has had a career as a backup and starter across multiple NFL teams.

items.headline: 2022 Second-round draft pick
items.summary: Wan'Dale Robinson was selected in the second round of the 2022 NFL Draft after playing college football at Kentucky.

items.headline: Minnesota Vikings former standout
items.summary: K.J. Osborn spent multiple seasons with the Minnesota Vikings as a wide receiver before joining the Titans.

items.headline: 2021 New York Jets draft pick
items.summary: Michael Carter was drafted by the New York Jets in 2021 and played running back. He was later traded to the Titans organization.

items.headline: 2023 Draft pick from UTEP
items.summary: Tyjae Spears was drafted in 2023 from the University of Texas at El Paso and joined the Titans as a running back.

items.headline: Penn State running back
items.summary: Nicholas Singleton played running back for Penn State University before being drafted into the NFL.

items.headline: Alabama offensive tackle
items.summary: JC Latham was a standout offensive tackle from the University of Alabama before joining the Titans.

items.headline: Notre Dame offensive lineman
items.summary: Peter Skoronski played offensive line for Notre Dame and was drafted by the Titans as a top guard prospect.

items.summary: Elic Ayomanor is a wide receiver from stanford for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Hank Beatty is a wide receiver prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Austin Deculus is a offensive tackle from arizona for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Garrett Dellinger is a guard from penn state for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Ryan Hayes is a offensive tackle from iowa for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Gunnar Helm is a tight end prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Trey Hill is a center from georgia for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Jaren Kanak is a tight end prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: David Martin-Robinson is a tight end prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Lance McCutcheon is a wide receiver from cal for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Rasheed Miller is a offensive tackle prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Tyren Montgomery is a wide receiver prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Dan Moore Jr. is a offensive tackle prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Drew Moss is a guard prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Kalel Mullings is a running back from wake forest for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Bryce Oliver is a wide receiver prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Hal Presley III is a wide receiver prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Xavier Restrepo is a wide receiver from miami for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Austin Schlottmann is a center prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Jackson Slater is a guard from ucla for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Carnell Tate is a wide receiver from ohio state for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Tony Adams is a safety prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: David Ebuka Agoha is a defensive end prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Sean Brown is a linebacker prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.summary: Earnest Brown IV is a defensive end prospect for the Tennessee Titans.

items.headline: Veteran journeyman QB with 14 NFL seasons across 9+ teams

items.headline: Close mentorship with Ray Lewis, refers to him as 'uncle'

items.headline: Set Super Bowl LI sack record with 3 sacks vs Brady
items.summary: In Super Bowl LI, Jarrett tied the Super Bowl single-game sack record with 3 sacks on Tom Brady, though the Falcons lost 34-28 in OT.

items.headline: Converted from quarterback to running back due to Texas injuries

items.headline: 2022 #1 overall pick, signed $110M extension in 2026
items.summary: Drafted 1st overall in 2022 NFL Draft by Jacksonville Jaguars. Signed $37.4M rookie contract (fully guaranteed). In 2023 posted 52 tackles and 10 sacks. Extended with 4-year, $110M deal on April 3, 2026.

items.headline: Georgia Bulldogs national champion (2022), 15 tackles + 2.5 sacks as freshman
items.summary: Played college football for University of Georgia. Part of 2022 College Football Playoff National Championship winning team. Started as junior defensive tackle.

items.headline: 2024 Heisman Trophy winner, drafted #2 overall by Jaguars in 2025
items.summary: Two-way star wide receiver and cornerback for Colorado Buffaloes. Won 2024 Heisman Trophy. Selected 2nd overall in 2025 NFL Draft by Jacksonville Jaguars after Browns trade.

items.headline: First rookie to earn PFF top grades at two positions simultaneously
items.summary: Made NFL history in Week 7 against LA Rams, scoring 34-yard TD reception from Trevor Lawrence. First ever rookie to earn PFF top grades at both WR and CB positions.

items.headline: Knee injury ended rookie season mid-campaign
items.summary: After strong Week 7 performance, Hunter underwent surgery for torn lateral collateral ligament (LCL) in right knee on November 11. Ruled out for remainder of season.

items.headline: 2024 Walter Payton NFL Man of the Year award winner
items.summary: Named 2024 Walter Payton NFL Man of the Year during NFL Honors ceremony. Recognized for community service and exemplary character.

items.headline: Switched teams in 2024: left 49ers after 11-year stint to join Jaguars
items.summary: Signed 3-year, $51M contract with Jacksonville Jaguars on March 15, 2024. Originally drafted by San Francisco 49ers in first round of 2015 NFL Draft.

items.headline: Founder of Armstead Academic Project supporting low-income students
items.summary: Founded Armstead Academic Project in 2019 with wife Melinda. Provides support to students from low-income families in Sacramento, Bay Area, and Jacksonville.

items.headline: Veteran cornerback: 8 seasons with Dallas Cowboys before joining Jaguars in 2025
items.summary: Originally 3rd round pick by Dallas Cowboys in 2017 NFL Draft. Two-time All-American at Michigan. Signed 3-year, $30M contract with Jacksonville on March 12, 2025.

items.headline: Interception in Week 1 vs Panthers, injured mid-season foot injury
items.summary: Intercepted pass from Panthers QB Bryce Young to help seal Week 1 victory. Later suffered foot injury requiring surgery during December game vs Broncos.

items.headline: Yale product with 1000+ career tackles and consistent 100+ tackle seasons
items.summary: Played college football for Yale Bulldogs. Drafted 6th round (2018) by Atlanta Falcons. Has recorded 100+ tackles every season since first achieving milestone.

items.headline: Jacksonville Jaguars defensive leader since 2022
items.summary: Signed 3-year, $45M deal with Jacksonville on March 16, 2022. Extended contract on 3/29/24. Head coach Liam Coen praised his leadership and consistency.

items.headline: Veteran safety with multi-team resume, joined Jaguars in 2025
items.summary: Played college football for Minnesota Golden Gophers. Drafted 4th round (2016) by Kansas City Chiefs. Also played for Cleveland Browns and Houston Texans. Signed 3-year, $19.5M contract with Jacksonville on March 12, 2025.

items.headline: Neck injury caused him to miss 5 games in 2025 season
items.summary: Missed five games due to neck injury after starting first seven games of season. Recorded 31 tackles (22 solo), 6 passes defensed, 1.0 sack in 12 games played.

items.headline: Traded from Vikings to Jaguars in 2023, signed 3-year extension in 2024
items.summary: Drafted 2nd round (2020) by Minnesota Vikings. Traded to Jacksonville on October 31, 2023 for 6th-round pick. Signed 3-year contract extension on March 8, 2024.

items.headline: Part of elite Jaguars O-line: 5 games with zero sacks in 2025, tied for NFL most
items.summary: Started all 17 games at left guard. Played on O-line that did not allow sacks in 5 games (tied for most in league) and no QB hits in 2 games (most in NFL). Boise State product, 6'6" 312 lbs.

items.headline: 2021 2nd round pick, delivered career year in 2024, contract extension 12/1/24
items.summary: Drafted 2nd round (2021) by Jacksonville Jaguars from Stanford Cardinal. Signed contract extension on December 1, 2024. Emerged as steady presence on O-line.

items.headline: Underrated O-tackle: 1.5% knockdown rate (29th among 115 since 2021)
items.summary: Jacksonville's best offensive lineman in 2025 season. Allowed just 1.5% knockdown rate (sacks + QB hits), ranking tied for 29th among 115 qualifying offensive tackles since 2021.

items.headline: 6th round 2023 pick, 58 catches for 847 yards and 5 TDs in 2025 season
items.summary: Drafted 6th round (2023) by Jacksonville from Penn State. Penn State teammate Brenton Strange drafted 2nd round by same team. Birth date: March 21, 2002.

items.headline: Breakout year 3: 58 receptions, 847 yards, 7 catches in playoffs for 107 yards + TD
items.summary: Took significant leap in third season with team. Caught 58 passes for 847 yards and 5 touchdowns. In playoff game, had 7 receptions for 107 yards and 1 TD.

items.headline: College star who surpassed Brett Favre's Southern Miss records, undrafted 49ers free agent
items.summary: Played for Southern Miss Golden Eagles. Surpassed Brett Favre's school records: 4,476 passing yards and 38 TD passes in single season. Signed as undrafted free agent by San Francisco 49ers in 2018.

items.headline: Multi-team journeyman, backup QB for Jaguars 2025 season
items.summary: Signed with Jacksonville Jaguars on March 12, 2025. Won backup QB job and maintained role through entire 2025 season. Played 36 offensive snaps total.

items.headline: 2nd round 2023 pick from Penn State, 40 catches for 411 yards in 2024 season
items.summary: Drafted 2nd round (61st overall) in 2023 NFL Draft from Penn State Nittany Lions. Redshirt junior, made 32 catches for 362 yards and 5 TDs. Born December 27, 2000. Penn State teammate Parker Washington also drafted by Jaguars.

items.headline: Quad injury in Week 5 vs Chiefs, placed on injured reserve October 7, 2025
items.summary: Recorded 20 catches for 204 yards in five games in 2025 season before suffering quad injury in Week 5 vs Kansas City Chiefs. Placed on injured reserve on October 7, 2025.

items.headline: Undrafted free agent (Cardinals 2018), re-signed with Jaguars June 4, 2025
items.summary: Signed as undrafted free agent by Arizona Cardinals on April 30, 2018. Played seven seasons with Cardinals (2018-24). Signed with Jacksonville Jaguars on June 4, 2025. Born August 9, 1994.

items.headline: 2025 season: 37 tackles, 2.5 sacks, led Jaguars LBs in sack production
items.summary: Played all 17 games with 8 starts in 2025. Recorded 37 tackles, 9 QB hits, 6 TFLs, 2.5 sacks, 2 passes defensed, 1 forced fumble, and 11 special teams tackles. Two-time Pro Bowl alternate, four-time team captain.

items.headline: 3rd round 2021 pick (Dolphins), traded to Rams in 2023, joined Jaguars in 2025
items.summary: Drafted 3rd round (2021) by Miami Dolphins from Boston College Eagles. Traded to LA Rams (March 15, 2023) with 3rd-round pick. Signed 2-year, $3M contract with Jacksonville Jaguars on March 12, 2025.

items.headline: Competing for roster spot behind Brenton Strange and Johnny Mundt
items.summary: At 6'5", 254 lbs, competing for tight end opportunities in 2025. Pursuing chances behind Brenton Strange and Johnny Mundt after Evan Engram departed for Denver.

items.headline: 7th round 2018 pick, most games played by Jaguars punter (112 games)
items.summary: Drafted 7th round (247th overall) in 2018 NFL Draft. Appeared in 112 games for Jaguars, most games played by punter in franchise history. 49.4 average in 2024 on 68 punts (3,359 yards).

items.headline: 2024 Pro Bowl, All-Pro, signed as NFL's highest-paid punter (4-year extension, June 9, 2025)
items.summary: Earned first Pro Bowl and All-Pro nomination in 2024 (48.0 average in 2025 on 60 punts). Signed 4-year contract extension on June 9, 2025, becoming NFL's highest-paid punter. 43.5 career net average (highest since 1976).

items.headline: 4th round 2023 pick from Florida, expected to help replace Devin Lloyd
items.summary: Drafted 4th round (121st overall) in 2023 NFL Draft from Florida Gators. Born January 15, 1999. College career: 240 total tackles, 23.5 TFLs, 7.5 sacks over 5 seasons (2018-2022) at Florida.

items.headline: Head coach Liam Coen praises his growth after increased snaps in 2024
items.summary: 6'0", 232 lbs outside linebacker known for run-stopping ability and pass-rush contributions. Coach Liam Coen said when Ventrell was asked to play meaningful snaps, they saw really good things.

items.headline: Arkansas product drafted 6th round 2024, PFWA All-Rookie Team selection
items.summary: Drafted 6th round (2024) by Jacksonville from Arkansas Razorbacks. Named PFWA All-Rookie Team selection. Born August 17, 2003. Holds highest field goal percentage in Arkansas history with zero missed extra points.

items.headline: Record-breaking: longest field goals in NFL history (70, 68, 67 yards)
items.summary: Made 70-yard field goal in 2025 preseason (longest in organized football). Made 68-yard FG in 2025 regular season, breaking Justin Tucker's 66-yard NFL record. Added 67-yard FG in Week 18, two longest in NFL history.

items.headline: 3rd round 2020 pick from Ohio State, nose tackle in Jaguars run defense
items.summary: Drafted 3rd round (73rd overall) in 2020 NFL Draft from Ohio State. Born February 1, 1997 (age 29). 6'4", 335 lbs nose tackle. Brother of LA Rams DT Ty Hamilton.

items.headline: 2024 season: 62 tackles, emergency long snapper duty Week 13 vs Titans
items.summary: Recorded 62 tackles (34 solo) in 17 games in 2024. Served as emergency long snapper during Week 13 vs Tennessee (snapped for 1 extra point, 1 FG, 3 punts) after Ross Matiscik injury.

items.headline: 4th round 2025 pick (107th overall) from Notre Dame, high-intelligence defender
items.summary: Selected 4th round (107th overall) in 2025 NFL Draft from Notre Dame Fighting Irish. Jack Burley Kiser, 6'2", 231 lbs. Played six seasons at Notre Dame with 975 snaps on special teams across five phases.

items.headline: 2025 season: 14 tackles (5 solo) in 14 games, 43 defensive snaps, 193 special teams snaps
items.summary: Linebacker coach praised his intelligence and problem-solving. 2025: 14 tackles (5 solo) across 14 appearances, 43 defensive snaps, 193 kick-coverage snaps. Good communicator and special teams contributor.

items.headline: 7th round 2022 pick (222nd overall) from Arkansas, breakthrough 2024 season
items.summary: Drafted 7th round (222nd overall) in 2022 NFL Draft from Arkansas Razorbacks. Born August 24, 1999. Backup role as rookie, part-time 2023, became full-time starter 2024-2025.

items.headline: 3-year, $33M extension March 9, 2026; 2025: 51 tackles, 2 INTs, team-leading 12 PD
items.summary: Signed 3-year, $33M deal (March 9, 2026) with $20.65M guaranteed. 2025 stats: 51 tackles, 2 interceptions, team-leading 12 passes defensed in 12 starts. 72.8 PFF grade (20th among 114 CBs).

items.headline: 1st round 2023 pick (27th overall) from Oklahoma, started all 17 games rookie year
items.summary: Drafted 1st round (27th overall) in 2023 NFL Draft from Oklahoma Sooners. 6'4", 315 lbs. First-team All-Big 12 selection (2022). Started each of past two seasons at left tackle in college.

items.headline: 2025: 15 games started at right tackle, part of elite O-line (5 zero-sack games)
items.summary: Started all 17 games as rookie (2023). 2025: started 15 games at right tackle on line allowing zero sacks in 5 games (tied NFL most) and no QB hits in 2 games. 71.8 PFF grade (34th among 89 OTs).

items.headline: Signed 3-year, $37.5M with Jaguars (March 12, 2025), versatile O-lineman
items.summary: 7-year NFL veteran with Baltimore Ravens, played center, guard, and tackle. Signed 3-year, $37.5M contract with Jacksonville on March 12, 2025. Known for versatility across interior offensive line.

items.headline: 2025: 14 games started at right guard on Jaguars' elite O-line
items.summary: Started 14 games at right guard in 2025 on offensive line that allowed zero sacks in 5 games (tied NFL most) and no QB hits in 2 games. Tool-box player with interior line flexibility.

items.headline: 3rd round 2021 pick (Buccaneers), signed 3-year, $21M with Jaguars (March 12, 2025)
items.summary: Drafted 3rd round (95th overall, 2021) by Tampa Bay from Notre Dame. Started all 17 games in 2022, 2023 at center for Buccaneers. 6'4", 306 lbs. Signed with Jacksonville March 12, 2025.

items.headline: Named Jaguars captain 2025, 62.5 PFF grade (23rd among 40 centers)
items.summary: Named one of six Jaguars captains for 2025 alongside Trevor Lawrence, Josh Hines-Allen, Jourdan Lewis, Logan Cooke, Foye Oluokun. Earned 62.5 PFF grade in 2025.

items.headline: 5th round 2023 pick (160th overall), breakout 2025 season with 5 INTs
items.summary: Drafted 5th round (160th overall, 2023) by Jacksonville. Safety from East Saint Louis, IL. Third-year safety showing progression as rising star. Versatile defender (box, free, slot).

items.headline: 2025 season-best 86.9 PFF grade (led all safeties), 5 INTs, 3 PD
items.summary: Led all safeties with 86.9 PFF grade in 2025. 5 interceptions and 3 pass breakups in regular season. Tied with LB Devin Lloyd for team lead with 5 INTs (2nd most in NFL). Both had 100+ INT return yards (first teammates since 2017).

items.headline: 3rd round 2019 pick (Jets), signed 2-year depth deal with Jaguars 2025
items.summary: Drafted 3rd round (92nd overall, 2019) by New York Jets. Played for Dallas Cowboys, starting 10 games past two seasons. Signed with Jaguars to provide offensive line depth. Plays left tackle, right tackle, and guard.

items.headline: Made impression on coach Coen but mixed 2025 performance with penalty issues
items.summary: Impressed head coach Liam Coen during training camp but 2025 season showed inconsistent play with penalty concerns. Versatile depth option across offensive line positions.
