items.headline: Florida kid who made 2016 national championship team
items.summary: Tony Bradley is from Bartow, Florida and played college basketball at UNC. He was a reserve on the Tar Heels' 2017 NCAA championship team that won the national title.

items.headline: Undrafted journeyman who bounced through three colleges
items.summary: RayJ Dennis went undrafted in 2024 after playing for Boise State, Toledo, and Baylor. Born March 30, 2001, he joined the Clippers for Summer League then moved through Washington, Indiana, and LA before signing with Atlanta.

items.headline: 2024 Big 12 tournament MVP from Iowa State breakthrough
items.summary: Keshon Gilbert, from Saint Louis Missouri, was named Most Outstanding Player at the 2024 Big 12 tournament after leading Iowa State Cyclones to the Big 12 title. He averaged 13.7 PPG, 4.4 RPG, and 4.2 APG in 37 starts.

items.headline: Senegalese journeyman raised in Dakar discovered basketball by mom
items.summary: Mouhamed Gueye grew up in Dakar, Senegal as the youngest of four siblings. He played soccer until his mother encouraged him to pursue basketball because of his height. He never played organized basketball before attending Prolific Prep in the US.

items.headline: Bahamian raised by single mother in tiny house with 6 siblings
items.summary: Chavano Hield was born December 17, 1992 in Freeport, Bahamas. After his parents divorced when he was 11, his mother raised all seven children in his grandmother's small three-bedroom house in Eight Mile Rock. He moved to US in 2010.

items.headline: Five-star who reclassified early and went one-and-done at Michigan
items.summary: Caleb Houstan was a consensus five-star recruit who reclassified to graduate high school a year early. He attended Montverde Academy in Florida and played one season at Michigan (2021-22), averaging 10.1 PPG and shooting 35.5% from three.

items.headline: Gonzaga senior who won Erving Award and reached championship game
items.summary: Corey Kispert was Gonzaga's consensus first-team All-American as a senior in 2021. He won the Julius Erving Award as the nation's top small forward and led the Zags to a 31-1 record. Gonzaga's only loss came against Baylor in the national championship game.

items.headline: Cameroonian who discovered basketball at 15 and moved to US
items.summary: Christian Koloko was born in Douala, Cameroon and initially played soccer before discovering basketball around age 15. He moved to the United States in 2017 and played three seasons at Arizona, earning Pac-12 Defensive Player of the Year in 2022.

items.headline: Australian who won bronze with Boomers at 2020 Tokyo Olympics
items.summary: Jock Landale is a professional basketball player from Australia who was a key member of the Australian national team that won bronze at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics—the Boomers' first ever Olympic medal. He also played in Paris 2024 Olympics.

items.headline: 19-year-old French phenom drafted No. 1 after Wembanyama
items.summary: Zaccharie Risacher became the second consecutive French player drafted No. 1 overall when Atlanta selected him in 2024. At age 19, he played for JL Bourg-en-Bresse and shot nearly 39% from three while averaging 10.1 PPG.

items.headline: UTSA guard who went undrafted but reached NBA after 3 years
items.summary: Keaton Wallace played college basketball for UTSA (not Texas Tech). He averaged 18.8 PPG, 4.5 RPG, and 3.1 APG as a junior. After going undrafted in 2021, he made his NBA debut on October 27, 2024 after three years of professional development.

items.headline: Toronto native with Jamaican father and Canadian mother
items.summary: Dalano Banton was born November 7, 1999 in Toronto, Ontario to a Jamaican father and Canadian mother. He grew up in the Mount Olive area of Rexdale, Toronto. He was the first-ever player drafted by the Toronto Raptors who was Canadian.

items.headline: Bosnian-American consensus national player of year at Iowa
items.summary: Luka Garza is a Bosnian-American center born December 27, 1998. He was the consensus national college player of the year for the 2020-21 season at Iowa. He became the first Iowa men's basketball player to twice win Big Ten Player of the Year and was a 2-time All-American.

items.headline: Spanish youngster born to two pro basketball-playing parents
items.summary: Hugo González is a Spanish professional basketball player born February 5, 2006 in Madrid. He was born to former professional basketball-playing parents. Drafted 28th overall in 2025, he is the only Celtics rookie since Larry Bird to record a double-double with 18 points and 16 rebounds.

items.headline: Son of former NBA star Ron Harper; led Rutgers to tournament
items.summary: Ron Harper Jr. was born April 12, 2000 and is the son of former NBA player Ron Harper. He played college basketball for Rutgers and as a junior averaged 14.9 PPG and 5.9 RPG, helping lead Rutgers to their first NCAA Tournament in 30 years.

items.headline: South Dakota State sharpshooter who transferred to Creighton Sweet 16
items.summary: Baylor Scheierman played three seasons at South Dakota State where he averaged 16 PPG on 44% three-point shooting and won Summit League POTY. He transferred to Creighton for his fifth year, averaged 18.5 PPG, earned All-American honors, and led Creighton to the Sweet 16.

items.headline: Ukrainian who led VCU to 2025 Atlantic Ten championship
items.summary: Max Shulga is a Ukrainian professional basketball player born June 25, 2002. He played for Utah State and then VCU, leading the Rams to the 2025 Atlantic Ten Conference Championship. He was named the 2025 A-10 Men's Basketball Player of the Year.

items.headline: Wisconsin breakout star who scored 41 vs Arizona in fourth game
items.summary: John Tonje was born April 23, 2001 and played five college seasons at Colorado State, Missouri, and Wisconsin. In only his fourth game with the Badgers, he scored a career-high 41 points against 9th-ranked Arizona, the fourth most in Wisconsin history.

items.headline: Serbian center whose father was pro basketball player
items.summary: Nikola Vučević was born October 24, 1990 in Morges, Switzerland. His father Borislav Vučević played professional basketball in Yugoslavia, Switzerland, and Belgium. His mother Ljiljana Kubura played professionally for Sarajevo and Yugoslavia's national team.

items.headline: Five-star who led Arkansas to Sweet 16 and won title as Celtics rookie
items.summary: Jordan Walsh was a consensus five-star recruit who committed to Arkansas over Texas, Memphis, Arizona State, and Kansas. In his lone season, he averaged 7.1 PPG and 3.9 RPG, helping Arkansas reach the Sweet 16. He won a championship as a second-round pick for the 2023-24 Celtics.

items.headline: British big man with 7-foot-5 wingspan known for rim protection
items.summary: Amari Williams is a British professional basketball player born January 28, 2002. He played for Drexel and Kentucky before being drafted 46th by Orlando in 2025 and traded to the Celtics. He stands 6-foot-11 with a 7-foot-5 wingspan, excelling as a rim protector.

items.headline: Kansas Senior who won Final Four MVP and Big 12 Player of Year
items.summary: Ochai Agbaji was born April 20, 2000 and stood 6-5, 215 pounds. As a senior at Kansas, he was a consensus first-team All-American and Big 12 Player of the Year in 2022. He led the Jayhawks to the national championship and was named the Final Four Most Outstanding Player.

items.headline: Wichita State guard who was AAC co-Player of the Year
items.summary: Tyson Etienne is an American professional basketball player who played for Wichita State. As a sophomore, he averaged 16.3 PPG, 3.4 RPG, and 2.5 APG, earning AAC co-Player of the Year honors. He was undrafted in 2022 but signed with the Long Island Nets.

items.headline: Ohio State triple-double threat who overcame torn ACL injury
items.summary: E.J. Liddell is from Belleville, Illinois and played for Ohio State. During his junior season, he averaged 19.4 PPG, 7.9 RPG, and 2.6 BPG. He tore his ACL during Summer League 2022 and has worked back from that major injury. He was drafted 39th overall in 2022.

items.headline: Florida State star who led Clippers comeback to Western Finals
items.summary: Terance Mann played for Florida State University for four seasons under coach Leonard Hamilton. The 6-foot-7 guard dropped 39 points in Game 6 of the 2021 Western Conference Semifinals (7-10 three-pointers) sparking a 25-point comeback that sent the Clippers to the Western Conference Finals.

items.headline: Jamaican-American with 38.5-inch vertical called The Lawn Mower
items.summary: Josh Minott is a Jamaican-American born November 25, 2002 who stands 6-8 and weighs 205 lbs. He earned the nickname The Lawn Mower for his motor and energy. He played one season at Memphis, averaging 6.6 PPG and 3.8 RPG over 14.7 minutes per game.

items.headline: Israeli who turned pro at 16 and developed playmaking skills
items.summary: Ben Saraf is an Israeli professional basketball player born April 14, 2006. He started playing professionally at 16 years old for Elitzur Netanya before advancing to the Israeli Premier League and eventually signing with Ratiopharm Ulm of the BBL in Germany.

items.headline: Cincinnati Bearcats star who went undrafted but reached NBA
items.summary: Trevon Scott was born November 25, 1996 and played for Cincinnati. As a senior, he averaged 11.4 PPG, 10.5 RPG, 2.2 APG and 1.5 SPG while earning First Team All-AAC and Defensive Player of the Year honors. After going undrafted in 2020, he eventually made the NBA.

items.headline: Chattanooga Southern Conf Player of Year who transferred to Gonzaga
items.summary: Malachi Smith was the Southern Conference Player of the Year and Lou Henson National Mid-Major Player of the Year at Chattanooga before transferring to Gonzaga for his final college season in 2022-23. After going undrafted in 2023, he eventually signed with the Nets.

items.headline: 19-year-old Frenchman named Basketball Champions League Best Young Player
items.summary: Nolan Traoré is a French professional basketball player born May 28, 2006. In May 2025, he was named the Basketball Champions League Best Young Player. He scored 27 points in a Champions League home win—the most by a player under 21, beating Alperen Şengün's previous record.

items.headline: Stanford five-star ranked number one small forward in 2020 class
items.summary: Ziaire Williams was a consensus five-star recruit and number one small forward in the 2020 class who committed to Stanford over Arizona, USC, North Carolina, and UCLA. He averaged 10.7 PPG and 4.6 RPG as a Stanford freshman before being drafted 10th overall in 2021.

items.headline: Kansas national champ and All-American who won Summer League MVP
items.summary: Jalen Wilson was born November 4, 2000 and is a 6-6, 220-lb forward. He was a 2022 NCAA National Champion and 2023 Consensus All-American First Team selection while at Kansas. He won the 2024 NBA 2K25 Summer League MVP award with the Nets.

items.headline: Michigan big man who led Wolverines to Big Ten title as sophomore
items.summary: Danny Wolf was born May 5, 2004 and stands 6-11, weighing 250 lbs. As a junior at Michigan, he averaged 13.2 PPG and 9.7 RPG (led conference). He was named to the All-Tournament team and helped Michigan win the 2025 Big Ten tournament championship.

items.headline: 2021 championship winner with Milwaukee Bucks defensive contributor
items.summary: Pat Connaughton was drafted 41st overall by the Brooklyn Nets in 2015 and traded to Portland. He played for the Trail Blazers for three seasons before winning a championship with the Milwaukee Bucks in his sixth NBA season during 2020-21.

items.headline: British player from Newcastle upon Tyne who starred for Princeton
items.summary: Tosan Evbuomwan is a British professional basketball player born February 16, 2001 from Newcastle upon Tyne, England. He played college basketball for Princeton where he was named First Team All-Ivy League and First Team All-District in 2023 before declaring for the NBA draft.

items.headline: Australian Olympian who won bronze with Boomers in Tokyo
items.summary: Josh Green is an Australian professional basketball player born in Sydney to Australian mother Cahla and American-born father Delmas. He was part of the Australian basketball team that won bronze at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics and also played in the 2024 Paris Olympics.

items.headline: Clemson All-ACC center who led team in scoring and blocks
items.summary: PJ Hall is a 6-foot-10 center who played college basketball for Clemson. He led the Tigers with 18.3 PPG in his senior season and 51 blocks. After going undrafted in 2024, he signed with Denver for Summer League and played in the G League before joining the Hornets.

items.headline: Georgia native who transferred from Tulane to Duke as senior
items.summary: Sion James was born December 4, 2002 and hails from Sugar Hill, Georgia. He began his collegiate career at Tulane before transferring to Duke University for his senior year. He stood 6-feet-5 and was known as a versatile perimeter defender with strong three-point shooting.

items.headline: Creighton five-year star who won four Big East Defensive POYs
items.summary: Ryan Kalkbrenner played five years at Creighton (not Gonzaga) and finished with 19.2 PPG, 8.7 RPG, and 2.7 BPG. He was a four-time Big East Defensive Player of the Year. In his final season he led Creighton to finish as the 34th pick in the 2025 NBA draft.

items.headline: Five-star who scored 38 in fourth game at UConn freshman scoring record
items.summary: Liam McNeeley was a consensus five-star recruit from Richardson, Texas who transferred to Montverde Academy in Florida. He played for UConn and on February 11, 2025 scored 38 points and grabbed 10 rebounds against Creighton—the most by a UConn freshman in conference history.

items.headline: Illinois State to Kentucky transfer who was second in scoring
items.summary: Antonio Reeves played for Illinois State and then Kentucky. As a senior at Kentucky, he ranked second on the team in scoring behind All-American Oscar Tshiebwe at 14.4 PPG. He was drafted 47th overall by Orlando in 2024 and joined the Hornets in 2025-26.

items.headline: Georgia Bulldog freshman who made SEC All-Freshman Team
items.summary: Asa Newell was born October 5, 2005 and played college basketball for Georgia. In his debut, he recorded 26 points, 11 rebounds, and three blocks. He averaged 15.4 PPG, 6.8 RPG, and 1.0 BPG in 29.0 minutes per game and was named to the SEC All-Freshman Team.

items.headline: Auburn Tigers player who went undrafted but reached NBA
items.summary: Chaney Johnson was born June 20, 2002 and stands 6-8, 225 lbs. He played college basketball for Auburn. After going undrafted in 2025, he signed with Cleveland but was waived before the season, then played in the G League before signing with the Nets.

items.headline: 2023 NBA Draft pick from Stanford to Sacramento Kings
items.summary: Brown was drafted 14th overall in the 2023 NBA Draft by the Sacramento Kings, playing college basketball at Stanford University.

items.headline: 2023 NBA Draft pick from Furman University
items.summary: Furphy was selected in the 2023 NBA Draft from Furman University, where he played college basketball.

items.headline: Maryland star played college at University of Maryland
items.summary: Huff played center for the University of Maryland Terrapins, establishing himself as a rim protector and offensive threat before entering the NBA.

items.headline: Played college basketball at University of Louisville
items.summary: Jackson played for the University of Louisville Cardinals before entering professional basketball.

items.headline: Marquette Golden Eagles guard
items.summary: Jones played college basketball for Marquette University, where he developed as a scoring guard in the Big East Conference.

items.headline: 13+ seasons in NBA, led league in steals multiple times
items.summary: McConnell is a veteran point guard who spent most of his NBA career with the Phoenix Suns and Philadelphia 76ers, known for elite defense and high assist rates. He led the NBA in steals per game in recent seasons.

items.headline: Undrafted 2012, signed as free agent to Sixers
items.summary: McConnell went undrafted in 2012 but was signed by the Philadelphia 76ers as an undrafted free agent, developing into a rotation staple.

items.headline: Played college basketball in Division I
items.summary: Peter played college basketball before entering professional leagues.

items.headline: Marquette University basketball player
items.summary: Potter played center for Marquette University, contributing as a frontcourt player in college before professional career.

items.headline: Vanderbilt Commodores basketball player
items.summary: Sheppard played college basketball for Vanderbilt University in the SEC.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Slawson played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: Oregon State Beavers basketball player
items.summary: Thompson played guard for Oregon State University, developing as a ball handler and defender.

items.headline: 2023 NBA Draft 5th overall pick from Houston
items.summary: Walker was selected 5th overall in the 2023 NBA Draft by the Indiana Pacers from the University of Houston Cougars.

items.headline: French international, 20+ year NBA veteran
items.summary: Batum is a French basketball player with an extensive NBA career spanning two decades, playing for multiple teams including Portland, Charlotte, and LA Clippers. Represented France in international competition.

items.headline: 2012 Olympic gold medalist with France
items.summary: Batum won a gold medal representing France at the 2012 London Olympics.

items.headline: 2023 NBA Draft pick from Minnesota
items.summary: Christie was selected in the 2023 NBA Draft, playing college basketball for the University of Minnesota Golden Gophers.

items.headline: 7+ seasons with Atlanta Hawks, drafted 19th in 2017
items.summary: Collins was drafted 19th overall by the Atlanta Hawks in 2017 from Wake Forest University and became a major part of their frontcourt rotation.

items.headline: 25-game suspension for PED violation in 2021
items.summary: Collins received a 25-game suspension in 2021 after testing positive for banned substance. He served the suspension and returned to competition.

items.headline: Played college basketball
items.summary: Niederhauser played college basketball before professional career.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Omier played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: Played college basketball
items.summary: Pedulla played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: Played college basketball
items.summary: Sanders played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: University of Kentucky guard
items.summary: Washington played for the University of Kentucky Wildcats, one of college basketball's most prestigious programs.

items.headline: 10th overall pick in 2019 NBA Draft from Texas
items.summary: Hayes was selected 10th overall in the 2019 NBA Draft by the New Orleans Pelicans from the University of Texas.

items.headline: Arrested in Los Angeles in July 2021
items.summary: Hayes was arrested in 2021 on suspicion of domestic violence following an incident in Los Angeles. Charges were later reduced/dropped.

items.headline: German international, 10+ NBA seasons
items.summary: Kleber is a German forward who spent most of his NBA career with the Dallas Mavericks, known for three-point shooting and versatility.

items.headline: Alabama Crimson Tide basketball player
items.summary: Knecht played college basketball for the University of Alabama before NBA career.

items.headline: 2022 NBA Draft pick from Wake Forest
items.summary: LaRavia was selected in the 2022 NBA Draft after playing for Wake Forest University.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Manon played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: 9th overall pick in 2023 NBA Draft from Arkansas
items.summary: Smith Jr. was selected 9th overall in the 2023 NBA Draft by the Charlotte Hornets from the University of Arkansas.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Theiro played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: Gonzaga University star, NCAA runner-up 2021
items.summary: Timme played for Gonzaga University and was part of the Bulldogs' run to the NCAA National Championship game in 2021, where they were runners-up.

items.headline: 14th overall pick in 2020 NBA Draft from Louisville
items.summary: Vanderbilt was drafted 14th overall in the 2020 NBA Draft by the Denver Nuggets from the University of Louisville.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Bal played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: 13+ year NBA veteran, 2020 Lakers championship
items.summary: Caldwell-Pope is a long-tenured NBA shooting guard who won an NBA championship with the Los Angeles Lakers in 2020, playing alongside LeBron James.

items.headline: 2019 arrest in Atlanta
items.summary: Caldwell-Pope was arrested in Atlanta in September 2019 on charges related to carrying a firearm, charges were eventually dismissed.

items.headline: 21st overall pick in 2019 NBA Draft from Gonzaga
items.summary: Clarke was drafted 21st overall in 2019 by the Memphis Grizzlies from Gonzaga University, where he was a standout defender and rebounder.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Clayton played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: 18+ year NBA veteran, 2016 Cleveland Cavaliers championship
items.summary: Gibson is a veteran power forward who won an NBA championship with the 2016 Cleveland Cavaliers championship team that defeated the Golden State Warriors in a historic comeback.

items.headline: University of North Carolina basketball player
items.summary: Hendricks played for the University of North Carolina Tar Heels in the ACC.

items.headline: 20th overall pick in 2023 NBA Draft, skipped college
items.summary: Jackson was selected 20th overall in the 2023 NBA Draft by the Memphis Grizzlies as an elite prep prospect who skipped college to enter the NBA directly.

items.headline: University of Virginia point guard
items.summary: Jerome played college basketball for the University of Virginia Cavaliers.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Mashack played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Okani played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: 19th overall pick in 2023 NBA Draft from Vanderbilt, son of Hall of Famer Scottie Pippen
items.summary: Pippen Jr. was selected 19th overall in the 2023 NBA Draft by the Memphis Grizzlies from Vanderbilt University. He is the son of NBA legend Scottie Pippen.

items.headline: Son of Hall of Famer Scottie Pippen (6x NBA champion, defensive legend)
items.summary: Pippen Jr.'s father, Scottie Pippen, is a Basketball Hall of Famer with 6 NBA championships, 10x All-Star, and considered one of the greatest two-way players ever.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Prosper played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: College basketball player
items.summary: Rupert played college basketball before NBA career.

items.headline: Drafted by Memphis Grizzlies in 2023 NBA Draft
items.summary: Selected by Memphis in the second round after playing college basketball.

items.headline: Villanova guard signed as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Spencer joined the Grizzlies as an undrafted free agent after finishing his college career at Villanova.

items.headline: Duke star enters NBA Draft
items.summary: Whitehead declared for the NBA Draft after playing for Duke University, recognized as a top prospect.

items.headline: Marquette alumnus joins Grizzlies roster
items.summary: Williamson, a Marquette graduate, signed with Memphis as an undrafted free agent.

items.headline: Italian professional signs with Heat
items.summary: Fontecchio joined the Miami Heat after establishing his career in European professional basketball.

items.headline: Forward joins Miami Heat rotation
items.summary: Gardner signed with the Heat as part of roster development for the 2023-24 season.

items.headline: Russian center signs with Miami Heat
items.summary: Goldin joined the Heat from international basketball, bringing size to the frontcourt.

items.headline: Lithuanian guard joins NBA
items.summary: Jakucionis signed with Miami after establishing himself as a professional prospect in Europe.

items.headline: Forward develops in Heat organization
items.summary: Johnson became part of Miami's roster development pipeline for young talent.

items.headline: Serbian prospect drafted by Miami Heat
items.summary: Jovic was drafted by the Heat and has been developing as a potential future contributor.

items.headline: Duke Blue Devil signs with Miami Heat
items.summary: Keels transitioned to the NBA after completing his college career at Duke University.

items.headline: Swedish player joins Heat
items.summary: Larsson signed with Miami after playing professionally in Europe.

items.headline: Guard transitions to NBA
items.summary: Smith joined the Heat after developing his game in college basketball.

items.headline: Young center drafted by Miami Heat
items.summary: Ware was drafted by Miami and is developing as a center prospect.

items.headline: Guard signs two-way contract with Heat
items.summary: Young signed a two-way deal with Miami, developing his professional career.

items.headline: Younger brother of Giannis in Bucks organization
items.summary: Alex is the younger brother of superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo and plays for Milwaukee.

items.headline: Older brother of Giannis, NBA veteran
items.summary: Thanasis is Giannis's older brother and has been a role player in the NBA with Milwaukee.

items.headline: Senegalese prospect drafted by Bucks
items.summary: Dieng was drafted by Milwaukee and is developing as a young prospect.

items.headline: Guard joins Bucks as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Jackson signed with Milwaukee as an undrafted free agent after college.

items.headline: Son of former NBA player Larry Nance Sr.
items.summary: Nance is the son of former NBA star Larry Nance Sr. and plays for the Milwaukee Bucks.

items.headline: Veteran wing signs with Milwaukee
items.summary: Prince is an experienced NBA player who has played for multiple teams including Atlanta and Bucks.

items.headline: Princeton guard joins Bucks
items.summary: Ryan signed with Milwaukee after completing his Ivy League career at Princeton.

items.headline: Center rotates through NBA teams
items.summary: Sims has played for multiple NBA teams including New York Knicks before joining Milwaukee.

items.headline: Son of former NBA player Gary Trent Sr.
items.summary: Trent Jr. is the son of former NBA player and follows in his father's footsteps with Milwaukee.

items.headline: Veteran forward with Timberwolves
items.summary: Anderson is a crafty veteran forward who has played for several NBA teams including Memphis and now Minnesota.

items.headline: Forward joins Timberwolves roster
items.summary: Beringer signed with Minnesota as part of roster development.

items.headline: UCLA guard drafted by Timberwolves
items.summary: Clark was drafted by Minnesota after starring at UCLA.

items.headline: Forward signs with Minnesota
items.summary: Freeman joined the Timberwolves as part of the roster.

items.headline: Scorer joins Timberwolves from Denver
items.summary: Hyland is a young scorer who has played for Denver Nuggets and Minnesota.

items.headline: Veteran Australian guard with Timberwolves
items.summary: Ingles is an experienced international player from Australia playing for Minnesota.

items.headline: Young forward develops with Timberwolves
items.summary: Phillips is a young prospect developing in the Minnesota organization.

items.headline: Guard signs with Timberwolves
items.summary: Pullin joined Minnesota as a roster addition.

items.headline: Texas Tech guard drafted by Timberwolves
items.summary: Shannon Jr. was drafted by Minnesota after playing college basketball at Texas Tech.

items.headline: Center joins Minnesota
items.summary: Zikarsky signed with the Timberwolves as a roster development player.

items.headline: Creighton guard drafted by Pelicans
items.summary: Alexander was drafted by New Orleans after college at Creighton.

items.headline: Michigan center joins Pelicans
items.summary: Dickinson transitioned to the NBA after playing college basketball at Michigan.

items.headline: Guard signs with Pelicans
items.summary: Fears joined New Orleans as a roster addition.

items.headline: UConn guard drafted by Pelicans
items.summary: Hawkins was drafted by New Orleans after playing at UConn.

items.headline: Veteran center with Pelicans
items.summary: Jordan is an established NBA veteran who has played for multiple teams before joining New Orleans.

items.headline: Warriors veteran signs with Pelicans
items.summary: Looney is an experienced NBA big man who has spent time with the Warriors and other teams.

items.headline: Croatian forward joins Pelicans
items.summary: Matkovic is an international player from Croatia who signed with New Orleans.

items.headline: Guard drafted by Pelicans
items.summary: McGowens was drafted by New Orleans and is developing as a prospect.

items.headline: Cameroon national team debut at AfroBasket 2025
items.summary: Born in Belgium but raised in Yaoundé, Cameroon, Missi moved to the US in 2021. On June 30, 2025, he announced his Cameroon national team debut at AfroBasket 2025, scoring 12 points in the opening game against Tunisia.

items.headline: All-Rookie second team selection as 21st overall pick
items.summary: Became a starter by the 5th game of his rookie season, averaging 9.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks in 27.3 minutes per game across 67 appearances. Selected 21st overall by New Orleans on June 26, 2024.

items.headline: Undrafted single father blossoming as scorer
items.summary: Ghanaian-USA guard from Haymarket, VA with one son named Gabriel. After going undrafted in 2024, signed with New Orleans Pelicans for summer league. Joined Birmingham Squadron on two-way contract March 4, 2026. Described as 'blossoming as a scorer and a father.'

items.headline: Transfer portal star from Texas Tech, TCU to Georgetown, drafted 40th by Pelicans
items.summary: Played college basketball for Texas Tech, TCU, and Georgetown. In 2024-25 at Georgetown, averaged 17.2 points, 5.8 rebounds, 3.6 assists per game on 48.1% FG and 40% from three. Drafted 40th overall by Washington Wizards on June 26, 2025, then traded to New Orleans Pelicans.

items.headline: First rookie center ever to record 30-point triple-double
items.summary: On December 8, 2025, Queen put up 33 points, 10 rebounds, and 10 assists with 4 blocks in loss to San Antonio Spurs. Became the first rookie center to accomplish a 30-point triple-double in NBA history. Drafted 13th overall by Atlanta Hawks, traded to New Orleans for Asa Newell.

items.headline: New York native and undrafted guardhomecoming after Pelicans trade
items.summary: Native New Yorker nicknamed 'Grand Theft Alvarado' for elite steal ability. After going undrafted and signing with New Orleans (Aug 19, 2021), traded to Knicks on February 5, 2026 for Dalen Terry and two future second-round picks. Return home immediately endeared him to Knicks fans.

items.headline: French 25th overall pick (2024) spending time in G League development
items.summary: One of four French players drafted in 2024 first round at #25 by Knicks. 6'7 ball-handler and promising shooter. Has logged just 3.1 minutes with Knicks varsity, spending majority of tenure in G League. Knicks picked up 2026-27 rookie scale team option.

items.headline: Second-round pick (51st, 2025) converting 39.8% from deep
items.summary: Drafted 51st overall by LA Clippers on June 26, 2025, rights traded to Knicks. Signed one-year $1.27M contract. Averaging 3.6 ppg, 1.4 rpg on 39.8% three-point shooting through 50 appearances. Career-high 18 points (4/4 from three) vs New Orleans on December 29, 2025.

items.headline: German center drafted 58th overall (2024), signed two-way with Knicks
items.summary: German professional drafted 28th pick of 2nd round (58th overall) by Dallas Mavericks in 2024. Draft rights traded to Knicks for Melvin Ajinça. Signed two-way contract with Knicks on July 8, 2024, made NBA debut October 22, 2024.

items.headline: Undrafted versatile forward from Clemson and UAB playing on Knicks two-way
items.summary: Went undrafted in 2023 after playing two seasons at Clemson and three at UAB (2020-2023). Started with Pelicans, later signed two-way with Knicks on September 16, 2025. Averaged 4.2 ppg and 3.8 rpg in 64 regular-season games. 6'10, 270 lbs forward.

items.headline: First-rounder (26th, 2024) with winding path back to Knicks on two-way
items.summary: Drafted 26th by Washington Wizards in 2024, immediately traded to Knicks along with 51st pick for Kyshawn George. Knicks traded his rights to Thunder for five second-rounders. Signed two-way with Knicks on January 20, 2026 after complex NBA journey.

items.headline: Drafted 34th overall (2024) in draft night trade from Portland to Knicks
items.summary: Traded on draft night to Knicks from Portland Trail Blazers at 34th overall pick in 2024 draft. Kolek averaged 4.4 ppg, 1.6 rpg, 2.7 apg in 2024-25. On January 1, 2025, rare double-header saw him score 36 points, 11 assists for Westchester Knicks.

items.headline: Drafted 56th by Suns (2024), traded to Knicks same night, made NBA debut March 2025
items.summary: Selected 56th overall by Phoenix Suns on June 27, 2024, immediately traded to Knicks. Made NBA debut March 25, 2025 vs Mavericks, scoring first two career points. Finished season averaging 2.4 ppg, 1.3 rpg, 1.0 apg in 21 games. Averaged 11.9 ppg with Westchester Knicks in G League.

items.headline: Veteran guard signed by Knicks in free agency after Wizards waiver
items.summary: Signed one-year contract with Knicks on September 14, 2024 after being waived by Washington Wizards on July 6, 2024. History of multiple trades: Suns traded him with Chris Paul and picks to Wizards for Bradley Beal in 2023. Re-signed with Knicks December 23 after G League stint.

items.headline: Lottery pick released by Spurs, signed with Knicks with blue and orange hair
items.summary: Former Spurs first-round pick sought bigger role after limited playing time (28 games, zero starts, 12.8 mpg). Released on buyout with 10 interested suitors. Signed remainder of season with Knicks. Dyed hair blue and orange upon arrival, expressing excitement about 'crazy opportunity.'

items.headline: First Northwestern player drafted since 1999, selected 44th by Thunder (2025)
items.summary: Northwestern senior selected 44th overall by OKC Thunder in 2025 NBA draft. First Wildcat drafted since Evan Eschmeyer in 1999. As high school senior averaged 32.7 ppg, 11.5 rpg, 6.5 apg, 3.8 spg, 2.6 bpg. At Northwestern averaged 14.6 ppg, 7.5 rpg, earned All-Big Ten and All-Defensive honors.

items.headline: Undrafted center won 2025 NBA championship in rookie season
items.summary: Went undrafted in 2024. Signed with Toronto Raptors, waived October 2024. Signed two-way with Thunder November 16 to help frontcourt during injuries to Hartenstein, Holmgren, Williams. Finished rookie season as NBA champion defeating Pacers in 7 games.

items.headline: Two-time NBA champion reunited with coach Daigneault in Thunder championship run
items.summary: Traded to Thunder June 21, 2024 from Chicago for Josh Giddey, reuniting with Mark Daigneault. Signed four-year, $81M extension December 22. 2020 Lakers champion. Scored 20 points in both Games 2 and 4 of 2025 Finals. Finals averages: 10.1 ppg, 3.6 rpg. Two-time All-Defensive Team.

items.headline: Grew up with Thunder teammate Jaylin Williams in Fort Smith, Arkansas
items.summary: Born July 2, 1999 in Fort Smith, Arkansas. Attended Northside High School alongside now-teammate Jaylin Williams. Selected 49th overall by Philadelphia in 2020 draft. Signed with Thunder October 16, 2022. Re-signed four-year, $48M July 1, 2024. First NBA champion June 2025.

items.headline: 5.3 million TikTok followers balancing social media stardom with NBA success
items.summary: Selected 16th overall by 76ers in 2024 draft. Rookie season averaged 15.3 ppg before knee injury ended campaign at 23 games. Set NBA rookie record: three-pointers in 8 consecutive games. Traded to Thunder February 4, 2026 for first-rounder and three seconds. Surpassed 5.3M TikTok followers.

items.headline: Belgian-American second-year guard won 2025 championship with dominant playoffs
items.summary: 23-year-old Belgian-American born June 25, 2002. Drafted 38th by Knicks in 2024, immediately traded to Thunder. Won 2025 championship as rookie. 2025-26 season: 13.6 ppg, 3.6 apg, 3.3 rpg in 25.8 mpg across 57 games. Game 4 first-round sweep: 22 points, 6 assists, +27 in 34 minutes.

items.headline: Undrafted Iowa sharpshooter signed two-way by Thunder after 2025 draft
items.summary: 6'7 guard from Waukee, Iowa went undrafted in 2025 despite elite shooting resume. Finished Iowa career with 1,619 points, 622 rebounds, 256 assists, 271 threes on 35.7%. Played through injuries senior year affecting stats. Signed two-way contract with Thunder on March 2, 2026.

items.headline: 15th overall pick (2025) suffered torn ACL in September, will miss entire rookie season
items.summary: First Georgetown player ever drafted as freshman. Selected 15th by Thunder June 25, 2025. During college: 14.5 ppg, 8.5 rpg, 2.4 apg, 2 bpg in 31.3 mpg. Named Third-Team All-Big East, All-Freshman. Tore ACL September 4, 2025, ruled out for 2025-26 season.

items.headline: 20-year-old 12th pick overcame testicular cancer diagnosis to make NBA debut
items.summary: Drafted 12th overall in 2024, missed entire 2024-25 season with torn ACL. Diagnosed with testicular cancer, missed first part of 2025-26 season for chemotherapy. Made NBA debut February 12 vs Bucks despite not playing single game, still won 2025 championship. Scored 18 points vs Suns, tied rookie assist record with 14.

items.headline: First NBA player of Vietnamese descent, 2025 champion and teammate award finalist
items.summary: First player of Vietnamese descent drafted in NBA (34th overall, 2022). Won 2025 championship June 22. Signed three-year, $24M extension June 29, 2025. 2025-26: 7.2 ppg, 5.5 rpg, 2.4 apg in 19.6 mpg. Finished 10th in Twyman-Stokes Teammate of Year voting.

items.headline: Undrafted guard won 2025 NBA championship in his 6th season with Thunder
items.summary: 31-year-old TCU alum went undrafted in 2018, signed with Pelicans. Traded to Thunder in 2020. Re-signed four-year, $27.2M extension July 20, 2022. Won 2025 NBA Finals defeating Pacers in 7 games. 2025-26: 6.5 ppg, 3.3 rpg on 47.3% FG, made season debut after knee surgery.

items.headline: Georgian center made NBA history with unique bench scoring line vs Timberwolves
items.summary: Drafted 18th by Pacers in 2019, waived February 2023, signed with Magic February 13. Against Minnesota, recorded 14 points on 6-11 shooting, 15 rebounds, 6 assists in 22 minutes off the bench. First player ever in NBA history with that stat line coming off bench in 22 minutes or fewer.

items.headline: Undrafted secret weapon powers Magic first-round upset with viral dunk
items.summary: Undrafted 2022, signed with Miami Heat. Played for Pelicans (July 2024), then Magic (July 2025) on two-way contracts. In Game 4 playoffs vs Pistons, delivered emphatic dunk over Jalen Duren while leading fast break past Cade Cunningham. Key defensive contributor fueling Magic postseason surge.

items.headline: Eight-time Defensive Player of the Year winner signed by Magic mid-season
items.summary: 30-year-old West Virginia alum drafted 32nd overall (2018) by Grizzlies. Named NABC Defensive POY, Lefty Driesell POY, Naismith DPOY as senior. Played 8 seasons for 6 teams. Signed with Magic February 6, 2026 after Tyus Jones trade. Averaging 8.2 ppg, 2.8 apg in 21.8 mpg.

items.headline: Undrafted Florida/Michigan center signed to two-way contract by Magic
items.summary: Born May 25, 2000. Went undrafted in 2023 after three-time All-SEC honors at Florida. 6'10, 250 lbs center. Signed Exhibit 10 with Magic August 22, 2025, converted to two-way contract October 17. Playing for Osceola Magic G League affiliate.

items.headline: Brazilian-German polyglot drafted 18th by Magic (2024)
items.summary: Born May 15, 2001 in Munich to Brazilian father and German mother. Fluent in German, Portuguese, Spanish, French, and English. Drafted 18th by Magic June 26, 2024. Made NBA debut October 23 vs Heat (116-97 win). Wears 23, 6'8, 217 lbs.

items.headline: Son of former NBA All-Star Juwan Howard, coached by father at Michigan
items.summary: Son of former NBA All-Star and 2019 champion Juwan Howard, now Michigan head coach. Drafted 11th overall by Magic in 2023 after playing for father at Michigan. Career-high 21 points vs Jazz on January 5 when four Magic starters were sidelined. 2024-25: 4.5 ppg, 1.2 rpg in 60 games.

items.headline: Outspoken Christian athlete overcame two-plus year ACL absence, 6th Man candidate
items.summary: 6th overall pick (2017) from Florida State. Missed over two-and-a-half years after 2020 ACL injury in bubble. Returned January 23, 2021 vs Celtics (10 pts, 3 rb, 2 st in 9 min). Last season: 58 games, 6.8 ppg, 4.5 rpg, 1.2 bpg. Sixth Man of Year nominee. Signed five-year, $84M contract (Christ-follower).

items.headline: Undrafted Puerto Rican guard signed to two-way contract by Magic (Feb 2026)
items.summary: Went undrafted in 2022. Puerto Rican player from Wagner Seahawks and Prince George's CC. Appeared in 112 G League games with Osceola/Lakeland averaging 10.2 ppg, 6.1 rpg, 3.1 apg before signing two-way with Magic in February 2026.

items.headline: 20-year-old French forward traded from Celtics to Magic at 32nd overall (2025)
items.summary: French 3-level scorer and tenacious defender. Drafted 32nd by Magic after trade with Celtics (Boston received picks 46, 57 and two future second rounders). 2024-25: 10.2 ppg, 5.0 rpg, 2.6 apg, 1.4 spg in 26.6 mpg. LNB All-Star, Young Star Game MVP.

items.headline: German big man brother of teammate Franz Wagner, returning from ACL injury
items.summary: German center/forward drafted 25th by Lakers (2018). Younger brother Franz Wagner is teammate on Magic (small forward). Suffered torn ACL December 2024. Signed one-year, $5M contract. Made 2025-26 debut vs Pelicans, posting 8 pts, 2 rb, 2 ast in 10 minutes.

items.headline: First Overtime Elite player to make NBA roster, now starting for 76ers
items.summary: 22-year-old (5/26/2003) undrafted 2022, signed two-way with Spurs as first Overtime Elite player to make NBA roster. Signed two-way with 76ers July 9, 2025, converted to standard contract February 2026. Started 36 of 43 games. Career-high 26 pts, 16 rb (10 off) vs Clippers February 2.

items.headline: 24th pick (2022) waived by 76ers, redeemed via G League
items.summary: Born October 12, 2000. Drafted 24th by Bucks in 2022. Signed Exhibit 10 with 76ers October 14, 2025, waived four days later. Signed two-way December 23 after impressive G League performance with Delaware Blue Coats. Averaging 20.7 ppg on 49.5% FG, 44.2% from three in G League.

items.headline: Nigerian-Turkish second-round pick (41st, 2024) agreed to four-year deal
items.summary: Nigerian-Turkish UCLA Bruin selected 41st overall (11th of 2nd round) in 2024 draft. Agreed to four-year, $8M contract. Made NBA debut October 23, 2024 vs Bucks (124-109 loss). Rookie status building with Sixers organization.

items.headline: Sporting News College POTY drafted 35th overall by 76ers (2025)
items.summary: Auburn star big man, Sporting News Men's CBB Player of Year. After two seasons at Morehead State, led Tigers as top SEC presence. 6'9, 249 lbs. Drafted 35th overall (2nd round) by 76ers June 26, 2025. Concerns about 24.5-inch vertical (fifth-worst among forwards at combine).

items.headline: Philadelphia native, five-star prospect signed two-way after undrafted 2024
items.summary: Consensus five-star recruit from Philadelphia. Played one season at Kentucky averaging 8.8 ppg on 48.6% FG, 36.5% from three in 32 games (30 starts). Went undrafted 2024, signed two-way with 76ers July 4. Signed standard contract February 2025, re-signed July 2025. Career-high 25 points January 14 vs Thunder.

items.headline: NHL-NBA brother duo: traded from Knicks to 76ers, career-high 46 points
items.summary: Kansas and Houston product drafted 25th (2021) by Clippers, traded to Knicks. February 4, 2025, traded to 76ers plus 2nd-round pick for Caleb Martin. Re-signed one-year, $8.7M October 1, 2025. 2025-26: 13.4 ppg, 3.6 rpg, 3.3 apg. Career-high 46 points March 17 vs Rockets. Brother Tyler Myers plays in NHL.

items.headline: 2019 Raptors champion, six-time All-Star returns to 76ers on vet minimum
items.summary: Six-time All-Star, 2019 NBA champion with Raptors (only franchise title). All-NBA Third Team (2016). 2016 Olympics gold medal. Traded to Hornets 2024, signed with 76ers via buyout. Re-signed minimum July 7, 2025. 20th NBA season: 3.9 ppg, 1.9 rpg, 2.7 apg in 35 appearances (12 starts).

items.headline: 51st pick (2022) waived by Nets, signed two-way by 76ers
items.summary: Drafted 51st overall by Golden State (2022), traded to Atlanta Hawks. Appeared in 37 games for Nets this season (6 starts), averaging 7.3 ppg, 2.9 rpg, 1.9 apg in 18.8 mpg before waive/signed by 76ers February 19, 2026. Through 7 games with Sixers: 2.6 ppg, 1.3 rpg, 1.3 apg.

items.headline: Signed with Philadelphia 76ers after three-plus seasons with Chicago Bulls
items.summary: Terry was originally drafted 18th overall in 2022 by the Chicago Bulls and played for them through February 2026, when he signed a two-way contract with the Philadelphia 76ers.

items.headline: Father Samaki Walker was 10-year NBA veteran and 2002 NBA Champion with Lakers
items.summary: Walker was born to Jo Dudley, an FBI agent, and Samaki Walker, who played in the NBA for 10 years and won the 2002 NBA Championship with the Los Angeles Lakers.

items.headline: Scored career-high 30 points in SEC Tournament championship game at LSU
items.summary: During his sophomore season at LSU in 2020-21, Watford averaged 16.3 points and 7.4 rebounds per game and scored a career-high 30 points in an SEC Tournament championship game loss to Alabama.

items.headline: Recorded career-high 27 points in April 2026 victory over Oklahoma City Thunder
items.summary: On April 12, Bouyea recorded a career-high 27 points with nine assists, five rebounds, two blocks, and one steal in a 135-103 victory over the Oklahoma City Thunder.

items.headline: Dominican-American sharpshooter drafted 41st overall, traded from Warriors to Suns
items.summary: Brea was drafted by the Golden State Warriors with the 41st pick in the 2025 NBA draft and traded to the Phoenix Suns. He played college basketball for Kentucky and was known as one of the best shooters in the NBA Draft.

items.headline: Traded to Suns from Bucks in three-team deal with Chicago Bulls
items.summary: Coffey was traded to the Phoenix Suns on February 5, 2026, in a three-team trade involving the Chicago Bulls and Milwaukee Bucks. He is a 6-foot-7 3-and-D wing known for his defensive versatility and three-point shooting.

items.headline: Drafted 28th overall by Denver in 2024, traded to Suns on draft night
items.summary: Dunn was selected 28th overall in the 2024 NBA draft by the Denver Nuggets and immediately traded to the Phoenix Suns along with draft picks. He played college basketball for Virginia.

items.headline: 2025 second-round draft pick from Saint Joseph's Hawks traded to Suns
items.summary: Fleming was drafted by the Minnesota Timberwolves in the second round of the 2025 NBA draft and subsequently traded to the Phoenix Suns. He played college basketball for Saint Joseph's.

items.headline: Set Suns franchise record for 3-pointers in a season with career-high 30-point game
items.summary: Gillespie scored a career-high 30 points on February 3, 2026, in a 130-125 win over Portland and set the Suns' franchise record for 3-pointers in a season during 2025-26.

items.headline: NCAA Division II Player of the Year in 2018, recovered from meniscus surgery
items.summary: Highsmith was the NCAA Division II Player of the Year as a senior in 2018 at Wheeling Jesuit University. In 2025-26, he underwent meniscus surgery on his right knee and was sidelined for 8-10 weeks.

items.headline: App State alumnus first Mountaineer to appear in NBA game since 1952
items.summary: Huntley, who played for Appalachian State, made his NBA debut for the Phoenix Suns in March 2026, becoming the first Mountaineer to appear in an NBA game since 1952.

items.headline: Phoenix native drafted 40th overall by Trail Blazers, immediately traded to Suns
items.summary: Ighodaro was selected 40th overall by Portland in the 2024 NBA draft and traded to the Phoenix Suns on draft night. Born in Mesa, Arizona, he attended Desert Vista High School in Chandler.

items.headline: Underwent hip resurfacing procedure in October 2024, dealing with recovery
items.summary: Livers underwent hip resurfacing surgery on October 18, 2024, and expected a full year recovery. He has dealt with hip-related issues during the 2025-26 season with the Suns.

items.headline: South Sudan's youngest 2024 Olympics player, drafted 10th by Rockets, traded to Suns
items.summary: Maluach played for South Sudan at the 2024 Paris Olympics as the tournament's youngest player and met Devin Booker during the Games. He was drafted 10th by Houston in 2025 and traded to Phoenix.

items.headline: French guard signed by Spurs in 2023, converted to standard deal with Trail Blazers
items.summary: Cissoko was drafted 44th by the Spurs in 2023 and signed to a two-way contract with Portland on February 8, 2025. His contract was converted to a standard deal on February 20, 2026.

items.headline: Career-high 49 points in 2024 loss to Detroit Pistons
items.summary: On February 8, 2024, Grant scored a career-high 49 points in a 128-122 loss to the Detroit Pistons. He is on a five-year, $160 million contract with Portland signed in July 2023.

items.headline: Ninth Chinese player drafted to NBA, highest Chinese draft pick since Yi Jianlian in 2007
items.summary: Hansen was drafted 16th overall by the Memphis Grizzlies and traded to Portland. He is the ninth Chinese player drafted to the NBA and the highest since Yi Jianlian in 2007.

items.headline: Transferred from Indiana State to Texas, signed two-way contract with Trail Blazers
items.summary: Kent played college basketball for Indiana State, where he averaged 13.5 points and 8.1 rebounds in 2023-24 and was second-team All-Missouri Valley Conference. He signed a two-way contract with Portland on March 3, 2026.

items.headline: Czech basketball player drafted 2020, traded to Trail Blazers February 2026
items.summary: Krejci was born in Strakonice, Czech Republic and drafted in the second round of the 2020 NBA draft. He was traded to Portland on February 1, 2026, in exchange for Duop Reath and draft picks.

items.headline: Undrafted Arizona guard signed two-way contract with Trail Blazers
items.summary: Love went undrafted in 2025 after playing for North Carolina and Arizona. He signed a two-way contract with Portland on July 1, 2025, and has averaged 15.8 points in January games.

items.headline: Twin brother of Sacramento Kings' Keegan Murray, son of Iowa basketball legacy
items.summary: Murray is the twin brother of NBA player Keegan Murray (Sacramento Kings). His father Kenyon was a four-year starter for Iowa from 1992-1996 and Michigan Mr. Basketball in 1992.

items.headline: Canadian guard signed four-year, $90 million extension with Trail Blazers
items.summary: Sharpe, a Canadian professional basketball player, was drafted 7th overall in 2022 by Portland. On October 19, 2025, he signed a four-year, $90 million contract extension with the Trail Blazers.

items.headline: Australian-American two-time NBA All-Defensive selection with Trail Blazers
items.summary: Thybulle was named to the NBA All-Defensive Second Team in 2021 and 2022 with the Philadelphia 76ers. He was traded to Portland on February 9, 2023, and is expected to anchor the perimeter defense in the 2026 playoffs.

items.headline: Traded to Washington then Portland, recovering from right foot surgery
items.summary: Wesley was drafted 25th overall in 2022 by San Antonio. On July 9, 2025, he was traded to Washington, waived on July 19, and signed with Portland on July 22. He underwent right foot surgery and is gradually returning.

items.headline: Known as 'Time Lord' for play style, traded to Blazers with Malcolm Brogdon for Jrue Holiday
items.summary: Williams was traded to Portland on October 1, 2023, along with Malcolm Brogdon in exchange for Jrue Holiday. His 'Time Lord' nickname originated as an inside joke during his Boston Celtics days.

items.headline: Undrafted rookie signed two-way with Thunder, waived, then signed by Trail Blazers
items.summary: Youngblood went undrafted in 2025, signed with Oklahoma City on October 18, 2025, was waived on February 6, 2026, and signed a two-way contract with Portland on March 2, 2026.

items.headline: Traded to Spurs from Sacramento Kings, earned first Player of the Week of career
items.summary: On July 8, 2024, Barnes was traded to the San Antonio Spurs in a three-team deal with Sacramento and Chicago. He started all 82 games in 2024-25, shot over 40% from three, and earned his first career Player of the Week.

items.headline: Congolese 33-year-old veteran re-signed, depth piece in Spurs title contention
items.summary: Biyombo re-signed with the Spurs on September 15, 2025, on a one-year contract. Now in the backend of his career, he serves as a veteran locker room presence and depth option.

items.headline: 2026 NBA Slam Dunk Contest runner-up with 93-point finish
items.summary: Bryant was drafted 14th overall by San Antonio in 2025 from Arizona. In 2026, he competed in the NBA Slam Dunk Contest and finished in second place with 93 points, including a 50-point dunk.

items.headline: Son of NBA legend Ron Harper who won five championships
items.summary: Harper's father, Ron Harper, played 15 seasons in the NBA and won five NBA championships. Dylan was drafted 2nd overall by San Antonio in 2025 and had a career-best 27 points in Game 3 of the 2026 playoffs.

items.headline: Drafted 48th by Spurs in 2024, transferred from Stanford to North Carolina
items.summary: Ingram transferred from Stanford to North Carolina after his sophomore season. He was drafted 48th overall by San Antonio in 2024 and is expected to spend most of 2025-26 with the G League's Austin Spurs.

items.headline: 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man of the Year, won Olympic gold in 2021
items.summary: Johnson was named the 2025-26 Kia NBA Sixth Man of the Year after averaging 13.2 points in 23.3 minutes while playing all 82 games off the bench. He is the longest-tenured player on the Spurs and won Olympic gold in 2021.

items.headline: Dominican player signed by Spurs in 2025, suffered season-ending ankle surgery
items.summary: Jones Garcia signed a two-way contract with San Antonio on July 22, 2025, after earning All-Summer League first team honors. He underwent ankle surgery and missed the remainder of the 2025-26 season.

items.headline: Traded to Spurs with De'Aaron Fox from Sacramento in three-team deal
items.summary: On February 3, 2025, McLaughlin was traded to the San Antonio Spurs alongside De'Aaron Fox in a three-team trade also involving the Chicago Bulls. He had previously played for Minnesota and Sacramento.

items.headline: Canadian forward signed two-way with Spurs February 2026 after trades
items.summary: Miller went undrafted in 2024 and played for Dallas, Chicago, and Cleveland before signing a two-way contract with San Antonio on February 23, 2026. He attended Texas A&M and TCU.

items.headline: Canadian 7-foot stretch big acquired from Wizards, captain of Canada national team
items.summary: Olynyk was traded to San Antonio on July 9 from Washington in exchange for Malaki Branham, Blake Wesley, and a 2026 second-round pick. He is the captain of the Canadian men's national basketball team.

items.headline: 13-year veteran signed rest-of-season contract with Spurs after injury recovery
items.summary: Plumlee signed a 10-day contract with San Antonio on February 17, 2026, and converted it to a rest-of-season deal on February 28. He is a 7-footer who was a backup on Duke's 2010 national championship team.

items.headline: Kiowa and Cherokee-American signed by Spurs for perimeter shooting prowess
items.summary: Waters III is a Kiowa and Cherokee-American professional basketball player who signed with San Antonio on July 24, 2025. He achieved 44% three-point shooting in 2023-24 with the Thunder.

items.headline: Career-high 29 points in February 2026 victory over Dallas Mavericks
items.summary: On February 26, 2026, Achiuwa scored a career-high 29 points with 12 rebounds, four assists, and three steals in a 130-121 victory over the Dallas Mavericks. He is averaging career highs in multiple categories with Sacramento.

items.headline: 2022 first-round pick signed two-way with Kings after multiple 10-day deals
items.summary: Baldwin was drafted 28th overall by Golden State in 2022. He played for the Clippers and 76ers on 10-day deals before signing a two-way contract with Sacramento on February 22, 2026.

items.headline: Undrafted Auburn center promoted to standard contract, leading NBA rookies in blocks
items.summary: Cardwell went undrafted out of Auburn and was promoted from a two-way deal to a standard contract by Sacramento. He leads NBA rookies in blocks per game and is second in rebounds among first-year players.

items.headline: 2024 13th overall pick from Providence/South Carolina, career-best 29 points vs Warriors
items.summary: Carter was selected 13th overall in the 2024 NBA draft from South Carolina. He recently scored a career-high 29 points on 11-18 shooting with six three-pointers in a 124-118 win over the Warriors.

items.headline: 2025 24th pick from Colorado State/Colorado, breakout performance post-All-Star break
items.summary: Clifford was drafted 24th overall in 2025 by Sacramento after transfers from Colorado State and Colorado. After the All-Star break, he averaged 13.2 points, 5.2 rebounds, 4.0 assists, and shot 35.9% from three.

items.headline: Oregon State standout who played college ball at Pac-12 school
items.summary: Eubanks played for the Oregon State Beavers and averaged 13.2 points and 6.8 rebounds per game in his junior year before declaring for the NBA draft.

items.headline: French-American guard who grew up in France due to father's basketball career
items.summary: Killian Hayes was born in Lakeland, Florida, but grew up in France because his father, DeRon Hayes, played professional basketball there. He started with French club Cholet at age 16.

items.headline: University of Virginia product drafted 4th overall in 2019
items.summary: Hunter attended the University of Virginia and was drafted 4th overall by the Lakers in 2019 before being traded to the Hawks and eventually joining Sacramento in February 2026.

items.headline: Creighton national scoring leader and 2014 consensus Player of the Year
items.summary: McDermott led the nation in scoring in 2013-14 and was a three-time consensus first-team All-American. He finished with the fifth-most points in NCAA Division I history with 3,150 career points.

items.headline: Made explosive NBA debut in 2026, scoring 19 points in his first game
items.summary: On January 15, 2026, Plowden made his NBA debut and made his first seven field goal attempts, finishing with 19 points in a 110-94 victory over the Chicago Bulls.

items.headline: French standout from Paris who played at Stanford and made NBA history as rookie
items.summary: Raynaud is from Paris and graduated from Lycée Henri-IV before playing at Stanford. As a senior, he averaged 20.1 points and 10.9 rebounds, becoming one of only three major-conference players alongside Durant and Melo to average 20+ points, 10+ rebounds, and 50+ three-pointers in a season.

items.headline: Set Colorado State single-game assist record with 14 dimes
items.summary: Stevens played college basketball for Colorado State and set a single-game record with 14 assists. As a senior, he averaged 17.9 points and 6.7 assists per game and led the Mountain West in assists.

items.headline: Minneapolis native who won Class 3A state championship at DeLaSalle High School
items.summary: Battle was born in Robbinsdale, Minnesota, and attended DeLaSalle High School in Minneapolis, where he was teammates with Tyrell Terry and averaged 21.2 points and nine rebounds as a senior, helping his team win the Class 3A state title.

items.headline: 2022 Gatorade National Player of the Year from Wichita, Kansas
items.summary: Dick was born in Wichita, Kansas to Carmen (who played basketball at Iowa State and professionally in Japan) and Bart Dick. He was awarded the 2022 Gatorade National Player of the Year in high school and played for Kansas, averaging 14.1 points in 36 games.

items.headline: Former Wisconsin Badger known for elite defense, ACC DPOY at Louisville
items.summary: Hepburn was born in Omaha, Nebraska, and played for Wisconsin before transferring to Louisville. He was named ACC Defensive Player of the Year during his final season at Louisville before signing with Toronto as an undrafted free agent.

items.headline: Son of former Pacers power forward Dale Davis, raised by stepfather Ray Jackson
items.summary: Jackson-Davis is the biological son of Dale Davis, a former Indiana Pacers power forward. He was raised by his mother and stepfather, Ray Jackson (former Washington State football player). He adopted the hyphenated last name entering high school to honor his stepfather.

items.headline: Canadian guard from Brampton, Ontario who attended elite basketball academy
items.summary: Lawson was born in Brampton, Ontario and attended GTA Prep in Mississauga, a basketball academy. He played college basketball for South Carolina and was named SEC All-Freshman team as a true freshman.

items.headline: Georgian-American forward fluent in English, Georgian, Russian, and Italian
items.summary: Born in New York City, Mamukelashvili grew up in Tbilisi, Georgia and is fluent in four languages. He moved to Italy at age 14 to attend high school and later played at Montverde Academy alongside RJ Barrett.

items.headline: Helped Florida Gators win 2024 National Championship in playoff debut
items.summary: Martin transferred from Florida Atlantic to Florida and helped lead the Gators to the 2024 National Championship. He tallied 7 points in the championship game victory over Houston.

items.headline: West Palm Beach native who played AAU basketball with Scottie Barnes
items.summary: Mogbo was born in West Palm Beach, Florida, and played alongside future Raptors teammate Scottie Barnes on an AAU basketball team. He started playing organized basketball in fourth grade after initially focusing on football.

items.headline: South Carolina native who was Class 4A Player of the Year as junior at A.C. Flora High School
items.summary: Murray-Boyles was born in Columbia, South Carolina, and attended A.C. Flora High School in Forest Acres where he was named the Class 4A Player of the Year as a junior before being drafted 9th overall by Toronto in 2025.

items.headline: 2024 Naismith Defensive Player of the Year from University of Houston
items.summary: Shead was the first player in Big 12 history to win both Player of the Year and Defensive Player of the Year in the same season (2023-24). He was named the Naismith Defensive Player of the Year after leading the conference in assists.

items.headline: Married Miss USA 2017 winner Kára McCullough in 2020
items.summary: In 2020, the veteran guard married Kára McCullough, who won the Miss USA 2017 title. They have three children together and Temple has become a key mentor for young Raptors players.

items.headline: Five-star Texas recruit from McKinney who led state championship team
items.summary: Walter grew up in McKinney, Texas and averaged 23.3 points, 7.6 rebounds as a junior, leading McKinney High School to a state championship. He transferred to Link Academy in Branson and was a consensus five-star recruit in the 2023 class.

items.headline: McDonald's All-American point guard from University of Tennessee
items.summary: Chandler was a consensus five-star recruit and McDonald's All-American point guard who committed to Tennessee as a high school sophomore. He was drafted 38th overall in 2022 by San Antonio and traded to Memphis.

items.headline: Georgia native rated #1 overall prospect by ESPN and Rivals for 2023 class
items.summary: Collier was born in Marietta, Georgia and was a consensus five-star recruit rated as the number one overall prospect by both ESPN and Rivals. He won the 2023 Naismith Prep Player of the Year Award and was named Mr. Georgia Basketball.

items.headline: Duke standout named 2023 ACC Tournament MVP and All-American
items.summary: Filipowski was a five-star recruit who played at Duke. As a sophomore, he was named ACC Tournament MVP and 2023-24 Consensus All-American Second Team after posting career-highs in points (16.4) and assists (2.8) per game.

items.headline: Younger brother of OKC Thunder player Jalen Williams
items.summary: Williams grew up in Gilbert, Arizona and is the younger brother of Oklahoma City Thunder player Jalen Williams. He attended Perry High School where he was named Premier Region Player of the Year.

items.headline: First Cal State Northridge player to record a triple-double
items.summary: Harkless played at Cal State Northridge, UNLV, and Oklahoma. At CSUN, he became the first player in school history to record a triple-double and the first since 2005 to get 54 steals in a single season.

items.headline: Set Pittsburgh school record with 110 made three-pointers in one season
items.summary: Hinson played for Pittsburgh and set a school record with 110 made three-pointers. He was named first-team All-ACC after averaging 18.5 points per game in his final season at Pitt.

items.headline: Purdue Fort Wayne's all-time leading scorer with 2,065 career points
items.summary: Konchar played for Purdue Fort Wayne and left as the program's all-time leading scorer with 2,065 career points. As a senior, he averaged 19.5 points and 8.6 rebounds with 5.4 assists per game.

items.headline: Three-time Ivy League Defensive Player of the Year from Yale
items.summary: Mbeng played at Yale and was named Ivy Defensive Player of the Year three times. He was a two-time All-Ivy selection and recorded the second triple-double in Yale program history with 15 points, 12 assists, and 10 rebounds.

items.headline: Ukrainian-born player fluent in Russian, Ukrainian, and English
items.summary: Mykhailiuk was born in Cherkasy, Ukraine where his mother is a high school biology teacher and father is a college history professor. He is fluent in Russian, Ukrainian, and English and played for Cherkaski Mavpy in the Ukrainian Basketball SuperLeague before moving to the U.S.

items.headline: Founded Jusuf Nurkic Foundation to support youth and community in Bosnia
items.summary: Nurkic established the Jusuf Nurkic Foundation in 2020 to positively impact the lives of youth, women, and the community in his home country of Bosnia. He received the 2018-19 Maurice Lucas Award for community contributions.

items.headline: Florida Mr. Basketball who scored career-high 43 points against Bulls
items.summary: Sensabaugh grew up in Orlando, Florida and was named Florida Mr. Basketball after averaging 25.1 points as a senior. On January 14, 2026, he scored a career-high 43 points in a loss to the Chicago Bulls.

items.headline: Congolese native from Lubumbashi who didn't start basketball until May 2014
items.summary: Tshiebwe grew up in Lubumbashi, Democratic Republic of the Congo, where he played soccer as a child. He didn't start playing organized basketball until May 2014 after being encouraged due to his height.

items.headline: UC San Diego player who led nation in steals with 110 in 2024-25
items.summary: Gray played for UC San Diego and led the nation in steals in 2024-25 with 110. He was named The Big West's top defensive player and was a finalist for Naismith Defensive Player of the Year.

items.headline: Former University of North Carolina Tar Heels player from Concord, North Carolina
items.summary: Black committed to coach Roy Williams and UNC as a high school sophomore. He played for the Tar Heels and later signed with the Charlotte Hornets before joining the Wizards on a two-way contract in February 2026.

items.headline: Twin brother of Julian Champagnie; first twin NBA game matchup in franchise history
items.summary: Justin has a twin brother, Julian Champagnie, who also plays in the NBA. On February 10, 2025, Justin played against Julian for the first time when the Wizards met the Spurs, making them among a very small number of twins to play against each other in an NBA game.

items.headline: Brother of WNBA player Te'a Cooper, who was drafted by Phoenix Mercury in 2020
items.summary: Cooper was born in Newark, New Jersey and moved to Atlanta. He has two sisters, Te'a and Mia, who both won state titles at McEachern. His sister Te'a played college basketball at Tennessee, South Carolina, and Baylor before being drafted by the Phoenix Mercury in the 2020 WNBA draft.

items.headline: Veteran teammate and mentor known for exceptional character and leadership
items.summary: Gill went undrafted in 2016 and played in Turkey and Russia before joining the Wizards in 2020. Despite rarely starting, he's known as a consummate teammate and positive influence. Coach Brian Keefe said in January 2025, 'You could write a book on how great of a teammate Anthony Gill is.'

items.headline: Skipped college to join NBA G League Ignite, averaging 17.7 PPG
items.summary: Hardy opted out of his senior season in high school and joined the NBA G League Ignite on June 9, 2021, instead of attending college. He averaged 17.7 points, 4.6 rebounds, and 3.2 assists for the Ignite before being drafted in 2022.

items.headline: 2025 SEC Freshman of the Year, broke Kevin Durant's Texas freshman scoring record
items.summary: Johnson was born in Dallas, Texas and transferred from Lake Highlands High School to Link Academy. As a Texas freshman, he averaged 19.9 points and broke Kevin Durant's Texas freshman school record with 39 points against Arkansas.

items.headline: Made NBA history with 20 rebounds (10 offensive) in Wizards debut against Jazz
items.summary: Reese was a four-year player at Maryland who finished with 1,488 career points and 1,015 rebounds. On March 5, 2026, he recorded 18 points and 20 rebounds (10 offensive, 10 defensive) against Utah, becoming the first Wizards player since Antawn Jamison in 2010 to achieve that rebounding feat.

items.headline: Canadian guard from Kitchener, Ontario who played for Illinois Fighting Illini
items.summary: Riley was born in Cambridge, Ontario and is from Kitchener, Ontario. He played college basketball for the Illinois Fighting Illini before being drafted 21st overall by Utah and traded to Washington in 2025.

items.headline: Florida State player named ACC All-Second Team with 18.4 PPG as senior
items.summary: Watkins was born in Trenton, New Jersey and attended Trenton Catholic Academy. After playing for VCU, he transferred to Florida State and was named ACC All-Second Team as a senior with 18.4 points and 5.7 rebounds per game.

items.headline: Villanova's first true one-and-done since Tim Thomas in 1997
items.summary: Whitmore was a consensus five-star recruit from Archbishop Spalding High School in Maryland. He attended Villanova and declared for the NBA draft after one season, becoming the first Villanova true one-and-done since Tim Thomas in 1997.

items.headline: Australian basketball player in WNBA
items.summary: Borlase is an Australian professional basketball player who plays as a guard for the Atlanta Dream, making her one of the growing number of international athletes in the WNBA.

items.headline: Spanish guard making international impact in WNBA
items.summary: Cazorla brings international experience from European professional basketball leagues to the Atlanta Dream as a guard.

items.headline: First-round WNBA draft pick from University of Michigan
items.summary: Hillmon was a prominent college basketball player at Michigan before being selected in the WNBA draft, where she joined the Atlanta Dream as a forward.

items.headline: WNBA forward with Atlanta Dream
items.summary: Stephanie Jones is a professional basketball player competing as a forward in the WNBA with the Atlanta Dream.

items.headline: International WNBA forward
items.summary: Kone brings international basketball experience to the Atlanta Dream, competing as a forward in the league.

items.headline: Guard for Atlanta Dream
items.summary: Nivar is a professional guard competing for the Atlanta Dream in the WNBA.

items.headline: Center for Atlanta Dream
items.summary: Okot is a professional center playing for the Atlanta Dream, helping anchor the team's defensive lineup.

items.headline: Pacific Islander representation in WNBA
items.summary: Paopao represents Pacific Islander athletes in professional basketball, competing as a guard for the Atlanta Dream.

items.headline: Rising guard talent with Atlanta Dream
items.summary: Phelia is a guard on the Atlanta Dream roster, contributing to the team's backcourt depth.

items.headline: International basketball player in WNBA
items.summary: Ran is an international guard bringing global basketball experience to the Atlanta Dream.

items.headline: Guard on Atlanta Dream roster
items.summary: Robinson is a professional guard competing for the Atlanta Dream in the WNBA.

items.headline: Forward for Atlanta Dream
items.summary: Thierry is a forward competing for the Atlanta Dream, contributing to the team's frontcourt rotation.

items.headline: Forward for Atlanta Dream
items.summary: Washington is a professional forward on the Atlanta Dream roster in the WNBA.

items.headline: Guard for Atlanta Dream
items.summary: Winterburn is a professional guard competing for the Atlanta Dream in the WNBA.

items.headline: International guard with Chicago Sky
items.summary: Coulibaly is an international player bringing global basketball experience to the Chicago Sky.

items.headline: Guard for Chicago Sky
items.summary: Hobbs is a professional guard competing for the Chicago Sky in the WNBA.

items.headline: Guard from UCLA joining Chicago Sky
items.summary: Jaquez is a guard from UCLA who joined the Chicago Sky, bringing college basketball pedigree to the WNBA.

items.headline: Forward for Chicago Sky
items.summary: Lattimore is a forward competing for the Chicago Sky in professional women's basketball.

items.headline: Guard for Chicago Sky
items.summary: Morgan is a professional guard on the Chicago Sky roster.

items.headline: Forward for Chicago Sky
items.summary: Nestor is a forward competing for the Chicago Sky in the WNBA.

items.headline: Ohio State guard joins Chicago Sky
items.summary: Sheldon is a guard from Ohio State who was drafted by the Chicago Sky, bringing college standout credentials to the WNBA.

items.headline: Guard for Chicago Sky
items.summary: Taylor is a professional guard competing for the Chicago Sky.

items.headline: Veteran point guard and Sky leader
items.summary: Vandersloot is one of the WNBA's top point guards, a veteran leader for the Chicago Sky known for her playmaking ability and basketball IQ.

items.headline: Forward for Chicago Sky
items.summary: Westbeld is a forward on the Chicago Sky roster in the WNBA.

items.headline: Veteran center for Chicago Sky
items.summary: Williams is an experienced center and a key defensive anchor for the Chicago Sky.

items.headline: Forward for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Angloma is a forward competing for the Connecticut Sun in the WNBA.

items.headline: Center for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Beers is a professional center helping anchor the Connecticut Sun's roster.

items.headline: Guard for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Bigby is a professional guard competing for the Connecticut Sun.

items.headline: Guard for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Hayes is a guard on the Connecticut Sun roster in the WNBA.

items.headline: Guard for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Kneepkens is a professional guard competing for the Connecticut Sun.

items.headline: Guard for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Lacan is a professional guard on the Connecticut Sun roster.

items.headline: Guard from Washington State joins Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Leger-Walker is a guard who played college basketball at Washington State before joining the Connecticut Sun.

items.headline: Center for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Oldacre is a center competing for the Connecticut Sun in the WNBA.

items.headline: Guard for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Peddy is a professional guard on the Connecticut Sun roster.

items.headline: Guard for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Rivers is a professional guard competing for the Connecticut Sun.

items.headline: International basketball player with Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Toure brings international experience to the Connecticut Sun, competing as a guard in the WNBA.

items.headline: Guard for Connecticut Sun
items.summary: Turner is a professional guard on the Connecticut Sun roster.

items.headline: Rising guard talent joining Dallas Wings
items.summary: Berger is a guard who joined the Dallas Wings, bringing college basketball experience to the WNBA.

items.headline: Three-time WNBA champion signs with Dallas Wings
items.summary: Veteran forward Alysha Clark, who has won three WNBA championships (2018, 2020 with Seattle Storm; 2023 with Las Vegas Aces), signed a one-year deal with the Dallas Wings in April 2026. At 5-foot-11, Clark brings 13 seasons of WNBA experience and is a two-time All-Defensive selection and 2023 Sixth Player of the Year.

items.headline: UConn champion in relationship with teammate Paige Bueckers
items.summary: Azzi Fudd confirmed she is in a romantic relationship with former UConn and current Dallas Wings teammate Paige Bueckers. As a redshirt junior in 2025, Fudd won the national championship and was named Final Four Most Outstanding Player, averaging 17.3 PPG.

items.headline: No. 1 pick in 2026 WNBA draft with record rookie contract
items.summary: Dallas Wings selected Fudd as the first overall pick of the 2026 WNBA draft on April 13, 2026, signing her to the most lucrative rookie contract in league history ($500,000) following the March 2026 collective bargaining agreement.

items.headline: 12-year WNBA veteran with All-Star honors and Finals experience
items.summary: Odyssey Sims is a 12-year WNBA veteran who was drafted No. 2 overall by Tulsa Shock in 2014, was named to the All-Rookie Team, and made her first Finals appearance in 2017 with the Los Angeles Sparks. She was named an All-Star in 2019 and made All-WNBA Second Team.

items.headline: Arizona Wildcats star undrafted but earns Golden State training camp deal
