items.summary: Cate Reese was not selected in the 2023 WNBA draft despite being a four-time All-Pac-12 selection and helping Arizona reach the national championship game as a junior. She played professional basketball in Australia, Belgium, and Poland before signing a training camp contract with the Golden State Valkyries.

items.headline: Three-time WNBA champion center from UConn
items.summary: Kiah Stokes, a 6-foot-3 center from UConn, won two NCAA national championships (2013, 2014) and has captured three WNBA championships (2022, 2023, 2025) all with the Las Vegas Aces. She was drafted No. 11 overall by the New York Liberty in 2015 and signed with Golden State Valkyries in April 2026.

items.headline: Italian international sharpshooter selected in expansion draft
items.summary: Cecilia Zandalasini, born March 16, 1996, is an Italian basketball player and MVP of the 2016 FIBA Europe Under-20 Championship. She averaged 19.0 PPG at EuroBasket Women 2017 and was selected by Golden State Valkyries from Minnesota Lynx in the December 2024 expansion draft.

items.headline: 2026 WNBA rookie from South Carolina joins Fever with Caitlin Clark
items.summary: Raven Johnson was the No. 10 overall pick of the 2026 WNBA Draft by the Indiana Fever. The South Carolina Gamecocks guard handed Caitlin Clark a loss in her final game as a Hawkeye during the 2024 NCAA Championship and now joins elite teammates including Caitlin Clark and Aliyah Boston.

items.headline: 2019 WNBA champion signs with Indiana Fever for championship experience
items.summary: Shatori Walker-Kimbrough, a 30-year-old nine-year veteran drafted 6th overall by Washington Mystics in 2017, won the 2019 WNBA championship and was drafted out of the University of Maryland. She signed with Indiana Fever in 2026 to add championship experience and defensive versatility to the backcourt.

items.headline: Chinese center makes WNBA impact after trade to Dallas Wings
items.summary: Li Yueru is a 6-foot-7 Chinese center who was drafted in the third round of the 2019 WNBA draft by Atlanta Dream but continued playing in China until being traded to Dallas Wings on June 14, 2025 in exchange for draft picks. In her first four games with the Wings, she averaged 7.3 PPG and 7.3 RPG.

items.headline: UCLA product and eight-year WNBA veteran signs with Indiana Fever
items.summary: Monique Billings, an eight-year veteran drafted 15th overall by Atlanta Dream in 2018 from UCLA, signed a two-year $1.6M contract with the Indiana Fever in April 2026. The 6-foot-4 forward is a plus-defender with elite rim efficiency who complements All-WNBA center Aliyah Boston.

items.headline: Brazilian center leads international tournaments, returns to Fever
items.summary: Damiris Dantas, a 33-year-old Brazilian center born November 17, 1992, re-signed with Indiana Fever on a two-year $610k deal for her third consecutive season. She is a consistent presence for Brazil's national team with 81 caps, recently leading the 2026 FIBA World Cup Qualifying and 2026 AmeriCup tournaments in scoring.

items.headline: Sister of Jacksonville Jaguars edge rusher Josh Hines-Allen
items.summary: Myisha Hines-Allen, born May 30, 1995, is the sister of Jacksonville Jaguars defensive end Josh Hines-Allen. She was drafted 2nd round by Washington Mystics in 2018 from Louisville and played 187 regular-season games for the team before being traded to Minnesota Lynx and later signing with Indiana Fever in April 2026.

items.headline: Sister of Indiana Pacers guard T.J. McConnell signs with Fever
items.summary: Megan McConnell, born November 12, 2001, is the sister of Indiana Pacers guard T.J. McConnell. The undrafted Duquesne guard won Atlantic 10 Defensive Player of the Year and holds school records in steals (370) and assists (660). She signed a training camp contract with the Indiana Fever on April 13, 2026.

items.headline: 36-year-old forward pursuing WNBA dream while being a mother
items.summary: Emma Cannon, born June 1, 1989 (age 36), is a 6-foot-2 forward in her 6th WNBA season. She signed with the Los Angeles Sparks on February 2, 2025 and played 21 games averaging 4.4 PPG and 1.8 RPG while shooting 50.8% FG and 38.9% from three. She has played professionally across Germany, Australia, Israel, Russia, Poland, Hungary, Turkey, and China.

items.headline: Maryland Terrapins guard re-signed by Golden State on multi-year deal
items.summary: Kaila Charles, who played college basketball for the Maryland Terrapins, was drafted 23rd overall by Connecticut Sun in 2020. She previously played for Atlanta Dream, Seattle Storm, and Dallas Wings before the Golden State Valkyries re-signed her to a multi-year contract in April 2026, averaging a career-high 7.4 PPG in 2025.

items.headline: First person of Taiwanese descent to win women's college basketball championship
items.summary: Kaitlyn Chen is the first person of Taiwanese descent to win a women's college basketball championship as a member of UConn's 2025 championship team. The guard played college basketball at UConn and Princeton before being selected by Golden State Valkyries in the third round of the 2025 WNBA draft on April 15, 2025.

items.headline: Golden State Valkyries first-ever draft pick from Lithuania
items.summary: Justė Jocytė, born in Washington D.C. but raised in Lithuania, was selected 5th overall by Golden State Valkyries in the 2025 WNBA draft as the franchise's first-ever pick. At just 19 years old, she became the youngest player ever in France's top league and EuroLeague. She plays for DLC ASVEL Féminin in Lyon, founded by Tony Parker, which won EuroCup in 2023.

items.headline: Australian guard from Duke shows promise in Valkyries preseason

items.summary: Taybor Pepper, long snapper, signed undrafted free agent contract with Miami Dolphins in 2026. Pepper, wearing number 41, competed for special teams long snapper position in training camp.

items.headline: Seth Vernon signed UDFA punter contract with Dolphins, number 49
items.summary: Seth Vernon, punter prospect, signed undrafted free agent contract with Miami Dolphins in 2026. Vernon, wearing number 49, competed for special teams punter position throughout training camp.

items.headline: Uncle Cory Harkey is Bills assistant special teams coach
items.summary: Uncle Cory Harkey is a former UCLA tight end and current Bills assistant special teams coach. Other uncle Mike Sweeney played MLB for Kansas City Royals 1995-2010.

items.headline: Played hockey for 10 years with NYC Cyclones travel team
items.summary: Bell played hockey competitively for 10 years including with NYC Cyclones travel team. He states hockey is his first love and stopped around age 15-16 to focus on football.

items.headline: Vertical jump ranked 98th percentile among OTs historically
items.summary: Boston College OT had elite athletic profile with vertical jump and broad jump in 97th-98th percentiles historically. Speed and explosion metrics typically belong to skill position players, not 311-pound linemen.

items.headline: Three-year starter at Ole Miss with 48 games appearance
items.summary: Played at University of Mississippi as three-year starter with two seasons at left tackle and one at left guard. Appeared in 48 games without missing a single contest during four-year career.

items.headline: English rugby player through International Player Pathway Program
items.summary: Born in England, played rugby union and joined NFL via International Player Pathway. Spent 10 weeks training at IMG Academy learning American football before being drafted by Bills in seventh round, becoming second-ever IPPP participant drafted.

items.headline: Played for Bills coach Joe Brady at LSU in 2019
items.summary: Lloyd Cushenberry III played college football for LSU and reunites with Bills head coach Joe Brady who coached him there. Previously started 23 games with Tennessee Titans before joining Buffalo.

items.headline: Attended Yale Bulldogs before transferring to Georgia Tech
items.summary: Played college football for Yale Bulldogs and Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets. Attended Highland High School in Salt Lake City, Utah before his collegiate career.

items.headline: Made 49 career starts for UConn missing just one game
items.summary: Chase Lundt finished career with Huskies in 2024 earning All-New England All-Star and HERO Sports Group of Five All-American status. Made 49 career starts at UConn, missing only one game during his time.

items.headline: Cousin of former Pro Bowl WR Jeremy Maclin
items.summary: Ja'Mori Maclin is cousin of former Pro Bowl wide receiver Jeremy Maclin. A 5-foot-11, 191-pound receiver who signed with Bills as undrafted free agent from Kentucky.

items.headline: Transferred from Alabama through Mississippi State to San Diego State
items.summary: Wide receiver played for Alabama Crimson Tide, Mississippi State Bulldogs, and San Diego State Aztecs before signing with Buffalo as undrafted free agent. Tore ACL during Wild Card playoff game vs Jaguars.

items.headline: Nephew of former NFL QB Mike Tomczak who played 15 seasons
items.summary: Max Tomczak is nephew of Mike Tomczak who played QB for Bears, Packers, Browns, Steelers 1986-2000. Set Youngstown State records with 225 career receptions and 3,024 receiving yards.

items.headline: First Florida offensive guard named consensus All-American
items.summary: Played for Louisiana and Florida. At Florida, Torrence became the first Gators offensive guard to be named a consensus All-American. Has been starter since Week 1 of rookie season.

items.headline: Started for Georgia in back-to-back national championships
items.summary: Played center for University of Georgia for three seasons, contributing to back-to-back national championships in 2021 and 2022. Drafted by Bills in fifth round (141st overall) of 2024 NFL Draft.

items.headline: Played for Eagles' Super Bowl LIX championship team
items.summary: Ben VanSumeren spent first three NFL seasons with Philadelphia Eagles, including their Super Bowl win following 2024 season. Transitioned from linebacker to fullback in 2024 after starting with Eagles.

items.headline: Transferred from Howard to Syracuse as starting left tackle
items.summary: Transferred from Howard to Syracuse before 2024 season and immediately slotted in as starting left tackle. Protected Kyle McCord who broke ACC single-season passing yards record (4,779 yards). Earned ACC Offensive Lineman of Week honors.

items.headline: Maryland defensive tackle drafted by Dallas in 2025
items.summary: Olatunbosun 'Tommy' Akingbesote played football at University of Maryland. Selected by Dallas Cowboys in Round 7 of 2025 NFL Draft before signing with Buffalo practice squad.

items.headline: Hometown hero from Buffalo signed by Bills as UDFA
items.summary: Joe Andreessen is hometown Buffalo linebacker who played at Bryant and University at Buffalo. Nicknamed 'Buffalo Joe' by Josh Allen after strong preseason performance. Set school record with 112 tackles at Bryant.

items.headline: Duke defensive tackle drafted in 2024 third round
items.summary: DeWayne Carter played college football for Duke Blue Devils. Selected by Buffalo Bills with 95th overall pick in third round of 2024 NFL Draft. Limited to 8 games in 2024 after tearing Achilles before season began.

items.headline: Army West Point linebacker with torn ACL recovery
items.summary: Ciarlo spent collegiate career at Army West Point 2020-2023. Elected team captain as senior and posted 58 tackles and 3 sacks. Suffered torn ACL four plays into Jets preseason debut but featured on HBO's Hard Knocks with Bills.

items.headline: Miami Hurricanes cornerback with four career interceptions
items.summary: Played college football for Miami Hurricanes 2019-2023, recording 156 tackles and 4 interceptions in 32 career starts. Suffered hamstring injury during 2025 preseason joint practice with Chicago Bears.

items.headline: Kentucky and Pittsburgh cornerback with 7 career interceptions
items.summary: Marlin 'M.J.' Devonshire Jr. played for Kentucky Wildcats and Pittsburgh Panthers. Returned 3 of his 7 interceptions for touchdowns. Drafted by Las Vegas Raiders in seventh round of 2024 draft.

items.headline: Penn State DT ran fastest 40-time among tackles since 2023
items.summary: At 6-foot-1 and 290 pounds, Zane Durant had fastest 40-yard time among defensive tackles since 2023 at 4.75 seconds. Penn State defensive tackle played mostly at three-technique with versatility across line.

items.headline: TCU linebacker led team with 130 tackles and All-Big 12
items.summary: Played for California Golden Bears and TCU Horned Frogs. Started every game for TCU in 2025, leading team with 130 total tackles. Named first-team All-Big 12 and second among all linebackers at NFL Combine with 4.47 speed.

items.headline: Temple safety returned interception 99 yards for touchdown
items.summary: Samuel Lamar Franklin Jr. played for Temple Owls as safety. In Week 4 of 2023 season vs Minnesota Vikings, recorded career first interception and returned it 99 yards for touchdown, setting Panthers franchise record.

items.headline: Ohio State four-year player with 3 career interceptions
items.summary: Played for Ohio State Buckeyes for four years with 42 games total. Recorded 98 tackles, 4 tackles for loss, 2.5 sacks, 14 pass breakups, 3 interceptions and 2 forced fumbles in college career.

items.headline: Virginia Tech DT transferred from North Dakota State
items.summary: 6-foot-4, 297-pound defensive lineman from Pella, Iowa. Started 7 of North Dakota State's 16 games in 2024 with 37 tackles and 7.5 tackles for loss. Transferred to Virginia Tech for redshirt senior season.

items.headline: Transferred from Ole Miss to Ohio State, won 2025 national championship
items.summary: Started at Ole Miss before transferring to Ohio State in 2023. As junior in 2025, part of National Champion Buckeyes, starting all 16 games with 45 tackles and 2 interceptions. Reduced penalties from 16 in 2024 to just 4 in 2025.

items.headline: Virginia Tech linebacker signed as undrafted free agent
items.summary: Played linebacker for Virginia Tech Hokies. Signed by Bills as undrafted free agent in 2025. Started alongside Matt Milano in exhibition games despite undrafted status and lack of experience.

items.headline: South Carolina safety with 8 interceptions in three seasons
items.summary: Played for South Carolina Gamecocks with 178 total tackles, 21 pass deflections and 8 interceptions in three seasons. Ranked No. 8 safety in draft class and was top-4 percent in RAS athleticism score.

items.headline: New Hampshire safety with 111 tackles and All-CAA honors
items.summary: New Hampshire product signed with Bills as undrafted free agent. In final season had 111 tackles, 3 tackles for loss, 1 interception. Named All-CAA Second-Team. Had zero regular season NFL snaps to date.

items.headline: Clemson edge rusher with 21.5 career sacks and 6 forced fumbles
items.summary: T.J. Parker played three seasons at Clemson with 21.5 sacks and led ACC with 6 forced fumbles in 2024. Earned All-ACC second team honors. Switching to outside linebacker in Bills' 3-4 scheme under head coach Sean McDermott.

items.headline: Son of former Boston College and Buffalo Bills safety Daryl Porter
items.summary: Daryl Porter Jr. is son of former Boston College and Buffalo Bills safety Daryl Porter. Started career at West Virginia before transferring to University of Miami. Father played for Bills in the past.

items.headline: Second-fastest 40-time among defensive players at combine
items.summary: Played first two seasons at Clemson before transferring to Missouri. Exceptional speed with 4.32 40-yard dash, second-fastest for defensive player in 2026 draft. Recorded 4 interceptions and 12 passes defensed over final two seasons.

items.headline: South Carolina DT with 109 tackles and violent playing style
items.summary: T.J. Sanders played four seasons for University of South Carolina with 109 tackles, 17.5 tackles for loss, 9.5 sacks. Known for violent pursuit and strong hands. Drafted 41st overall in 2025 second round.

items.headline: Virginia Tech cornerback suffered neck injury, career uncertain
items.summary: Played college football for Virginia Tech Hokies. Suffered frightening neck injury in Week 4 against New Orleans Saints that ended rookie season. Career playing future remains in jeopardy as of February 2026.

items.headline: Arkansas Razorbacks cornerback roster player
items.summary: Kani Walker is cornerback from Arkansas Razorbacks. Consensus three-star recruit from Douglas County, Georgia who originally committed to Boston College before signing with Louisville as consensus recruit.

items.headline: Tulane linebacker with team-leading 117 tackles in 2025
items.summary: Played for Tulane Green Wave and was thrust into starting role following preseason injury to Matt Milano in 2024. Led team with 117 tackles in 2025 with 5 for loss and 1 forced fumble. Suffered neck injury vs Denver Broncos in Wild Card.

items.headline: Won 2023 national championship at Michigan before transferring to Florida
items.summary: Tommy Doman Jr. played for Michigan Wolverines, winning national championship in 2023. Transferred to Florida Gators for 2025 season. Led FBS in hang time with 4.5-second average punt hang time. Earned All-SEC honors from PFF.

items.headline: Long snapper won national championship with Indiana
items.summary: 6-foot-tall, 200-pound long snapper from Savannah, Georgia who signed with Bills as undrafted free agent. Won national championship with Indiana before joining Buffalo organization.

items.headline: Temple kicker went 21-for-21 on extra points in 2024
items.summary: Maddux Chase Trujillo played for Austin Peay and Temple. Had perfect 21-for-21 extra-point season at Temple with 16-for-22 field goals. Signed as undrafted free agent after preseason with Indianapolis Colts.

items.headline: Florida Gators tackle with third-team All-SEC honors
items.summary: Started all 12 games as senior at Florida in 2025, earning third-team All-SEC honors. Received invite to 2026 Senior Bowl. Drafted 86th overall by Browns in third round of 2026 draft.

items.headline: Played for Mississippi State and Utah State before joining Browns
items.summary: Davon Booth played for Mississippi State Bulldogs and Utah State Aggies. In final 2025 season had 552 rushing yards and 9 touchdowns plus 165 receiving yards. Signed with Browns as undrafted free agent.

items.headline: Washington WR with 20 TDs, 14 came in red zone
items.summary: Denzel Boston played four seasons at Washington with 132 receptions, 1,781 yards and 20 touchdowns. Big-bodied contested-catch specialist especially productive near goal line with 14 of 20 TDs in red zone.

items.headline: Started for Joe Moore Award-winning Washington O-line
items.summary: Played for Washington Huskies and Alabama Crimson Tide. Redshirted at Washington in 2022, then started all 15 games of 2023 season including 13 at center for Joe Moore Award-winning O-line. Transferred to Alabama under coach Kalen DeBoer.

items.headline: 34-year-old Super Bowl champion fullback with 11-year NFL career
items.summary: Michael Burton played for Rutgers. Drafted by Detroit Lions in 2015 fifth round. 11-year NFL veteran who played for Bears, Saints, Washington, Chiefs, and Broncos. Super Bowl champion and known for his blocking ability.

items.headline: Florida State offensive lineman named team's Offensive Newcomer
items.summary: Jeremiah Byers played at Florida State and UTEP. In two-year career as Seminole appeared in 23 games with 22 starts. Named Florida State Offensive Newcomer of Year in 2023 and won Bobby Bowden Leadership Award in 2024.

items.headline: Transferred from NC State to Texas A&M, won Paul Hornung Award
items.summary: KC Concepcion played first two seasons at NC State as ACC Freshman All-American and All-American. Transferred to Texas A&M for 2025 season. Recipient of 2025 Paul Hornung Award recognizing versatility. Also punt returner.

items.headline: Florida Gators center signed off Detroit Lions practice squad
items.summary: Kingsley Eguakun played college football for Florida Gators. Originally signed by Detroit Lions as undrafted free agent in 2024. Browns signed him off Lions' practice squad on December 31, 2025. Appeared in 4 games with 2 starts for Detroit.

items.headline: Illinois tight end team captain in final two seasons
items.summary: Tanner Arkin played for Illinois and Colorado State. Named team captain in each of final two seasons with Illini. 6-foot-4, 265-pound blocking tight end who fits Mike Vrabel's system. Signed as UDFA with Patriots.

items.headline: Missouri tackle started 13 games and won Music City Bowl
items.summary: Marcus Bryant played for SMU Mustangs and Missouri Tigers. Started all 13 games as Missouri's left tackle in 2024, allowing just 5 sacks while helping Tigers to 10 wins and Music City Bowl victory.
